# The Secret Da'wah — The First Three Years of Islamic Propagation (610–613 CE)

## Context

After the first revelation descended in the Cave of Hira and the pause of revelation (*fatrah al-wahy*) ended, **Prophet Muhammad SAW** began his mission with extraordinary caution: secretly and limited to those closest to him.^1 This approach was far from arbitrary. Seventh-century Mecca was controlled by the Quraysh oligarchy whose economic, social, and political life depended entirely on the polytheistic religious system — idol worship at the Ka'bah generated enormous revenue from pilgrims across the Arabian Peninsula.^2

Publicly announcing monotheistic teachings in such an environment would provoke a fierce reaction capable of destroying the movement before it could take root. Therefore, for approximately three years (610–613 CE), Prophet Muhammad SAW conducted his da'wah in secret, inviting people one by one, selecting those he knew to possess clarity of heart and courage.^1

## The Events

### The First Believers

History records that the first people to embrace Islam came from the Prophet's innermost circle:

**Khadijah bint Khuwaylid** was the first person to believe. Without the slightest hesitation, she affirmed what her husband had experienced in the Cave of Hira. Ibn Hisham narrates that Khadijah was the first human being to believe in Allah and affirm the message brought by the Messenger of Allah — she strengthened him in carrying out this mission.^1

**Ali ibn Abi Talib**, then only about ten years old, became the first child to embrace Islam. He had lived in the Prophet's household since childhood because Abu Talib faced financial difficulties, and so he witnessed the Prophet's daily life and worship firsthand.^3

**Zaid ibn Harithah**, a freed slave whom the Prophet had adopted, became the first freed person (*mawla*) to embrace Islam.^1

**Abu Bakr al-Siddiq** was the most influential companion during the secret da'wah phase. He was a highly respected Quraysh merchant known for his honesty and wisdom. When Prophet Muhammad SAW conveyed the message to him, Abu Bakr believed immediately without any hesitation — he asked for no miracles or additional proof.^2

### Abu Bakr's Role in the Early Da'wah

Abu Bakr did not merely believe; he became the second *da'i* after the Prophet Muhammad SAW. Leveraging his social network and reputation among Meccan merchants, he succeeded in bringing several prominent individuals to Islam. Among those who embraced Islam through Abu Bakr's efforts were **Uthman ibn Affan**, **Zubayr ibn al-Awwam**, **Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf**, **Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas**, and **Talhah ibn Ubaydullah** — five men who would become pillars of Islamic civilization.^2

Ibn Sa'd records in *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra* that those who embraced Islam in this early phase — known as *al-Sabiqun al-Awwalun* (the foremost pioneers) — numbered approximately forty individuals.^3

### Dar al-Arqam: The Secret Center of Da'wah

As the number of Muslims grew, a gathering place was needed to study the Qur'an and worship together. The choice fell upon the house of **Al-Arqam ibn Abi al-Arqam**, a young Qurayshi from the clan of Banu Makhzum who had embraced Islam. His house was located at the foot of Mount Safa — sufficiently secluded yet easily accessible.^1 ^4

It was here that the Prophet Muhammad SAW taught newly revealed Qur'anic verses, guided the companions in prayer, and built the spiritual foundation of the first Muslim community. Dar al-Arqam functioned simultaneously as a school, a mosque, and a coordination center — all under complete secrecy.^2

### Characteristics of the Secret Da'wah Phase

The da'wah during this phase had several important characteristics:

**First**, a personal approach (*da'wah fardiyyah*). Prophet Muhammad SAW and Abu Bakr approached people individually rather than through public declarations. They chose individuals known for their pure disposition and sound judgment.^2

**Second**, social diversity. From the very beginning, Islam made no distinction based on social class. Among the first Muslims were nobles like Uthman ibn Affan, merchants like Abu Bakr, freed slaves like Zaid ibn Harithah, women like Khadijah, and children like Ali. This diversity was a statement that Islam was a religion for all of humanity.^1

**Third**, character formation before confrontation. The three years of secret da'wah were not passive — they were a period of forging the core cadre who would become the backbone of Islamic civilization. They were nurtured with the Qur'an, taught firm faith, and trained to withstand pressure.^2

## Quotations and Evidence

The Qur'anic verses revealed during this period largely focused on three themes: strengthening faith, warnings about the Hereafter, and critiques of greed and social injustice. Among the early surahs revealed:

> وَٱلْعَصْرِ ﴿١﴾ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَفِى خُسْرٍ ﴿٢﴾ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"By time. ﴾1﴿ Indeed, mankind is in loss, ﴾2﴿ except those who believe and do righteous deeds and advise each other to truth and advise each other to patience. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. Al-'Asr (103): 1–3

This short surah encapsulates the four conditions of success that formed the foundation for nurturing the companions: faith, righteous deeds, mutual counsel toward truth, and mutual counsel toward patience.

> ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ﴿١﴾
>
> *"Read in the name of your Lord who created. ﴾1﴿"*
> — QS. Al-'Alaq (96): 1

The first command in Islam was to read — a revolutionary message in a predominantly illiterate society, affirming that Islam is fundamentally a religion of knowledge.

## Analysis and Lessons

### The Strategy of Gradual Da'wah

The secret da'wah teaches an important principle: great change need not begin with a grand movement. Prophet Muhammad SAW started from the smallest circle — family and closest friends — then gradually expanded his reach. This approach ensured that the foundation of faith was solid before facing external pressure.^2

### Dar al-Arqam as an Educational Model

The house of Al-Arqam became the prototype for the first Islamic educational institution. There, no social hierarchy existed — Quraysh nobles sat alongside freed slaves, learning together from the same source. This model became the blueprint for the madrasas and universities of later Islamic civilization.^1

### The Courage of Faith Amid Uncertainty

Embracing Islam during the secret phase required extraordinary courage. The *al-Sabiqun al-Awwalun* had no guarantee of safety, no large community to protect them, and no certainty that this movement would succeed. They believed solely because of the truth they perceived in Muhammad's teachings — and this courage became the primary capital for Islam's struggle in the stages that followed.^3

## Bibliography

1. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa et al. (Cairo: Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
2. Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman, *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum [The Sealed Nectar]* (Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976).
3. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*, vol. 1 (Beirut: Dar Sadir, 1968).
4. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-'Asr (103): 1–3; QS. Al-'Alaq (96): 1.

# Dakwah Sembunyi-Sembunyi — Tiga Tahun Pertama Penyebaran Islam (610–613 M)

## Konteks

Setelah wahyu pertama turun di Gua Hira dan jeda wahyu (*fatrah al-wahy*) berakhir, **Nabi Muhammad SAW** memulai misi dakwahnya dengan cara yang sangat hati-hati: secara sembunyi-sembunyi dan terbatas pada orang-orang terdekat.^1 Pendekatan ini bukan tanpa alasan. Makkah pada abad ke-7 Masehi dikuasai oleh oligarki Quraisy yang kehidupan ekonomi, sosial, dan politiknya sangat bergantung pada sistem keagamaan politeistik — penyembahan berhala di Ka'bah mendatangkan keuntungan besar dari para peziarah seluruh Jazirah Arab.^2

Mengumumkan ajaran tauhid secara terbuka di tengah kondisi seperti ini akan memicu reaksi keras yang dapat menghancurkan gerakan dakwah sebelum sempat berakar. Oleh karena itu, selama kurang lebih tiga tahun (610–613 M), Nabi Muhammad SAW menjalankan dakwah secara diam-diam, mengajak orang satu per satu, memilih mereka yang beliau kenal memiliki kejernihan hati dan keberanian.^1

## Peristiwa

### Orang-Orang Pertama yang Beriman

Sejarah mencatat bahwa orang-orang pertama yang menyambut seruan Islam berasal dari lingkaran terdekat Nabi Muhammad SAW:

**Khadijah binti Khuwaylid** adalah orang pertama yang beriman. Tanpa ragu sedikit pun, ia membenarkan apa yang dialami suaminya di Gua Hira. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa Khadijah adalah manusia pertama yang beriman kepada Allah dan membenarkan risalah yang dibawa Rasulullah SAW — ia memperkuat beliau dalam menjalankan misi ini.^1

**Ali bin Abi Thalib**, saat itu baru berusia sekitar sepuluh tahun, menjadi anak pertama yang masuk Islam. Ia tinggal di rumah Nabi Muhammad SAW sejak kecil karena Abu Thalib kesulitan ekonomi, sehingga ia menyaksikan langsung kehidupan dan ibadah Nabi sehari-hari.^3

**Zaid bin Haritsah**, mantan budak yang dibebaskan dan diadopsi oleh Nabi, menjadi orang merdeka pertama (*mawla*) yang memeluk Islam.^1

**Abu Bakr al-Shiddiq** adalah sahabat paling berpengaruh dalam fase dakwah sembunyi-sembunyi. Ia adalah saudagar Quraisy yang sangat dihormati, dikenal dengan kejujuran dan kebijaksanaannya. Ketika Nabi Muhammad SAW menyampaikan risalah kepadanya, Abu Bakr langsung beriman tanpa keraguan — ia tidak meminta mukjizat atau bukti tambahan.^2

### Peran Abu Bakr dalam Dakwah Awal

Abu Bakr tidak sekadar beriman; ia menjadi *dai* kedua setelah Nabi Muhammad SAW. Dengan memanfaatkan jaringan sosial dan reputasinya di kalangan pedagang Makkah, ia berhasil mengajak sejumlah orang terkemuka masuk Islam. Di antara mereka yang masuk Islam melalui dakwah Abu Bakr adalah **Utsman bin Affan**, **Zubair bin Awwam**, **Abdurrahman bin Auf**, **Sa'd bin Abi Waqqash**, dan **Thalhah bin Ubaidillah** — lima orang yang kelak menjadi pilar utama peradaban Islam.^2

Ibn Sa'd dalam *Al-Thabaqat al-Kubra* mencatat bahwa orang-orang yang masuk Islam pada fase awal ini — yang disebut *al-Sabiqun al-Awwalun* (perintis pertama) — berjumlah sekitar empat puluhan orang.^3

### Dar al-Arqam: Pusat Dakwah Rahasia

Seiring bertambahnya jumlah muslim, diperlukan tempat untuk berkumpul, belajar Al-Qur'an, dan beribadah secara bersama. Pilihan jatuh pada rumah **Al-Arqam bin Abi al-Arqam**, seorang pemuda Quraisy dari Bani Makhzum yang telah masuk Islam. Rumahnya terletak di kaki bukit Shafa — lokasi yang cukup tersembunyi namun mudah dijangkau.^1 ^4

Di sinilah Nabi Muhammad SAW mengajarkan ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an yang baru turun, membimbing para sahabat dalam salat, dan membangun fondasi spiritual komunitas muslim pertama. Dar al-Arqam berfungsi sebagai madrasah, masjid, dan pusat koordinasi sekaligus — semua dalam kerahasiaan penuh.^2

### Karakteristik Dakwah Fase Sembunyi

Dakwah pada fase ini memiliki beberapa karakteristik penting:

**Pertama**, pendekatan personal (*da'wah fardiyyah*). Nabi Muhammad SAW dan Abu Bakr mendatangi orang-orang secara individual, bukan melalui seruan publik. Mereka memilih orang-orang yang dikenal memiliki fitrah yang bersih dan akal yang jernih.^2

**Kedua**, keberagaman sosial. Sejak awal, Islam tidak membedakan kelas sosial. Di antara muslim pertama terdapat bangsawan seperti Utsman bin Affan, pedagang seperti Abu Bakr, mantan budak seperti Zaid bin Haritsah, perempuan seperti Khadijah, dan anak-anak seperti Ali. Keberagaman ini menjadi pernyataan bahwa Islam adalah agama untuk seluruh manusia.^1

**Ketiga**, pembentukan karakter sebelum konfrontasi. Tiga tahun dakwah sembunyi-sembunyi bukan masa pasif — ini adalah masa pembentukan kader inti yang kelak akan menjadi tulang punggung peradaban Islam. Mereka digembleng dengan Al-Qur'an, diajarkan keimanan yang kokoh, dan dibiasakan menghadapi tekanan.^2

## Dialog dan Dalil

Ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an yang turun pada periode ini sebagian besar berfokus pada tiga tema: penguatan keimanan, peringatan tentang akhirat, dan kritik terhadap keserakahan serta ketidakadilan sosial. Di antara surah-surah awal yang turun:

> وَٱلْعَصْرِ ﴿١﴾ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَفِى خُسْرٍ ﴿٢﴾ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَوْا۟ بِٱلصَّبْرِ ﴿٣﴾
>
> *"Demi masa. ﴾1﴿ Sesungguhnya manusia berada dalam kerugian, ﴾2﴿ kecuali orang-orang yang beriman dan mengerjakan kebajikan serta saling menasihati untuk kebenaran dan saling menasihati untuk kesabaran. ﴾3﴿"*
> — QS. Al-'Ashr (103): 1–3

Surah pendek ini merangkum empat syarat keberuntungan yang menjadi fondasi pembinaan para sahabat: iman, amal saleh, saling menasihati dalam kebenaran, dan saling menasihati dalam kesabaran.

> ٱقْرَأْ بِٱسْمِ رَبِّكَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ﴿١﴾
>
> *"Bacalah dengan (menyebut) nama Tuhanmu yang menciptakan. ﴾1﴿"*
> — QS. Al-'Alaq (96): 1

Perintah pertama dalam Islam adalah membaca — sebuah pesan revolusioner di tengah masyarakat yang mayoritas buta huruf, sekaligus menegaskan bahwa Islam adalah agama ilmu pengetahuan.

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Strategi Dakwah Bertahap

Dakwah sembunyi-sembunyi mengajarkan prinsip penting: perubahan besar tidak harus dimulai dengan gerakan besar. Nabi Muhammad SAW memulai dari lingkaran terkecil — keluarga dan sahabat terdekat — lalu perlahan memperluas jangkauan. Pendekatan ini memastikan bahwa fondasi iman sudah kokoh sebelum menghadapi tekanan dari luar.^2

### Dar al-Arqam sebagai Model Pendidikan

Rumah Al-Arqam menjadi prototipe lembaga pendidikan Islam pertama. Di sana tidak ada hierarki sosial — bangsawan Quraisy duduk berdampingan dengan mantan budak, belajar bersama dari sumber yang sama. Model ini menjadi cetak biru bagi madrasah dan universitas Islam di kemudian hari.^1

### Keberanian Iman di Tengah Ketidakpastian

Memeluk Islam pada fase sembunyi-sembunyi memerlukan keberanian luar biasa. Para *al-Sabiqun al-Awwalun* tidak memiliki jaminan keamanan, tidak ada komunitas besar yang melindungi, dan tidak ada kepastian bahwa gerakan ini akan berhasil. Mereka beriman semata-mata karena kebenaran yang mereka rasakan dalam ajaran yang dibawa Muhammad — dan keberanian ini menjadi modal utama perjuangan Islam di tahap-tahap selanjutnya.^3

## Daftar Pustaka

1. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, jilid 1, tahqiq Musthafa al-Saqqa dkk. (Kairo: Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
2. Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman, *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976).
3. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Thabaqat al-Kubra*, jilid 1 (Beirut: Dar Shadir, 1968).

4. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-'Ashr (103): 1–3; QS. Al-'Alaq (96): 1.

# The Call from the Hill! 📢

## 🤫 From Secret to Open

Remember the story of the secret da'wah? For three years, Prophet Muhammad SAW invited people to Islam quietly. But now, Allah commanded something new:

"And warn your closest kindred! ﴾214﴿" (QS. Al-Shu'ara': 214)

It was time to speak openly! No more hiding. Prophet Muhammad SAW had to deliver the message of Islam to everyone — starting with his own family.

How nerve-wracking! Three years of secrecy, and now he had to stand before everyone and say: "I bring a message from Allah." But the Prophet was not afraid, because he knew this was Allah's command.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Prophet Muhammad SAW stands at the foot of Mount Safa, looking upward. At the summit, bright morning sunlight shines. Behind him, the silhouette of Mecca with stone houses. Golden morning light, brown hillside, clear blue sky. Mood full of determination and courage. Vertical composition — figure below, hill and light above.*

---

## 🍽️ The Family Dinner

The Prophet's first step was to invite his extended family — Banu Hashim — for a meal together. He prepared good food, and about forty relatives came.

After eating, Prophet Muhammad SAW stood and said: "O my family, I bring you something better than anything else — the good of this world and the Hereafter."

But before he could finish, his uncle **Abu Lahab** cut him off: "Enough! Everyone leave!" And the gathering broke up.

Did the Prophet give up? No! He held another dinner and delivered his message again. That's our Prophet — he never gave up!

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Large room with mats on the floor. Many people sit in a circle with plates of food in the center. Prophet Muhammad SAW stands on one side, speaking with a raised hand. In the corner, an angry older man (Abu Lahab) is about to stand. Warm room colors, yellow lamplight, varied clothing. Tense but determined mood. Wide composition — many figures, focus on the standing Prophet.*

---

## 📢 The Shout on Mount Safa

After inviting his family, Prophet Muhammad SAW did something incredibly brave. He climbed to the top of **Mount Safa** in the center of Mecca and shouted:

**"YAA SHABAAHAH!"** which means: **"DANGER AT DAWN!"**

This was like a fire alarm! Everyone in Mecca rushed over because that shout was used to warn of enemy attacks.

When everyone had gathered, the Prophet asked: "If I told you there was an army behind that hill about to attack you, would you believe me?"

Everyone answered: **"Of course! You have never lied to us!"**

Then the Prophet said: **"Then listen. I am a warner from Allah. Save yourselves from the Hellfire!"**

He called out to each family one by one. He even named his own daughter, Fatimah: "O Fatimah, I cannot help you before Allah. Save yourself!"

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Prophet Muhammad SAW stands atop Mount Safa, hand raised, calling out. Below, hundreds of Meccans look up with various expressions — shocked, curious, angry. The city of Mecca visible in the background with the Ka'bah in the distance. Blue sky, brown hill, colorful crowd clothing. Dramatic and tense mood. Bottom-to-top composition — crowd below, Prophet at the peak.*

---

## 😤 Angry Abu Lahab

After the Prophet finished speaking, everyone was silent. Then Abu Lahab stood and shouted: **"May you perish! Is this what you gathered us for?!"**

Abu Lahab was furious. He was the Prophet's uncle who opposed Islam the most fiercely. His wife, **Umm Jamil**, even placed sharp thorns on paths the Prophet walked so his feet would be hurt!

But happened? Allah revealed a special surah about Abu Lahab — **Surah al-Lahab** — declaring that Abu Lahab's wealth and power would not save him.

The lesson: wealth and power mean nothing if our hearts reject the truth.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Two contrasting sides. On the left, Abu Lahab red-faced with anger, finger pointing, mouth open shouting. On the right, Prophet Muhammad SAW stands calm with a peaceful, patient face. Between them, light separates — dark on Abu Lahab's side, bright on the Prophet's side. Contrasting colors: red-dark vs white-gold. Dramatic mood. Split composition — two opposing figures.*

---

## 🌟 Lessons for Us

**Courage means speaking the truth.** Prophet Muhammad SAW knew people would be angry. But he delivered Allah's message anyway because it was the right thing to do.

**No favoritism in truth.** The Prophet even named his own daughter — meaning truth applies to everyone without exception, including family.

**If you fail once, try again!** The first dinner failed because Abu Lahab broke it up. But the Prophet didn't give up — he tried again and again.

**Insults are not the end.** Abu Lahab insulted the Prophet in front of everyone. But the Prophet didn't insult back. He stayed calm and continued his mission. What extraordinary character!

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A child stands before classmates, hands slightly trembling but face full of determination, delivering something important. listen — some smile supportively, some watch curiously. Light from a window illuminates the child. Bright classroom colors, warm light. Mood full of courage. Focused composition — one child in front, behind.*
dakwah-terang-terangan-dari-bukit-shafa.children-id:
# Seruan dari Bukit! 📢

## 🤫 Dari Rahasia Menjadi Terbuka

Ingat kisah dakwah sembunyi-sembunyi? Selama tiga tahun, Nabi Muhammad SAW mengajak orang masuk Islam secara diam-diam. Tapi sekarang, Allah memerintahkan sesuatu yang baru:

"Peringatilah kerabat-kerabatmu yang terdekat! ﴾214﴿" (QS. Al-Syu'ara': 214)

Artinya, waktunya berbicara terang-terangan sudah tiba! Tidak ada lagi sembunyi-sembunyi. Nabi Muhammad SAW harus menyampaikan pesan Islam kepada semua orang — dimulai dari keluarganya sendiri.

Betapa gugupnya! Selama tiga tahun bersembunyi, dan sekarang harus berdiri di depan semua orang dan berkata: "Aku membawa pesan dari Allah." Tapi Nabi Muhammad SAW tidak gentar, karena beliau tahu ini perintah Allah.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Nabi Muhammad SAW berdiri di kaki bukit Shafa, mendongak ke atas. Di puncak bukit, cahaya matahari pagi bersinar terang. Di belakang beliau, bayangan kota Makkah dengan rumah-rumah batu. Warna emas cahaya pagi, coklat bukit, biru langit cerah. Mood penuh tekad dan keberanian. Komposisi vertikal — figur di bawah, bukit dan cahaya di atas.*

---

## 🍽️ Makan Malam Keluarga

Langkah pertama Nabi Muhammad SAW adalah mengundang keluarga besarnya — Bani Hasyim — untuk makan bersama. Beliau menyiapkan makanan yang enak, dan sekitar empat puluh orang kerabat datang.

Setelah makan, Nabi Muhammad SAW berdiri dan berkata: "Wahai keluargaku, aku datang membawa sesuatu yang lebih baik dari apa pun — kebaikan dunia dan akhirat."

Tapi sebelum beliau selesai, paman beliau yang bernama **Abu Lahab** langsung memotong: "Sudahlah! Bubar semua!" Dan pertemuan itu pun bubar.

Apakah Nabi menyerah? Tidak! Beliau mengadakan makan malam lagi dan menyampaikan pesannya lagi. Itulah Nabi kita — tidak pernah menyerah!

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Ruangan besar dengan tikar-tikar di lantai. Banyak orang duduk melingkar dengan piring-piring makanan di tengah. Nabi Muhammad SAW berdiri di satu sisi, berbicara dengan tangan terangkat. Di pojok, seorang pria tua (Abu Lahab) berwajah marah hendak berdiri. Warna hangat ruangan, kuning lampu, beragam warna pakaian. Mood tegang tapi penuh tekad. Komposisi lebar — banyak figur, fokus pada Nabi yang berdiri.*

---

## 📢 Teriakan di Bukit Shafa

Setelah mengajak keluarga, Nabi Muhammad SAW melakukan sesuatu yang sangat berani. Beliau naik ke atas **Bukit Shafa** di tengah kota Makkah dan berteriak:

**"YAA SHABAAHAH!"** yang artinya: **"BAHAYA DI PAGI HARI!"**

Ini seperti alarm kebakaran! Semua orang di Makkah bergegas datang karena teriakan itu biasa dipakai untuk memperingatkan serangan musuh.

Ketika semua sudah berkumpul, Nabi bertanya: "Kalau aku bilang ada pasukan musuh di balik bukit itu yang mau menyerang kalian, apakah kalian percaya?"

Semua orang menjawab: **"Tentu saja! Kamu tidak pernah bohong!"**

Lalu Nabi berkata: **"Kalau begitu, dengarkanlah. Aku adalah pemberi peringatan dari Allah. Selamatkanlah diri kalian dari api neraka!"**

Beliau memanggil setiap keluarga satu per satu. Bahkan beliau menyebut nama putrinya sendiri, Fatimah: "Wahai Fatimah, aku tidak bisa menolongmu di hadapan Allah. Selamatkanlah dirimu sendiri!"

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Nabi Muhammad SAW berdiri di puncak Bukit Shafa, tangan terangkat, mulut terbuka berseru. Di bawah, ratusan orang Makkah mendongak dengan berbagai ekspresi — ada yang terkejut, ada yang penasaran, ada yang marah. Kota Makkah terlihat di latar belakang dengan Ka'bah di kejauhan. Warna biru langit, coklat bukit, warna-warni pakaian kerumunan. Mood dramatis dan penuh ketegangan. Komposisi dari bawah ke atas — kerumunan di bawah, Nabi di puncak.*

---

## 😤 Abu Lahab yang Marah

Setelah Nabi selesai berbicara, semua orang terdiam. Lalu Abu Lahab berdiri dan berteriak: **"Celakalah kamu! Untuk inikah kamu mengumpulkan kami?!"**

Abu Lahab sangat marah. Dia adalah paman Nabi yang paling menentang Islam. Istrinya, **Ummu Jamil**, bahkan sering meletakkan duri-duri tajam di jalan yang biasa dilalui Nabi agar kaki beliau terluka!

Tapi tahukah apa yang terjadi? Allah menurunkan surah khusus tentang Abu Lahab — **Surah al-Lahab** — yang menyatakan bahwa harta dan kekuasaan Abu Lahab tidak akan bisa menolongnya.

Pelajarannya: kekayaan dan kekuasaan tidak ada artinya kalau hati kita menolak kebenaran.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Dua sisi yang kontras. Di sisi kiri, Abu Lahab berwajah merah marah, telunjuk teracung, mulut terbuka berteriak. Di sisi kanan, Nabi Muhammad SAW berdiri tenang dengan wajah damai dan sabar. Di antara keduanya, cahaya memisahkan — gelap di sisi Abu Lahab, terang di sisi Nabi. Warna kontras: merah-gelap vs putih-emas. Mood dramatis. Komposisi split — dua figur berlawanan.*

---

## 🌟 Pelajaran untuk Kita

**Keberanian adalah menyampaikan kebenaran.** Nabi Muhammad SAW tahu bahwa orang-orang akan marah. Tapi beliau tetap menyampaikan pesan Allah karena itu adalah hal yang benar.

**Tidak ada pilih kasih dalam kebenaran.** Nabi bahkan menyebut nama putrinya sendiri — artinya kebenaran berlaku untuk semua orang tanpa kecuali, termasuk keluarga sendiri.

**Kalau gagal sekali, coba lagi!** Makan malam pertama gagal karena Abu Lahab membubarkan. Tapi Nabi tidak menyerah — beliau mencoba lagi dan lagi.

**Hinaan bukan akhir dari segalanya.** Abu Lahab menghina Nabi di depan semua orang. Tapi Nabi tidak membalas dengan hinaan. Beliau tetap tenang dan melanjutkan tugasnya. Itulah akhlak yang.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Seorang anak berdiri di depan nya di kelas, tangan sedikit gemetar tapi wajah penuh tekad, sedang menyampaikan sesuatu yang penting. nya mendengarkan — ada yang tersenyum mendukung, ada yang menatap penasaran. Cahaya dari jendela menyinari si anak. Warna cerah kelas, cahaya hangat. Mood penuh keberanian. Komposisi fokus — satu anak di depan, di belakang.*
dakwah-terang-terangan-dari-bukit-shafa.general-en:
# The Open Da'wah — The Proclamation from Mount Safa (613 CE)

## Context

After three years of secret da'wah, the number of Muslims in Mecca had reached approximately forty. The foundation of faith had been laid, the core cadre had been formed, and now the time had come for Allah's command to expand the reach of the mission. The verse descended:^1 ^3

> وَأَنذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ ﴿٢١٤﴾
>
> *"And warn your closest kindred. ﴾214﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Shu'ara' (26): 214

This verse marked the transition from *sirriyyah* (secret) da'wah to *jahriyyah* (open) da'wah. Mecca at this time was still entirely dominated by the Quraysh elite whose livelihood depended on the idol-worship system. An open call to monotheism meant challenging the entire social, economic, and religious order that had been established for centuries.^2

## The Events

### The Banquet for Banu Hashim

Ibn Hisham narrates that the Prophet's first step after receiving this command was to invite the extended family of Banu Hashim and Banu Abd al-Muttalib to a banquet.^2 He prepared food and invited approximately forty people from his closest relatives.

At this gathering, Prophet Muhammad SAW presented his message openly before his family for the first time. He offered the teachings of monotheism and asked for their support. However, before he could finish speaking, **Abu Lahab** — the Prophet's own uncle — interrupted and dispersed the gathering with harsh words.^2

The Prophet did not give up. He held a second banquet and delivered his message again. This time, he addressed his relatives firmly, as narrated by Ibn Hisham:

"O Banu Abd al-Muttalib, by Allah, I do not know of any young man among the Arabs who has brought to his people something better than what I have brought to you. I have brought you the good of this world and the Hereafter."^2

### The Proclamation from Mount Safa

After addressing his family, the next step was far more dramatic. Prophet Muhammad SAW ascended **Mount Safa** — a small hill in the center of Mecca traditionally used for making important announcements — and called out in a loud voice:

"Yaa Shabaahah!" ("Danger at dawn!")^1

This was the traditional Arab alarm used to warn of enemy attack. Every Quraysh clan — Banu Hashim, Banu Abd al-Muttalib, Banu Zuhrah, Banu Taym, Banu Makhzum, and others — rushed to Mount Safa. Those who could not come sent representatives.^1

When all had gathered, Prophet Muhammad SAW asked:

"What would you think if I told you there was a cavalry behind this valley about to attack you — would you believe me?"^1

They responded spontaneously: "Yes! We have never known you to lie."^1

Then the Prophet declared:

"Indeed, I am a warner to you before a severe punishment."^1

In the narration recorded by al-Bukhari, he called out to each clan individually:

> *"O Banu Abd al-Muttalib, O Banu Abd Manaf, O Banu Zuhrah... Save yourselves from the Fire, for I have no power to protect you from Allah in any way."*
> — Sahih al-Bukhari, no. 2753, Kitab al-Wasaya

### Abu Lahab's Reaction and the Revelation of Surah al-Lahab

In the tense silence following the Prophet's proclamation, **Abu Lahab** stood up and said with fury:

"May you perish! Is this what you gathered us for?"^1

Abu Lahab — whose real name was Abd al-'Uzza ibn Abd al-Muttalib — was the Prophet's uncle most fiercely opposed to Islam. His reaction at Mount Safa became the occasion for the revelation of an entire surah:

> تَبَّتْ يَدَآ أَبِى لَهَبٍ وَتَبَّ ﴿١﴾ مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَمَا كَسَبَ ﴿٢﴾ سَيَصْلَىٰ نَارًا ذَاتَ لَهَبٍ ﴿٣﴾ وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ حَمَّالَةَ ٱلْحَطَبِ ﴿٤﴾ فِى جِيدِهَا حَبْلٌ مِّن مَّسَدٍ ﴿٥﴾
>
> *"May the hands of Abu Lahab perish, and may he perish. ﴾1﴿ His wealth and what he earned will not benefit him. ﴾2﴿ He will burn in a Fire of blazing flame. ﴾3﴿ And his wife — the carrier of firewood (spreader of slander). ﴾4﴿ Around her neck is a rope of palm fiber. ﴾5﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Lahab (111): 1–5

Ibn Kathir explains in his tafsir that this surah was a direct response to Abu Lahab's rejection and to the role of his wife, **Umm Jamil bint Harb** (Abu Sufyan's sister), who frequently scattered thorns on the Prophet's path and spread malicious rumors about him among the Quraysh.

## Quotations and Evidence

The event at Mount Safa contains several extraordinary lessons in the rhetoric of da'wah:

**First**, the Prophet established credibility before delivering his message. He asked: "Would you believe me?" The positive response from the Quraysh — "We have never known you to lie" — created a moral foundation that made it difficult for them to reject what followed without contradiction.^1

**Second**, he used an analogy his audience deeply understood. The threat of cavalry behind a valley was a real danger in Arabian tribal life. Through this analogy, he translated the concept of divine punishment into something that felt tangible and urgent.^1

**Third**, he addressed each clan individually — ensuring no one felt the message was not for them. Even his closest family received no exemption:

> *"O Fatimah bint Muhammad, ask me whatever you wish from my wealth, but I cannot avail you anything against Allah."*
> — Sahih al-Bukhari, no. 2753

## Analysis and Lessons

### A Historical Turning Point

The event at Mount Safa marked an irreversible turning point. Before it, Islam was an underground movement that could be ignored. After Mount Safa, Islam became an open challenge to the status quo of Mecca. From this point, there was no turning back — neither for the Prophet nor for the Quraysh.^2

### Justice Without Exception

One of the most striking aspects of the Mount Safa proclamation was the Prophet's insistence that family ties confer no privilege before Allah. He explicitly named his own daughter, Fatimah, to emphasize that salvation comes only through faith and deeds, not through lineage or proximity to the Prophet.^1

### Response to Rejection

Abu Lahab's reaction and the revelation of Surah al-Lahab demonstrate that rejection of truth carries eternal consequences. Yet the Prophet's response to this rejection also serves as a model: he did not answer insult with insult, did not resort to violence, and continued his mission with unwavering resolve.^2

## Bibliography

1. Al-Bukhari, Muhammad ibn Isma'il, *Sahih al-Bukhari* (Damascus: Dar Ibn Kathir, 2002).
2. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa et al. (Cairo: Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
3. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-Shu'ara' (26): 214; QS. Al-Lahab (111): 1–5.

# Dakwah Terang-Terangan — Seruan dari Bukit Shafa (613 M)

## Konteks

Setelah tiga tahun dakwah secara sembunyi-sembunyi, jumlah muslim di Makkah telah mencapai sekitar empat puluhan orang. Fondasi keimanan telah dibangun, kader-kader inti telah terbentuk, dan kini tibalah saatnya perintah Allah untuk memperluas jangkauan dakwah. Turunlah ayat:^1

> وَأَنذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ ٱلْأَقْرَبِينَ ﴿٢١٤﴾
>
> *"Dan berilah peringatan kepada kerabat-kerabatmu yang terdekat. ﴾214﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Syu'ara' (26): 214

Ayat ini menandai peralihan dari dakwah *sirriyyah* (sembunyi-sembunyi) ke dakwah *jahriyyah* (terang-terangan). Makkah pada saat itu masih sepenuhnya dikuasai oleh elit Quraisy yang bergantung pada sistem penyembahan berhala. Seruan tauhid secara terbuka berarti menantang seluruh tatanan sosial, ekonomi, dan keagamaan yang telah mapan selama berabad-abad.^2

## Peristiwa

### Jamuan Makan untuk Bani Hasyim

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa langkah pertama Nabi Muhammad SAW setelah turunnya perintah ini adalah mengundang keluarga besar Bani Hasyim dan Bani Abdul Muththalib ke sebuah jamuan makan.^2 Beliau menyiapkan makanan dan mengundang sekitar empat puluh orang dari kerabat terdekat.

Dalam pertemuan ini, Nabi Muhammad SAW menyampaikan risalahnya secara terbuka untuk pertama kali di hadapan keluarganya. Beliau menawarkan ajaran tauhid dan meminta dukungan mereka. Namun, sebelum beliau selesai berbicara, **Abu Lahab** — paman Nabi sendiri — memotong pembicaraan dan membubarkan pertemuan dengan ucapan kasar.^2

Nabi Muhammad SAW tidak menyerah. Beliau mengadakan jamuan kedua dan kembali menyampaikan pesannya. Kali ini, beliau berkata kepada para kerabatnya dengan tegas, sebagaimana diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Hisham:

"Wahai Bani Abdul Muththalib, demi Allah, aku tidak mengetahui ada seorang pemuda Arab yang datang kepada kaumnya dengan membawa sesuatu yang lebih baik daripada apa yang aku bawa kepada kalian. Aku datang membawa kebaikan dunia dan akhirat."^2

### Seruan dari Bukit Shafa

Setelah menyampaikan dakwah kepada keluarga, langkah berikutnya jauh lebih dramatis. Nabi Muhammad SAW naik ke atas **Bukit Shafa** — sebuah bukit kecil di tengah Makkah yang biasa digunakan untuk menyampaikan pengumuman penting — dan berseru dengan suara lantang:

"Yaa Shabaahah!" ("Bahaya di pagi hari!")^1

Seruan ini adalah alarm tradisional Arab yang digunakan untuk memperingatkan serangan musuh. Seluruh klan Quraisy — Bani Hasyim, Bani Abdul Muththalib, Bani Zuhrah, Bani Taim, Bani Makhzum, dan lainnya — bergegas datang ke bukit Shafa. Mereka yang tidak bisa datang mengirim perwakilan.^1

Ketika semua telah berkumpul, Nabi Muhammad SAW bertanya:

"Bagaimana pendapat kalian jika aku memberitahu kalian bahwa ada pasukan berkuda di balik lembah ini yang hendak menyerang kalian, apakah kalian akan mempercayaiku?"^1

Mereka menjawab dengan spontan: "Ya! Kami tidak pernah mendapatimu berdusta."^1

Maka Nabi Muhammad SAW bersabda:

"Sesungguhnya aku adalah pemberi peringatan bagi kalian di hadapan azab yang berat."^1

Dalam hadits riwayat al-Bukhari, beliau menyeru setiap klan satu per satu:

> يَا بَنِي عَبْدِ الْمُطَّلِبِ، يَا بَنِي عَبْدِ مَنَافٍ، يَا بَنِي زُهْرَةَ... أَنْقِذُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ مِنَ النَّارِ، فَإِنِّي لاَ أَمْلِكُ لَكُمْ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا
>
> *"Wahai Bani Abdul Muththalib, wahai Bani Abdi Manaf, wahai Bani Zuhrah... Selamatkanlah diri kalian dari api neraka, karena sesungguhnya aku tidak memiliki kuasa sedikit pun untuk menolong kalian dari (azab) Allah."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 2753, Kitab al-Washaya

### Reaksi Abu Lahab dan Turunnya Surah al-Lahab

Di tengah keheningan yang mencekam setelah seruan Nabi, **Abu Lahab** berdiri dan berkata dengan penuh kemarahan:

"Celakalah engkau! Inikah yang engkau kumpulkan kami untuknya?"^1

Abu Lahab — yang nama aslinya Abdul 'Uzza bin Abdul Muththalib — adalah paman Nabi yang paling keras menentang dakwah Islam. Reaksinya di Bukit Shafa ini menjadi sebab turunnya salah satu surah Al-Qur'an^3:

> تَبَّتْ يَدَآ أَبِى لَهَبٍ وَتَبَّ ﴿١﴾ مَآ أَغْنَىٰ عَنْهُ مَالُهُۥ وَمَا كَسَبَ ﴿٢﴾ سَيَصْلَىٰ نَارًا ذَاتَ لَهَبٍ ﴿٣﴾ وَٱمْرَأَتُهُۥ حَمَّالَةَ ٱلْحَطَبِ ﴿٤﴾ فِى جِيدِهَا حَبْلٌ مِّن مَّسَدٍ ﴿٥﴾
>
> *"Binasalah kedua tangan Abu Lahab dan benar-benar binasa dia. ﴾1﴿ Tidaklah berguna baginya harta bendanya dan apa yang dia usahakan. ﴾2﴿ Kelak dia akan masuk ke dalam api yang bergejolak (neraka). ﴾3﴿ Dan (begitu pula) istrinya, pembawa kayu bakar (penyebar fitnah). ﴾4﴿ Di lehernya ada tali dari sabut yang dipintal. ﴾5﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Lahab (111): 1–5

Ibn Kathir dalam tafsirnya menjelaskan bahwa surah ini merupakan respons langsung terhadap penolakan Abu Lahab dan juga terhadap peran istrinya, **Ummu Jamil binti Harb** (saudari Abu Sufyan), yang kerap menaburkan duri di jalan yang dilalui Nabi Muhammad SAW dan menyebarkan fitnah tentang beliau di kalangan Quraisy.

## Dialog dan Dalil

Peristiwa di Bukit Shafa mengandung beberapa pelajaran retorika dakwah yang luar biasa:

**Pertama**, Nabi Muhammad SAW membangun kredibilitas sebelum menyampaikan pesan. Beliau bertanya: "Apakah kalian mempercayaiku?" Jawaban positif dari Quraisy — "Kami tidak pernah mendapatimu berdusta" — menjadi fondasi moral yang membuat mereka sulit menolak pesan selanjutnya tanpa kontradiksi.^1

**Kedua**, beliau menggunakan analogi yang sangat dipahami oleh audiensnya. Ancaman pasukan berkuda di balik lembah adalah bahaya nyata yang sering terjadi dalam kehidupan suku-suku Arab. Dengan analogi ini, beliau menerjemahkan konsep azab akhirat menjadi sesuatu yang terasa nyata dan mendesak.^1

**Ketiga**, beliau menyeru setiap klan secara individual — memastikan tidak ada yang merasa pesan ini bukan untuk mereka. Bahkan keluarga terdekatnya pun tidak mendapat pengecualian:

> يَا فَاطِمَةُ بِنْتَ مُحَمَّدٍ، سَلِينِي مَا شِئْتِ مِنْ مَالِي، لاَ أُغْنِي عَنْكِ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئًا
>
> *"Wahai Fatimah binti Muhammad, mintalah kepadaku apa pun dari hartaku, (namun) aku tidak mampu menolong kalian sedikit pun dari (azab) Allah."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 2753

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Titik Balik Sejarah

Peristiwa Bukit Shafa menandai titik balik yang tidak dapat dikembalikan. Sebelumnya, Islam adalah gerakan bawah tanah yang bisa diabaikan. Setelah Bukit Shafa, Islam menjadi tantangan terbuka terhadap status quo Makkah. Dari titik ini, tidak ada jalan kembali — baik bagi Nabi Muhammad SAW maupun bagi Quraisy.^2

### Keadilan Tanpa Pengecualian

Salah satu aspek paling mencolok dari seruan Bukit Shafa adalah ketegasan Nabi bahwa hubungan kekeluargaan tidak memberikan keistimewaan di hadapan Allah. Beliau secara eksplisit menyebut nama putrinya sendiri, Fatimah, untuk menegaskan bahwa keselamatan hanya melalui iman dan amal, bukan melalui nasab atau kedekatan dengan Nabi.^1

### Respons terhadap Penolakan

Reaksi Abu Lahab dan turunnya Surah al-Lahab menunjukkan bahwa penolakan terhadap kebenaran memiliki konsekuensi abadi. Namun, respons Nabi Muhammad SAW terhadap penolakan ini juga menjadi teladan: beliau tidak membalas hinaan Abu Lahab dengan hinaan, tidak menggunakan kekerasan, dan terus melanjutkan dakwah dengan penuh keteguhan.^2

## Daftar Pustaka

1. Al-Bukhari, Muhammad bin Ismail, *Shahih al-Bukhari* (Damaskus: Dar Ibn Kathir, 2002).
2. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, jilid 1, tahqiq Musthafa al-Saqqa dkk. (Kairo: Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
3. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-Syu'ara' (26): 214; QS. Al-Lahab (111): 1–5.

# A Journey Across the Sea! ⛵

## ⚔️ Why Did They Have to Leave?

Living in a place where bad people keep hurting you just because you believe in Allah. They hit you, mock you, and forbid you from praying.

That's what happened to Muslims in Mecca. After Prophet Muhammad SAW began preaching openly, the Quraysh became even angrier and crueler. They tortured Muslims — especially those who were poor and had no protection.

Prophet Muhammad SAW was deeply worried about his companions' safety. He thought: "Where is a safe place for them?"

Then he remembered a faraway land across the Red Sea — called **Abyssinia** (now known as Ethiopia). There lived a very just king named **the Negus** (Najashi). Even though the Negus was Christian, he was famous for never treating anyone unfairly.

Prophet Muhammad SAW told his companions: "Go to Abyssinia. There is a king who never wrongs anyone."

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A simple cartoon-style map showing Mecca on one side and Abyssinia on the other, separated by a blue Red Sea. A small boat sails in the middle. Dark clouds over Mecca (symbolizing sadness), bright sun over Abyssinia (symbolizing hope). Bright, child-friendly colors. Adventurous mood. Horizontal composition — two landmasses with sea between them.*

---

## 🛡️ The Story

The first group to leave numbered about sixteen people, including **Uthman ibn Affan** and his wife **Ruqayyah** — the Prophet's own daughter! They left secretly at night, slipped to the coast, and boarded a boat to cross the Red Sea.

How sad it must have been to leave home, family, and the land where they grew up. But they knew it was for their safety.

Soon after, a much larger group followed — about a hundred people! They were led by **Ja'far ibn Abi Talib**, the Prophet's cousin who was very clever and a wonderful speaker.

When they arrived in Abyssinia, King Negus welcomed them kindly and gave them a safe place to live. At last, after months of living in fear, they could worship in peace!

But the story wasn't over yet..

### The Quraysh Wouldn't Give Up!

The Quraysh were furious when they learned the Muslims had escaped to Abyssinia. They sent two envoys carrying expensive gifts for the Negus. The envoys said: "O King, these people are rebels. Send them back to us!"

The Negus was a just king. He wouldn't make a decision without hearing both sides. He called the Muslims to his palace and asked: "What do you have to say for yourselves?"

Then **Ja'far** stood up and delivered an amazing speech. He described how their lives were before Islam — full of wickedness and ignorance — and how Islam transformed them into honest, kind, and just people.

Then the Negus asked: "Do you have anything from Allah's revelation?"

Ja'far recited verses from **Surah Maryam** — the chapter about Mary and Jesus. When the Negus heard those verses, tears streamed down his face! He declared:

**"Indeed, this comes from the same source of light as what Jesus brought!"**

Then he turned to the Quraysh envoys and said firmly: **"Go! By Allah, I will never hand them over to you!"**

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A magnificent palace hall. The Negus sits on his throne, tears on his cheeks. Before him, Ja'far stands tall with his hand on his chest, having just recited the Qur'an. Behind Ja'far, a group of Muslims stand hopefully. On the other side, two Quraysh envoys look disappointed. Gold palace tones, red royal robes, white clothing of Ja'far. Dramatic and touching mood. Three-group composition — king center-top, Muslims left, Quraysh right.*

---

## 🕊️ What Happened After

The Muslims lived safely in Abyssinia for many years. They could worship peacefully, study their religion, and build their community. King Negus protected them from all threats.

What's amazing is that even though the Negus was Christian, he deeply respected the Muslims. Years later, when the Negus passed away, Prophet Muhammad SAW performed a special funeral prayer for him from far away (called salat al-gha'ib)! This shows how much the Prophet valued the Negus's kindness and justice.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A peaceful scene in Abyssinia. A group of Muslims sit under a large tree — some reading the Qur'an, some teaching children, some praying. In the background, green Ethiopian mountains and a clear blue sky. Greens, blues, warm browns. Mood full of peace and gratitude. Wide composition — beautiful nature with small, happy figures.*

---

## 💪 Lessons for Us

**If you're being treated unfairly, seek help.** Prophet Muhammad SAW taught that when we cannot survive in a dangerous place, it's okay to go somewhere safe. What matters is that we hold on to our faith.

**Justice knows no religion.** The Negus was a Christian king who protected Muslims. Goodness and justice can come from anyone. We should also be fair to everyone, no matter their religion or background.

**Honesty is stronger than gifts.** The Quraysh brought expensive presents, but Ja'far brought only the truth. And truth won! This teaches us that honesty is always worth more than riches.

**Never be ashamed to tell the truth about yourself.** Ja'far wasn't ashamed to say that his people were once ignorant and wicked. It was precisely his honesty that made the Negus trust him.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Two hands clasping — one lighter-skinned (representing the Negus), one darker-skinned (representing Ja'far). In the background, a flag of peace flies. Above the handshake, symbols: a crescent and a cross side by side in light. Gold, white, blue colors. Mood full of hip and mutual respect. Central composition — handshake in the middle, symbols above.*
