A former dark-skinned slave — who in Mecca had been regarded as no more than property — now stood at the highest point in Medina, calling out in a voice heard throughout the city, summoning Muslims to stand as equals before Allah in prayer.^5

Bilal's voice was described as melodious and powerful. He called the adhan every day throughout the Prophet's lifetime, and his standing in the Prophet's eyes was immensely honored.

## Quotations and Evidence

Bilal's story is a living embodiment of the Qur'anic verse:

> *"O mankind, indeed We have created you from male and female and made you peoples and tribes that you may know one another. Indeed, the most noble of you in the sight of Allah is the most righteous of you."*

> يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَاكُمْ مِنْ ذَكَرٍ وَأُنْثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَاكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَائِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوا ۚ إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ أَتْقَاكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌ ﴿13﴾
>
> — QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 13

The Prophet SAW also declared in his Farewell Sermon:

> *"There is no superiority of an Arab over a non-Arab, nor of a non-Arab over an Arab; no superiority of a red-skinned person over a dark-skinned person, nor of a dark-skinned person over a red-skinned one — except through piety."*
> — Musnad Ahmad, no. 23489

Regarding Bilal's status in the Hereafter, the Prophet SAW said:

> *"I heard the sound of your sandals ahead of me in Paradise."*
> — Sahih al-Bukhari, no. 1149, Kitab al-Tahajjud

## Analysis and Lessons

### A Revolution of Equality

Bilal's story demonstrates that Islam — from its very first days — was a movement of radical equality. Fourteen centuries before modern civil rights movements, Islam declared that skin color, social status, and wealth do not determine a person's worth before God.^2

### The Power of One Word

"Ahad" — the single word Bilal uttered under torture — carries a power that transcends all theological argument. It shows that true faith is not merely intellectual knowledge, but a conviction of the heart so firm that no physical torment can break it.^2

### Abu Bakr: A Model of Humanitarian Activism

Abu Bakr used his wealth to free human beings. His actions were not mere charity — they were concrete social activism: using privilege to liberate the oppressed. This model remains relevant today.^3

### A Voice That Changed the World

The selection of Bilal as the first muezzin was a decision laden with symbolic significance. The voice that called Muslims to worship Allah — the most important voice in the community's spiritual life — was given to a former slave. This proved that in Islam, spiritual authority is not determined by lineage but by the quality of one's faith and sacrifice.^1

## Bibliography

1. Al-Bukhari, Muhammad ibn Isma'il, *Sahih al-Bukhari* (Damascus: Dar Ibn Kathir, 2002).
2. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, vol. 1, ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa et al. (Cairo: Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
3. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*, vol. 3 (Beirut: Dar Sadir, 1968).
4. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, *Al-Isabah fi Tamyiz al-Sahabah*, vol. 1 (Beirut: Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah, 1995).
5. Ahmad ibn Hanbal, *Musnad Ahmad*, vol. 5 (Cairo: Mu'assasat Qurtubah, n.d.).
6. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 13.
bilal-bin-rabbah-muadzin-pertama-simbol-.general-id:
# Bilal bin Rabbah — Muadzin Pertama & Simbol Kesetaraan Islam (614 M)

## Konteks

Kisah **Bilal bin Rabbah** merupakan salah satu narasi paling kuat tentang keberanian iman dan revolusi sosial yang dibawa oleh Islam. Bilal adalah seorang budak berkulit hitam keturunan Habasyah (Ethiopia) yang dimiliki oleh **Umayyah bin Khalaf**, salah satu bangsawan Quraisy paling keras menentang Islam.^1

Dalam struktur sosial Makkah pra-Islam, budak berada di strata paling bawah — mereka bukan manusia dalam arti penuh, melainkan properti yang bisa dijual, dipukul, atau dibunuh tanpa konsekuensi hukum. Islam datang dengan pesan yang secara radikal menolak hierarki ini. Ayat-ayat awal Al-Qur'an menegaskan bahwa kemuliaan manusia ditentukan oleh ketakwaan, bukan warna kulit atau status sosial.^2 ^6

Ketika Bilal memeluk Islam — kemungkinan besar pada tahun kedua atau ketiga kenabian (sekitar 612–613 M) — ia tidak sekadar mengucapkan syahadat. Ia secara sadar menolak seluruh sistem yang menjadikannya budak, dan memilih tunduk hanya kepada Allah. Keputusan ini memicu salah satu episode penyiksaan paling memilukan dalam sejarah Islam awal.^3

## Peristiwa

### Keislaman Bilal

Bilal termasuk di antara *al-Sabiqun al-Awwalun* — generasi pertama yang memeluk Islam. Ibn Sa'd mencatat bahwa ia masuk Islam melalui dakwah Abu Bakr al-Shiddiq, yang secara aktif mengajak orang-orang dari berbagai lapisan sosial selama fase dakwah sembunyi-sembunyi.^3

Bagi seorang budak, memeluk Islam memiliki risiko yang jauh lebih besar daripada bagi seorang bangsawan. Budak tidak memiliki perlindungan kesukuan, tidak memiliki harta untuk membeli keselamatan, dan sepenuhnya berada di bawah kuasa tuannya. Bilal mengetahui semua ini — dan tetap beriman.^2

### Penyiksaan oleh Umayyah bin Khalaf

Ketika Umayyah bin Khalaf mengetahui keislaman Bilal, ia murka. Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa Umayyah menyiksa Bilal dengan cara yang sangat kejam: setiap tengah hari — saat matahari Makkah berada di puncak panasnya — Umayyah memerintahkan agar Bilal dibaringkan telentang di atas pasir gurun yang membara, lalu sebuah batu besar diletakkan di atas dadanya.^2

Dalam kondisi ini, Umayyah berkata kepada Bilal: "Kamu akan tetap seperti ini sampai kamu mati, atau sampai kamu mengingkari Muhammad dan menyembah Lata dan 'Uzza!"^2

Namun Bilal, dengan dada tertindih batu dan punggung terbakar pasir, hanya mengucapkan satu kata:

**"Ahad... Ahad..."** ("Yang Maha Esa... Yang Maha Esa...")^2

Kata ini — sederhana, tegas, penuh keimanan — menjadi salah satu ungkapan paling ikonik dalam sejarah Islam. Di tengah penyiksaan fisik yang luar biasa, Bilal menolak menyangkal keesaan Allah. Setiap teriakan "Ahad" adalah proklamasi tauhid yang menembus kekerasan dan penindasan.

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa Waraqah bin Naufal pernah melewati Bilal yang sedang disiksa, dan ia berkata: "Ahad, Ahad, demi Allah, wahai Bilal." Ia kemudian menoleh kepada Umayyah dan para penyiksa lainnya dan berkata: "Demi Allah, jika kalian membunuhnya dalam keadaan seperti ini, aku akan menjadikan kuburannya sebagai tempat ziarah."^2

### Pembebasan oleh Abu Bakr

**Abu Bakr al-Shiddiq** — yang hartanya tercatat paling banyak digunakan untuk membebaskan budak-budak muslim — mengetahui penderitaan Bilal. Ia mendatangi Umayyah bin Khalaf dan menawar untuk membeli Bilal.^1

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa Abu Bakr berkata kepada Umayyah: "Tidakkah engkau takut kepada Allah terhadap orang miskin ini?" Umayyah menjawab dengan sinis: "Kamu yang merusaknya, maka bebaskanlah dia." Abu Bakr pun membelinya dengan harga tinggi dan segera memerdekakan Bilal di jalan Allah.^2

Dalam Shahih al-Bukhari, Rasulullah SAW bersabda tentang Abu Bakr:

> إِنَّ أَمَنَّ النَّاسِ عَلَيَّ فِي صُحْبَتِهِ وَمَالِهِ أَبُو بَكْرٍ
>
> *"Sesungguhnya orang yang paling banyak berjasa kepadaku dalam persahabatan dan hartanya adalah Abu Bakr."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 466, Kitab al-Shalah

Abu Bakr tidak hanya membebaskan Bilal. Ibn Sa'd mencatat bahwa Abu Bakr membebaskan tujuh orang budak muslim lainnya, di antaranya **Amir bin Fuhairah** dan **Ummu Ubais**, semuanya dengan hartanya sendiri.^3

### Bilal sebagai Muadzin Pertama

Setelah hijrah ke Madinah dan pembangunan Masjid Nabawi, Nabi Muhammad SAW memilih Bilal sebagai **muadzin pertama** dalam Islam.^1 Pemilihan ini bukan sekadar penunjukan fungsional — ini adalah pernyataan revolusioner.

Seorang mantan budak berkulit hitam — yang di Makkah dianggap tidak lebih dari properti — kini berdiri di tempat paling tinggi di Madinah, mengumandangkan seruan yang didengar seluruh kota, memanggil umat Islam untuk berdiri setara di hadapan Allah dalam salat.^4 ^5

Suara Bilal digambarkan sebagai merdu dan kuat. Ia mengumandangkan adzan setiap hari selama hidup Nabi Muhammad SAW, dan kedudukannya di sisi Nabi sangat mulia.

## Dialog dan Dalil

Kisah Bilal menjadi perwujudan nyata ayat Al-Qur'an:

> يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقْنَـٰكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ شُعُوبًا وَقَبَآئِلَ لِتَعَارَفُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ أَكْرَمَكُمْ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ أَتْقَىٰكُمْ
>
> *"Wahai manusia, sesungguhnya Kami menciptakan kamu dari seorang laki-laki dan seorang perempuan dan menjadikan kamu berbangsa-bangsa dan bersuku-suku supaya kamu saling mengenal. Sesungguhnya orang yang paling mulia di antara kamu di sisi Allah ialah orang yang paling bertakwa."*
> — QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 13

Nabi Muhammad SAW juga bersabda dalam Khutbah Wada':

> لَا فَضْلَ لِعَرَبِيٍّ عَلَى أَعْجَمِيٍّ، وَلَا لِعَجَمِيٍّ عَلَى عَرَبِيٍّ، وَلَا لِأَحْمَرَ عَلَى أَسْوَدَ، وَلَا أَسْوَدَ عَلَى أَحْمَرَ إِلَّا بِالتَّقْوَى
>
> *"Tidak ada kelebihan orang Arab atas orang non-Arab, dan tidak ada kelebihan orang non-Arab atas orang Arab; tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit merah atas orang berkulit hitam, dan tidak ada kelebihan orang berkulit hitam atas orang berkulit merah — kecuali dengan ketakwaan."*
> — HR Ahmad, no. 23489

Tentang kedudukan Bilal di akhirat, Rasulullah SAW bersabda:

> سَمِعْتُ دَفَّ نَعْلَيْكَ بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ فِي الْجَنَّةِ
>
> *"Aku mendengar derap sandalmu di depanku di surga."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 1149, Kitab al-Tahajjud

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Revolusi Kesetaraan

Kisah Bilal mendemonstrasikan bahwa Islam — sejak hari-hari pertamanya — adalah gerakan kesetaraan radikal. Empat belas abad sebelum gerakan hak-hak sipil modern, Islam telah mendeklarasikan bahwa warna kulit, status sosial, dan kekayaan tidak menentukan nilai seorang manusia di hadapan Tuhan.^2

### Kekuatan Satu Kata

"Ahad" — satu kata yang diucapkan Bilal di tengah penyiksaan — mengandung kekuatan yang melampaui seluruh argumen teologis. Kata ini menunjukkan bahwa iman sejati bukan soal pengetahuan intelektual semata, melainkan keyakinan hati yang begitu kokoh hingga tidak bisa dipatahkan oleh siksaan fisik apa pun.^2

### Abu Bakr: Model Aktivisme Kemanusiaan

Abu Bakr menggunakan kekayaannya untuk membebaskan manusia. Tindakannya bukan sekadar amal — ini adalah aktivisme sosial yang konkret: menggunakan privilege untuk membebaskan mereka yang tertindas. Model ini tetap relevan hingga hari ini.^3

### Suara yang Mengubah Dunia

Pemilihan Bilal sebagai muadzin pertama adalah keputusan yang sarat makna simbolik. Suara yang memanggil umat Islam untuk menyembah Allah — suara paling penting dalam kehidupan spiritual komunitas — diberikan kepada mantan budak. Ini membuktikan bahwa dalam Islam, otoritas spiritual tidak ditentukan oleh garis keturunan, melainkan oleh kualitas iman dan pengorbanan.^1

## Daftar Pustaka

1. Al-Bukhari, Muhammad bin Ismail, *Shahih al-Bukhari* (Damaskus: Dar Ibn Kathir, 2002).
2. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, jilid 1, tahqiq Musthafa al-Saqqa dkk. (Kairo: Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
3. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Thabaqat al-Kubra*, jilid 3 (Beirut: Dar Shadir, 1968).
4. Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani, *Al-Ishabah fi Tamyiz al-Shahabah*, jilid 1 (Beirut: Dar al-Kutub al-Ilmiyyah, 1995).
5. Ahmad bin Hanbal, *Musnad Ahmad*, jilid 5 (Kairo: Mu'assasah Qurthubah, t.t.).

6. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-Hujurat (49): 13.

perang-tabuk-ekspedisi-terakhir-nabi-muh.children-en:
# The Battle of Tabuk ⚔️🏜️

---

## ⚔️ Why Did It Happen?

To the north of Arabia, there was a very powerful empire called the **Roman Empire** (Byzantium). Their emperor, **Heraclius**, heard that Islam was growing stronger. He grew worried and began gathering a huge army at the border.

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ received news about this threat. He made a brave decision: **don't wait for the enemy to come — move first!** The destination was **Tabuk** — a place far away at the northern border, about 700 kilometers from Madinah.

The problem? It was the **scorching hot summer** and the fruits in the orchards had just ripened. Leaving all that behind to march hundreds of kilometers across a blazing desert — that was an incredibly tough test!

> 🎨 *Illustration: A vast desert with a scorching sun. In the distance, the silhouette of an army marching toward the horizon. In the lower corner, a date palm with ripe fruit left behind. Colors: blazing yellow, orange, pale sky blue. Mood: hot and challenging. Composition: small army in the distance, desert dominating, date palm in the corner.*

---

## 🛡 What Happened?

This is one of the most inspiring moments in Islamic history!

**Abu Bakr** — the Prophet's closest companion — donated **all of his wealth** for this journey. The Prophet asked: *"What have you left for your family?"* Abu Bakr calmly replied: **"Allah and His Messenger."** SubhanAllah!

**Uthman ibn Affan** donated **300 camels** fully equipped and **1,000 gold coins**! The Prophet was so moved that he said: *"Nothing Uthman does after today will harm him."*

Even the **poorest companions** contributed. One could only bring **a handful of dates** — but that was everything he had! The hypocrites mocked them, but Allah defended these humble givers in the Quran.

The most touching part: **seven men** came asking for camels to ride to the battle, but the Prophet had no more animals. They went home **crying** because they couldn't join! — they cried not because they were afraid of war, but because **they couldn't participate**!

Finally, the Prophet set out with about **30,000 soldiers** — the longest and hardest journey he ever led.

> 🎨 *Illustration: Abu Bakr smiling as he hands over a large bag of his wealth. Next to him, Uthman leads a long line of camels. In the corner, a poor companion extends a handful of dates with trembling hands. Colors: gold, warm brown, olive green. Mood: noble and full of sacrifice. Composition: three scenes side by side — Abu Bakr, Uthman, and the poor companion.*

---

## 🕊 What Happened Next?

When the Muslim army arrived at Tabuk... **the enemy was already gone!** The Roman forces had retreated, intimidated by Islam's great strength. There was no battle at all!

Prophet Muhammad ﷺ stayed at Tabuk for twenty days. Several border tribal leaders came and made **peace agreements**. Mission accomplished without bloodshed!

But the most interesting story happened **after returning** to Madinah. There was a companion named **Ka'b ibn Malik** who didn't go to Tabuk — not because he was a hypocrite, but because he kept putting it off until it was too late.

When the Prophet asked why he didn't join, Ka'b had a choice: **lie or be honest**. The hypocrites chose to lie and make up false excuses. But Ka'b chose **honesty**: *"O Messenger of Allah, I have no excuse. I was wrong."*

As punishment, **no one was allowed to speak to Ka'b for 50 days**. how lonely that was! But after those difficult 50 days, Allah revealed a verse in the Quran accepting his repentance. Ka'b said it was the **happiest day** of his entire life!

> 🎨 *Illustration: Ka'b ibn Malik sitting alone in a corner of the mosque, people walking past him without greeting. His face is sad but hopeful. Soft light from a window shines on him. Colors: blue-gray, with a sliver of gold from the window light. Mood: lonely but hopeful. Composition: Ka'b alone on the left, people passing on the right, light from above.*

---

## 💪 What Can We Learn?

- **Give what you can** 💰 — Abu Bakr gave all his wealth; a poor companion gave a handful of dates. Both were equally noble in Allah's eyes! What matters is the intention, not the amount.

- **Crying because you can't do good** 😭 — The seven companions who wept because they couldn't join the expedition teach us: true believers actually WANT to do good, not avoid it.

- **Honesty is better than lying** 🗣️ — Ka'b ibn Malik chose honesty even knowing he would be punished. The liars were safe for a while, but Ka'b, who was honest, received forgiveness directly from Allah! **Honesty always wins in the end.**

- **Tests aren't always scary things** 🌾 — The test at Tabuk wasn't a monster or a terrifying enemy. The test was ripe dates, lush orchards, and hot weather. Sometimes the hardest test is leaving behind comfort!

> 🎨 *Illustration: A child standing at a fork in the road. One path leads to a fun playground, the other to a school uphill. The child smiles and chooses the uphill path. Colors: bright green, blue, sunshine yellow. Mood: determined and spirited. Composition: child in the center at the fork, two paths left and right.*
perang-tabuk-ekspedisi-terakhir-nabi-muh.children-id:
# Perang Tabuk ⚔️🏜️

---

## ⚔️ Kenapa?

Di utara Jazirah Arab, ada kerajaan yang sangat besar dan kuat bernama **Romawi** (Bizantium). Raja mereka, **Heraklius**, mendengar bahwa Islam semakin kuat. Dia khawatir dan mulai mengumpulkan pasukan besar di perbatasan.

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ mendapat kabar tentang ancaman ini. Beliau membuat keputusan berani: **jangan tunggu musuh datang, tapi bergerak duluan!** Tujuannya ke **Tabuk** — sebuah tempat yang sangat jauh di perbatasan utara, sekitar 700 kilometer dari Madinah.

Masalahnya? Saat itu sedang **musim panas yang sangat terik** dan buah-buahan di kebun baru saja matang. Meninggalkan semua itu untuk berjalan ratusan kilometer di padang pasir yang membara — itu ujian yang sangat berat!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Padang pasir luas dengan matahari yang menyengat. Di kejauhan, siluet pasukan berjalan menuju cakrawala. Di sudut bawah, pohon kurma dengan buah matang yang ditinggalkan. Warna: kuning terik, oranye, biru langit pucat. Mood: panas dan menantang. Komposisi: pasukan kecil di kejauhan, padang pasir mendominasi, pohon kurma di sudut.*

---

## 🛡 Ceritanya

Inilah salah satu momen paling menginspirasi dalam sejarah Islam!

**Abu Bakr** — sahabat terdekat Nabi — menyumbangkan **seluruh hartanya** untuk perjalanan ini. Nabi bertanya: *"Apa yang kamu sisakan untuk keluargamu?"* Abu Bakr menjawab dengan tenang: **"Allah dan Rasul-Nya."** Subhanallah!

**Utsman bin Affan** menyumbangkan **300 unta** lengkap dengan perlengkapannya dan **1.000 keping emas**! Nabi begitu terkesan sampai bersabda: *"Tidak akan merugikan Utsman apa pun yang ia lakukan setelah hari ini."*

Bahkan sahabat yang **sangat miskin** pun ikut menyumbang. Ada yang cuma bisa membawa **segenggam kurma** — tapi itulah semua yang dia punya! Orang-orang munafik mengejek mereka, tapi Allah membela orang-orang kecil itu di dalam al-Quran.

Yang paling mengharukan: ada **tujuh orang** yang datang minta tunggangan unta untuk ikut berperang, tapi Nabi tidak punya unta lagi. Mereka pulang sambil **menangis** karena tidak bisa ikut berjihad! — mereka menangis bukan karena takut perang, tapi karena **tidak bisa ikut**!

Akhirnya, Nabi berangkat dengan sekitar **30.000 pasukan** — perjalanan terpanjang dan terberat yang pernah beliau pimpin.

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Abu Bakr tersenyum menyerahkan sekantong besar berisi hartanya. Di sampingnya, Utsman menuntun barisan unta panjang. Di pojok, seorang sahabat miskin mengulurkan segenggam kurma dengan tangan gemetar. Warna: emas, coklat hangat, hijau zaitun. Mood: mulia dan penuh pengorbanan. Komposisi: tiga adegan berjajar — Abu Bakr, Utsman, dan sahabat miskin.*

---

## 🕊 Setelahnya

Ketika pasukan muslimin tiba di Tabuk... **musuh sudah pergi!** Pasukan Romawi mundur karena takut melihat kekuatan besar Islam. Tidak ada pertempuran sama sekali!

Nabi Muhammad ﷺ tinggal di Tabuk selama dua puluh hari. Beberapa pemimpin suku di perbatasan datang dan membuat **perjanjian damai**. Misi berhasil tanpa tumpah darah!

Tapi kisah paling menarik terjadi **setelah pulang** ke Madinah. Ada seorang sahabat bernama **Ka'b bin Malik** yang tidak ikut ke Tabuk — bukan karena munafik, tapi karena menunda-nunda sampai terlambat.

Ketika Nabi bertanya kenapa dia tidak ikut, Ka'b punya pilihan: **berbohong atau jujur**. Orang-orang munafik memilih bohong dan mencari-cari alasan palsu. Tapi Ka'b memilih **jujur**: *"Ya Rasulullah, aku tidak punya alasan. Aku salah."*

Sebagai hukuman, **tidak ada yang boleh bicara dengan Ka'b selama 50 hari**. betapa kesepiannya! Tapi setelah 50 hari yang berat itu, Allah menurunkan ayat al-Quran yang menerima taubatnya. Ka'b berkata itu adalah **hari paling bahagia** dalam hidupnya!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Ka'b bin Malik duduk sendirian di sudut masjid, orang-orang berjalan melewatinya tanpa menyapa. Wajahnya sedih tapi penuh harap. Cahaya lembut dari jendela menyinarinya. Warna: biru kelabu, dengan secercah emas dari cahaya jendela. Mood: kesepian tapi penuh harapan. Komposisi: Ka'b sendirian di kiri, orang-orang berlalu di kanan, cahaya dari atas.*

---

## 💪 Teladannya

- **Berkorban sesuai kemampuan** 💰 — Abu Bakr memberi semua hartanya, sahabat miskin memberi segenggam kurma. Keduanya sama mulianya di mata Allah! Yang penting niatnya, bukan jumlahnya.

- **Menangis karena tidak bisa berbuat baik** 😭 — Tujuh sahabat yang menangis karena tidak bisa ikut berjihad mengajarkan kita: orang beriman sejati justru INGIN melakukan kebaikan, bukan menghindarinya.

- **Jujur itu lebih baik dari berbohong** 🗣️ — Ka'b bin Malik memilih jujur meskipun tahu akan dihukum. Orang-orang yang bohong memang selamat sementara, tapi Ka'b yang jujur mendapat pengampunan langsung dari Allah! **Kejujuran selalu menang pada akhirnya.**

- **Ujian tidak selalu berupa hal yang menakutkan** 🌾 — Ujian di Tabuk bukan monster atau musuh yang mengerikan. Ujiannya adalah buah kurma yang matang, kebun yang subur, dan cuaca yang panas. Kadang ujian terberat adalah meninggalkan kenyamanan!

> 🎨 *Ilustrasi: Seorang anak berdiri di persimpangan jalan. Satu jalan menuju taman bermain yang menyenangkan, jalan lain menuju sekolah yang menanjak. Anak itu tersenyum dan memilih jalan yang menanjak. Warna: hijau cerah, biru, kuning matahari. Mood: penuh tekad dan semangat. Komposisi: anak di tengah persimpangan, dua jalan di kiri dan kanan.*
perang-tabuk-ekspedisi-terakhir-nabi-muh.general-en:
# The Battle of Tabuk: The Prophet's Final Expedition

## Context: The Byzantine Threat from the North

In the ninth year of Hijrah (630 CE), the Arabian Peninsula had almost entirely come under Islamic authority. But on the northern frontier, the shadow of the Eastern Roman Empire (Byzantium) loomed as a real threat. Reports reached the Prophet ﷺ that the Roman emperor **Heraclius** was amassing a large army at the Syrian border, supported by Christian Arab tribes such as Lakhm, Judham, Amil, and Ghassan, with the intent of attacking the Arabian Peninsula.^1 ^2

The geopolitical context was clear: after Fath Makkah and the victory at Hunayn, Islam had become the dominant force in Arabia. For Byzantium, this posed a direct threat to their sphere of influence in al-Sham (Syria, Palestine, Jordan). The Battle of Mu'tah two years earlier (629 CE) — in which three Muslim commanders fell, including Zayd ibn Harithah and Ja'far ibn Abi Talib — had demonstrated that a clash between Islam and Rome was only a matter of time.^1

The Prophet ﷺ made an unusual decision: rather than wait for the enemy to come, he chose to march north first. This was the last *ghazwah* (military expedition led personally by the Prophet) in prophetic history.

## The Event: Mobilization Under Extreme Hardship

The Tabuk expedition was dubbed *Jaysh al-'Usrah* (the Army of Hardship) because of the extreme conditions: scorching summer heat, an enormous distance (approximately 700 km from Madinah to Tabuk), and the harvest season that had just arrived.^1 ^2 This was an extraordinary test of faith — leaving freshly ripened fruits and livelihoods to march through a burning desert.

The Quran recorded this trial^5:

> انفِرُوا خِفَافًا وَثِقَالًا وَجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ﴿٤١﴾
>
> *"March forth whether light or heavy, and strive with your wealth and your lives in the cause of Allah. ﴾41﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 41

In these difficult conditions, the nobility of the companions shone brilliantly:

**Abu Bakr al-Siddiq** donated **all of his wealth**. When the Prophet ﷺ asked: *"What have you left for your family?"* Abu Bakr replied: *"I have left them Allah and His Messenger."*^3

**Uthman ibn Affan** financed a third of the army with an enormous contribution — 300 camels fully equipped with saddles and gear, 1,000 gold dinars, and other military supplies. The Prophet ﷺ said:^3

> مَا ضَرَّ عُثْمَانَ مَا عَمِلَ بَعْدَ الْيَوْمِ
>
> *"Nothing Uthman does after today will harm him. ﴾79﴿"*
> — Tirmidhi, Kitab al-Manaqib

Even the poorest companions competed to contribute. One came with only a handful of dates — all he possessed. The hypocrites mocked them, but Allah defended these humble givers:

> الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ الْمُطَّوِّعِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الصَّدَقَاتِ وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ ۙ سَخِرَ اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴿٧٩﴾
>
> *"Those who criticize the believers who give charity voluntarily and ridicule those who find nothing to give except their own effort — Allah will ridicule them. ﴾79﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 79

There were also seven companions who came asking for mounts to join the expedition, but the Prophet ﷺ had no animals for them. They went home **weeping** because they could not participate. The Quran immortalized them:

> وَلَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ إِذَا مَا أَتَوْكَ لِتَحْمِلَهُمْ قُلْتَ لَا أَجِدُ مَا أَحْمِلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَوَلَّوا وَّأَعْيُنُهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ حَزَنًا أَلَّا يَجِدُوا مَا يُنفِقُونَ ﴿٩٢﴾
>
> *"Nor upon those who, when they came to you that you might provide them with mounts, you said: 'I can find no mounts for you,' and they turned back while their eyes overflowed with tears out of grief that they could find nothing to spend. ﴾92﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 92

## Key Dialogues and Evidence: Hypocrites and the Test of Loyalty

The Tabuk expedition became the test that most clearly separated true believers from hypocrites. Surah At-Tawbah — revealed in connection with Tabuk — thoroughly exposed the masks of the hypocrites.

They searched for excuses not to join the expedition. Some requested exemption on false pretenses. Al-Jadd ibn Qays said: *"Permit me to stay and do not tempt me — I fear being seduced by Roman women."*^1 Allah responded:

> أَلَا فِي الْفِتْنَةِ سَقَطُوا ﴿٤٩﴾
>
> *"Unquestionably, into temptation they have already fallen. ﴾49﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 49

A group of hypocrites also constructed a mosque — known as **Masjid al-Dirar** — as a base to divide the Muslim community. Allah exposed their scheme and commanded the Prophet ﷺ to demolish it.^1

### The Story of Ka'b ibn Malik: Honesty That Saved

One of the most celebrated stories from Tabuk is that of **Ka'b ibn Malik** — one of three companions who honestly admitted they had stayed behind without a valid excuse. Ka'b was no hypocrite; he was a sincere believer who kept postponing his departure until the army had gone too far.

When the Prophet ﷺ returned to Madinah, he asked everyone who had stayed behind about their reasons. The hypocrites swore false oaths, and the Prophet ﷺ accepted their outward claims while leaving their inner truth to Allah. But Ka'b chose honesty:^3

*"O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, were I sitting before any ruler in the world other than you, I could talk my way out of his anger with excuses. I have been given the gift of argument. But by Allah, I know that if I lie to you today, Allah will surely make you angry with me. And if I tell the truth — even if you are angry — I hope Allah will grant me a good outcome. By Allah, I had no excuse. I was at my strongest and most prosperous when I stayed behind. "*

The Prophet ﷺ said: *"As for this man, he has spoken the truth."* But Ka'b was punished with a **fifty-day boycott** — no one was permitted to speak to him. The vast earth felt confining. After fifty agonizing days, Allah revealed the verse accepting their repentance:^3

> وَعَلَى الثَّلَاثَةِ الَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوا أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوا ﴿١١٨﴾
>
> *"And upon the three who were left behind, until when the earth closed in on them in spite of its vastness, and their own souls confined them, and they were certain that there is no refuge from Allah except in Him — then He turned to them so they could repent. ﴾118﴿"*
> — QS. At-Tawbah (9): 118

## Analysis and Lessons

### An Expedition Without Battle

What makes Tabuk unique is that no battle took place. When the Muslim force of approximately 30,000 arrived at Tabuk, the Roman army and their Arab allies had already withdrawn.^1 ^2 The strategic objective was achieved without a sword drawn — the presence of the Muslim army at the northern border sent an unmistakable message to Byzantium: Islam was not a regional force to be dismissed, but a power capable of projecting military might to the borders of the greatest empire on earth.

The Prophet ﷺ remained at Tabuk for approximately twenty days, during which several border chieftains — including **Yuhanna ibn Ru'bah**, the ruler of Aylah (modern Aqaba) — came to pay *jizyah* and conclude peace agreements.^1 This marked the beginning of Islam's expansion into al-Sham, which would be fully realized during the caliphates of Abu Bakr and Umar.

### Tabuk as a Test of Faith

Tabuk was primarily not about warfare — it was a test of faith. In the summer heat, over an immense distance, with ripe fruits beckoning, Allah tested who was truly a believer and who believed only in word.

The result: three categories of people were revealed at Tabuk:
1. **The sincere** — those who marched despite the extreme difficulty, like Abu Bakr, Uthman, and the poor companions who wept because they could not join
2. **Those honest about their weakness** — like Ka'b ibn Malik, who confessed his fault and accepted the consequences
3. **The hypocrites** — who manufactured excuses, swore false oaths, and built Masjid al-Dirar to divide the community

### Honesty as Salvation

The story of Ka'b ibn Malik is an eternal lesson in the power of truth. Ka'b could have lied — he admitted himself that he possessed eloquence and persuasive skill. But he chose honesty despite the enormous risk. And it was that honesty that saved him. While the hypocrites who swore falsely were spared outwardly, they were ruined in the sight of Allah.

### A Message for History

Tabuk marked the end of the era of the Prophet's military expeditions. After Tabuk, there were no more *ghazwat*. The two great events that followed — the Year of Delegations (*'Am al-Wufud*) and the Farewell Pilgrimage — were diplomatic and spiritual, not military. Tabuk was the point at which Islam had grown strong enough to no longer need to fight for survival — it had become a governing force, no longer merely a surviving one.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Edited by Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Safi al-Rahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (The Sealed Nectar). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih al-Bukhari). Hadith on Abu Bakr's and Uthman's donations (Kitab al-Manaqib), Story of Ka'b ibn Malik (Kitab al-Maghazi, no. 4418).

4. **Muslim, Abu al-Husayn Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih Muslim). Story of Ka'b ibn Malik (Kitab al-Tawbah).
5. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. At-Tawbah (9): 41, 79, 92, 49, 118.
perang-tabuk-ekspedisi-terakhir-nabi-muh.general-id:
# Perang Tabuk: Ekspedisi Terakhir Nabi Muhammad ﷺ

## Konteks: Ancaman Romawi di Utara

Pada tahun kesembilan Hijriah (630 M), Jazirah Arab telah hampir seluruhnya tunduk di bawah kekuasaan Islam. Namun di perbatasan utara, bayang-bayang Kekaisaran Romawi Timur (Bizantium) menjulang sebagai ancaman nyata. Informasi sampai kepada Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa kaisar Romawi **Heraklius** sedang menghimpun pasukan besar di perbatasan Syam, didukung oleh suku-suku Arab Kristen seperti Lakhm, Juzam, Amil, dan Ghassan, untuk menyerang Jazirah Arab.^1 ^2

Konteks geopolitisnya jelas: setelah Fath Makkah dan kemenangan di Hunain, Islam telah menjadi kekuatan dominan di Jazirah Arab. Bagi Bizantium, ini adalah ancaman langsung terhadap zona pengaruh mereka di Syam (Suriah, Palestina, Yordania). Pertempuran Mu'tah dua tahun sebelumnya (629 M) — di mana tiga panglima muslim gugur, termasuk Zaid bin Haritsah dan Ja'far bin Abi Thalib — telah menunjukkan bahwa konflik antara Islam dan Romawi tinggal masalah waktu.^1

Rasulullah ﷺ mengambil keputusan yang tidak lazim: alih-alih menunggu musuh datang, beliau memilih untuk bergerak terlebih dahulu ke utara. Ini adalah *ghazwah* (ekspedisi militer yang dipimpin langsung oleh Nabi) terakhir dalam sejarah kenabian.

## Peristiwa: Mobilisasi dalam Ujian Berat

Ekspedisi Tabuk dijuluki *Jaysyul 'Usrah* (Pasukan Kesulitan) karena kondisi yang sangat berat: musim panas yang terik, jarak tempuh yang sangat jauh (sekitar 700 km dari Madinah ke Tabuk), dan masa panen yang baru tiba.^1 ^2 Ini adalah ujian keimanan yang luar biasa — meninggalkan buah-buahan yang baru matang dan mata pencaharian untuk berperang di padang pasir yang membara.

Al-Quran merekam ujian ini^5:

> انفِرُوا خِفَافًا وَثِقَالًا وَجَاهِدُوا بِأَمْوَالِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ﴿٤١﴾
>
> *"Berangkatlah kalian baik dalam keadaan ringan maupun berat, dan berjihadlah dengan harta dan diri kalian di jalan Allah. ﴾41﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 41

Dalam kondisi sulit ini, kemuliaan para sahabat bersinar terang:

**Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** menyumbangkan **seluruh hartanya**. Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ bertanya: *"Apa yang kamu sisakan untuk keluargamu?"* Abu Bakr menjawab: *"Aku sisakan Allah dan Rasul-Nya."*^3

**Utsman ibn Affan** membiayai sepertiga pasukan dengan sumbangan yang sangat besar — 300 unta lengkap dengan pelana dan perlengkapannya, 1.000 dinar emas, dan perlengkapan perang lainnya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda:^3

> مَا ضَرَّ عُثْمَانَ مَا عَمِلَ بَعْدَ الْيَوْمِ
>
> *"Tidak akan merugikan Utsman apa pun yang ia lakukan setelah hari ini. ﴾79﴿"*
> — HR Tirmidzi, Kitab al-Manaqib

Para sahabat yang miskin pun berlomba-lomba menyumbang. Ada yang datang hanya membawa segenggam kurma — itulah semua yang ia miliki. Kaum munafik mengejek mereka, namun Allah membela para penyumbang kecil ini:

> الَّذِينَ يَلْمِزُونَ الْمُطَّوِّعِينَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الصَّدَقَاتِ وَالَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ فَيَسْخَرُونَ مِنْهُمْ ۙ سَخِرَ اللَّهُ مِنْهُمْ ﴿٧٩﴾
>
> *"Orang-orang yang mencela kaum mukmin yang bersedekah dengan sukarela dan mencela orang-orang yang tidak memperoleh (sesuatu untuk disedekahkan) selain sekedar kesanggupannya, lalu mereka mengejeknya — Allah akan membalas ejekan mereka. ﴾79﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 79

Di sisi lain, ada tujuh orang sahabat yang datang meminta tunggangan untuk ikut berperang, namun Rasulullah ﷺ tidak memiliki hewan tunggangan untuk mereka. Mereka pulang dengan **menangis** karena tidak mampu ikut berjihad. Al-Quran mengabadikan mereka:

> وَلَا عَلَى الَّذِينَ إِذَا مَا أَتَوْكَ لِتَحْمِلَهُمْ قُلْتَ لَا أَجِدُ مَا أَحْمِلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ تَوَلَّوا وَّأَعْيُنُهُمْ تَفِيضُ مِنَ الدَّمْعِ حَزَنًا أَلَّا يَجِدُوا مَا يُنفِقُونَ ﴿٩٢﴾
>
> *"Dan tidak pula atas orang-orang yang apabila mereka datang kepadamu agar engkau memberikan kendaraan kepada mereka, lalu engkau berkata: 'Aku tidak memperoleh kendaraan untuk kalian,' mereka kembali sedang mata mereka bercucuran air mata karena sedih, karena tidak memiliki sesuatu untuk diinfakkan. ﴾92﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 92

## Dialog dan Dalil: Kaum Munafik dan Ujian Kesetiaan

Ekspedisi Tabuk menjadi ujian yang memisahkan orang beriman sejati dari kaum munafik secara paling gamblang. Surah At-Taubah — yang turun berkaitan dengan peristiwa Tabuk — membongkar habis kedok kaum munafik.

Mereka mencari-cari alasan untuk tidak ikut berperang. Sebagian meminta izin dengan alasan palsu. Al-Jadd bin Qais berkata: *"Izinkan aku dan jangan engkau fitnah aku — aku takut tergoda oleh perempuan-perempuan Romawi."*^1 Allah menjawabnya:

> أَلَا فِي الْفِتْنَةِ سَقَطُوا ﴿٤٩﴾
>
> *"Ketahuilah, sesungguhnya mereka telah jatuh ke dalam fitnah (keburukan). ﴾49﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 49

Ada pula sekelompok munafik yang membangun masjid — disebut **Masjid al-Dhirar** — sebagai markas untuk memecah belah umat. Allah membongkar rencana mereka dan memerintahkan Rasulullah ﷺ untuk menghancurkan masjid tersebut.^1

### Kisah Ka'b bin Malik: Kejujuran yang Menyelamatkan

Salah satu kisah paling terkenal dari Tabuk adalah kisah **Ka'b bin Malik** — salah satu dari tiga sahabat yang jujur mengakui bahwa mereka tidak ikut berperang tanpa alasan yang sah. Ka'b bukanlah munafik; ia adalah sahabat yang beriman, tapi ia menunda-nunda keberangkatan hingga pasukan sudah terlalu jauh.

Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ kembali ke Madinah, beliau bertanya kepada setiap orang yang tidak ikut tentang alasan mereka. Kaum munafik bersumpah dengan alasan-alasan palsu, dan Rasulullah ﷺ menerima lahiriah mereka sambil menyerahkan batin mereka kepada Allah. Namun Ka'b memilih jujur:^3

*"Wahai Rasulullah, demi Allah, seandainya aku duduk di hadapan seorang penguasa dunia selain engkau, niscaya aku bisa keluar dari kemarahannya dengan dalih. Aku dianugerahi kemampuan berargumen. Tapi demi Allah, aku tahu bahwa jika aku berdusta kepadamu hari ini, Allah pasti akan membuatmu murka kepadaku. Dan jika aku berkata jujur — walaupun engkau marah — aku berharap Allah akan memberikan jalan keluar yang baik. Demi Allah, aku tidak punya uzur. Aku dalam keadaan paling kuat dan paling berkecukupan saat aku tertinggal darimu."*

Rasulullah ﷺ berkata: *"Adapun orang ini, dia telah berkata jujur. "* Namun Ka'b dihukum dengan **pemboikotan selama lima puluh hari** — tidak ada seorang pun yang boleh berbicara dengannya. Bumi yang luas terasa sempit baginya.^4 Setelah lima puluh hari yang menyiksa, Allah menurunkan ayat menerima taubat mereka:^3

> وَعَلَى الثَّلَاثَةِ الَّذِينَ خُلِّفُوا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا ضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ الْأَرْضُ بِمَا رَحُبَتْ وَضَاقَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ وَظَنُّوا أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ مِنَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا إِلَيْهِ ثُمَّ تَابَ عَلَيْهِمْ لِيَتُوبُوا ﴿١١٨﴾
>
> *"Dan terhadap tiga orang yang ditangguhkan (penerimaan taubat mereka), hingga apabila bumi terasa sempit bagi mereka meskipun luas, dan jiwa mereka pun terasa sempit, dan mereka yakin bahwa tidak ada tempat lari dari (siksa) Allah kecuali kembali kepada-Nya, kemudian Allah menerima taubat mereka agar mereka tetap bertaubat. ﴾118﴿"*
> — QS. At-Taubah (9): 118

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Ekspedisi Tanpa Pertempuran

Yang unik dari Tabuk adalah: tidak terjadi pertempuran. Ketika pasukan muslim yang berjumlah sekitar 30.000 orang tiba di Tabuk, pasukan Romawi dan sekutu-sekutu Arab mereka telah mundur.^1 ^2 Tujuan strategis tercapai tanpa pedang terhunus — kehadiran pasukan muslim di perbatasan utara mengirimkan pesan yang jelas kepada Bizantium: Islam bukan kekuatan regional yang bisa diabaikan, melainkan kekuatan yang mampu memproyeksikan kekuatan militernya hingga ke perbatasan kekaisaran terbesar dunia saat itu.

Rasulullah ﷺ tinggal di Tabuk selama sekitar dua puluh hari, selama itu beberapa pemimpin suku di perbatasan — termasuk **Yuhanna bin Ru'bah**, penguasa Ailah (Aqaba modern) — datang menyerahkan jizyah dan membuat perjanjian damai.^1 Ini menandai awal perluasan pengaruh Islam ke wilayah Syam yang akan terealisasi sepenuhnya pada masa kekhalifahan Abu Bakr dan Umar.

### Tabuk sebagai Ujian Keimanan

Tabuk bukan terutama tentang perang — ia adalah ujian keimanan. Dalam panasnya musim panas, jauhnya perjalanan, dan manisnya buah-buahan yang baru matang, Allah menguji siapa yang benar-benar beriman dan siapa yang hanya beriman di mulut.

Hasilnya: tiga kategori manusia terungkap di Tabuk:
1. **Orang-orang yang tulus** — mereka yang berangkat meskipun sangat berat, seperti Abu Bakr, Utsman, dan para sahabat miskin yang menangis karena tidak bisa ikut
2. **Orang-orang yang jujur tentang kelemahan mereka** — seperti Ka'b bin Malik, yang mengaku salah dan menerima konsekuensinya
3. **Kaum munafik** — yang mencari-cari alasan, bersumpah palsu, dan membangun Masjid al-Dhirar untuk memecah belah umat

### Kejujuran sebagai Penyelamat

Kisah Ka'b bin Malik adalah pelajaran abadi tentang kekuatan kejujuran. Ka'b bisa saja berbohong — ia mengakui sendiri bahwa ia punya kemampuan berargumen yang baik. Tapi ia memilih jujur meskipun risikonya besar. Dan kejujuran itulah yang menyelamatkannya. Sementara kaum munafik yang bersumpah palsu selamat secara lahiriah, mereka justru hancur di sisi Allah.

### Pesan untuk Sejarah

Tabuk menandai akhir dari era ekspedisi militer Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Setelah Tabuk, tidak ada lagi *ghazwah*. Dua peristiwa besar berikutnya — Tahun Delegasi (*'Am al-Wufud*) dan Haji Wada — bersifat diplomatik dan spiritual, bukan militer. Tabuk adalah titik di mana Islam telah cukup kuat untuk tidak perlu lagi berperang demi bertahan hidup — ia telah menjadi kekuatan yang mengatur, bukan yang bertahan.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

2. **Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman.** *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Sirah Nabawiyah). Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976.

3. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Bukhari). Hadits tentang sumbangan Abu Bakr dan Utsman (Kitab al-Manaqib), Kisah Ka'b bin Malik (Kitab al-Maghazi, no. 4418).

4. **Muslim, Abu al-Husain Muslim bin al-Hajjaj.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Muslim). Kisah Ka'b bin Malik (Kitab al-Taubah).

5. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. At-Taubah (9): 41, 79, 92, 49, 118.

am-al-huzn-wafat-khadijah-abu-thalib.children-en:
# The Saddest Year

## 💔 The Uncle Who Always Protected

You have an uncle who loves you very much. Since you were little, he has always watched over you. If anyone wanted to harm you, that uncle would stand in front of you and say: "If you want to hurt him, you'll have to get through me first!"

That is exactly how **Abu Talib** was toward **Prophet Muhammad SAW**. Ever since the Prophet lost his father and mother as a child, it was Abu Talib who raised him. When the Prophet grew up and began inviting people to Islam, Abu Talib continued to protect him even though the Quraysh leaders were furious.

But one day, Abu Talib fell gravely ill. His body had become very weak after three years of living in hunger at the valley of Shi'b Abi Talib. Prophet Muhammad SAW sat beside him, held his hand, and gently pleaded with his uncle to say the testimony of faith. But Abu Talib did not say it, and he passed away.

Prophet Muhammad SAW was deeply saddened. He had lost the uncle who had always been his shield and the shield of the Islamic mission.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A simple room in Mecca. Prophet Muhammad SAW sits beside Abu Talib's sickbed, holding his uncle's hand with both hands. The Prophet's face is full of love and sadness. In the corner of the room, afternoon sunlight enters through a small window. Warm brownish tones, soft golden light, gentle shadows. Mood full of tenderness and love. Intimate composition — two figures in the center, window light as an accent.*

---

## 🌹 The Extraordinary First Wife

Not long after Abu Talib passed away, Prophet Muhammad SAW received another sorrow — one that hurt his heart even more deeply. **Khadijah bint Khuwaylid**, his beloved wife, also passed away.

Khadijah was a truly extraordinary woman. She was the very first person in the entire world to believe that Muhammad was Allah's messenger. When the Prophet returned home from the Cave of Hira, trembling with fear after receiving the first revelation, Khadijah embraced him and said: "Be calm, O my husband. Allah will never humiliate you."

Throughout their twenty-five years of marriage, Khadijah was always at the Prophet's side. She gave her wealth to help the Islamic mission. She was the person the Prophet confided in during times of sadness and joy. She was Prophet Muhammad SAW's best companion.

Allah Himself even sent greetings to Khadijah through the Angel Jibril! how special she was in the sight of Allah.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A small garden with date palm trees in Mecca. Khadijah stands gracefully, smiling gently, wearing simple yet neat clothing. In her hand is a bowl of food to be given to those in need. Warm morning light envelops the scene. Colors of green foliage, cream-white clothing, golden morning light. Mood full of gentleness and nobility. Portrait composition — Khadijah in the center with date palms behind her.*

---

## 😢 The Year of Sorrow

Because the two people closest to him passed away within nearly the same time, that year came to be called *'Am al-Huzn* — which means **the Year of Sorrow**.

After Abu Talib passed away, there was no one left to protect the Prophet from the cruelty of the Quraysh. Someone even dared to throw dust on the Prophet's head while he was walking through the marketplace! Such a thing had never happened while Abu Talib was alive.

Prophet Muhammad SAW also tried traveling to the city of **Ta'if** to seek help. But instead of being welcomed, he was driven out and pelted with stones by the people of Ta'if until his feet bled. It was truly an incredibly difficult day.

But Prophet Muhammad SAW never gave up hope. He continued to pray to Allah. He remained patient. He was certain that Allah never abandons His faithful servants.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *Prophet Muhammad SAW sitting alone under a tree on the outskirts of Ta'if at dusk. His face is bowed down, bloodstained sandals beside him. But both his hands are raised in prayer. In the sky, beautiful purple-hued sunset colors. Colors of purple dusk, brown earth, red blood, golden last light. Mood is sorrowful yet hopeful. Wide composition — a small figure beneath a vast sky, showing solitude yet also grandeur.*

---

## 🌟 Allah's Beautiful Promise

Even though that year was incredibly hard, Allah did not abandon His Prophet. Allah said to Prophet Muhammad SAW:

"Did He not find you an orphan and give you shelter? Did He not find you lost and guide you? Did He not find you in need and provide for you?"

These words of Allah were like a warm embrace on a cold night. Allah reminded the Prophet that He had always watched over him, and He would continue to watch over him.

Happened after this year of sorrow, ? Allah gave Prophet Muhammad SAW an absolutely incredible gift — the journey of Isra' Mi'raj! A miraculous journey from Mecca to Masjid al-Aqsa, then ascending through seven heavens to meet Allah directly. Subhanallah!

That is the important lesson from this story: after heavy sorrow, beautiful happiness is sure to come. Allah never forgets His patient servants.

> **🎨 Illustration Brief:**
> *A night sky scattered with brilliant stars above the city of Mecca. From below, Prophet Muhammad SAW looks up at the sky with a face full of hope, and from the sky a warm golden light descends, illuminating him. In the distance, the silhouette of the Ka'bah is visible. Colors of deep blue sky, divine gold light, white stars. Mood full of hope and wonder. Vertical composition — light from above descending downward, connecting heaven and earth.*
am-al-huzn-wafat-khadijah-abu-thalib.children-id:
# Tahun Paling Sedih

## 💔 Paman yang Selalu Melindungi

Kalian punya paman yang sangat sayang kepada kalian. Sejak kecil, ia selalu menjaga kalian. Kalau ada yang mau jahat, paman itu berdiri di depan dan berkata: "Kalau kalian mau menyakitinya, kalian harus lewati aku dulu!"

Begitulah **Abu Thalib** terhadap **Nabi Muhammad SAW**. Sejak Nabi kecil kehilangan ayah dan ibu, Abu Thalib-lah yang merawat beliau. Ketika Nabi dewasa dan mulai mengajak orang-orang kepada Islam, Abu Thalib tetap melindungi beliau meskipun orang-orang Quraisy marah besar.

Tapi suatu hari, Abu Thalib jatuh sakit berat. Tubuhnya sudah sangat lemah setelah tiga tahun hidup kelaparan di lembah Syi'b Abu Thalib. Nabi Muhammad SAW duduk di sampingnya, memegang tangannya, dan memohon dengan sangat lembut agar pamannya mengucapkan kalimat syahadat. Tapi Abu Thalib tidak mengucapkannya, dan beliau pun wafat.

Nabi Muhammad SAW sangat sedih. Beliau kehilangan paman yang selama ini menjadi perisai bagi beliau dan dakwah Islam.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Sebuah ruangan sederhana di Makkah. Nabi Muhammad SAW duduk di samping tempat tidur Abu Thalib yang terbaring sakit, memegang tangan pamannya dengan kedua tangan. Wajah Nabi penuh kasih sayang dan kesedihan. Di sudut ruangan, cahaya matahari sore masuk dari jendela kecil. Warna hangat kecoklatan, cahaya lembut keemasan, bayangan lembut. Mood penuh haru dan cinta. Komposisi intim — dua figur di tengah, cahaya jendela sebagai aksen.*

---

## 🌹 Istri Pertama yang Luar Biasa

Belum lama setelah Abu Thalib wafat, Nabi Muhammad SAW menerima kesedihan yang lain — yang bahkan lebih menyakitkan hatinya. **Khadijah binti Khuwaylid**, istri tercinta beliau, juga wafat.

Khadijah adalah wanita yang sangat istimewa. Dia adalah orang pertama di seluruh dunia yang percaya bahwa Muhammad adalah utusan Allah. Ketika Nabi pulang dari Gua Hira dalam keadaan gemetar ketakutan setelah menerima wahyu pertama, Khadijah memeluk beliau dan berkata: "Tenanglah, wahai suamiku. Allah tidak akan pernah menghinakanmu."

Selama dua puluh lima tahun pernikahan mereka, Khadijah selalu ada di samping Nabi. Dia memberikan hartanya untuk membantu dakwah Islam. Dia menjadi tempat Nabi bercerita saat sedih dan saat senang. Dia adalah sahabat terbaik Nabi Muhammad SAW.

Bahkan Allah sendiri mengirimkan salam untuk Khadijah melalui Malaikat Jibril! betapa istimewanya beliau di sisi Allah.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Taman kecil dengan pohon kurma di Makkah. Khadijah berdiri dengan anggun, tersenyum lembut, mengenakan pakaian sederhana namun rapi. Di tangannya ada sebuah wadah makanan yang akan diberikan kepada orang yang membutuhkan. Cahaya pagi yang hangat menyelimuti. Warna hijau dedaunan, putih krem pakaian, emas cahaya pagi. Mood penuh kelembutan dan kemuliaan. Komposisi potret — Khadijah di tengah dengan pohon kurma di belakang.*

---

## 😢 Tahun Kesedihan

Karena kedua orang yang paling dekat dengannya wafat dalam waktu yang hampir bersamaan, tahun itu disebut *'Am al-Huzn* — yang artinya **Tahun Kesedihan**.

Setelah Abu Thalib wafat, tidak ada lagi yang melindungi Nabi dari kejahatan Quraisy. Ada orang yang berani melempar debu ke kepala Nabi saat beliau berjalan di pasar! Hal seperti itu tidak pernah terjadi selama Abu Thalib masih hidup.

Nabi Muhammad SAW pun mencoba pergi ke kota **Tha'if** untuk mencari pertolongan. Tapi bukannya disambut baik, beliau malah diusir dan dilempari batu oleh penduduk Tha'if sampai kaki beliau berdarah. Sungguh hari yang sangat berat.

Tapi Nabi Muhammad SAW tidak pernah putus asa. Beliau tetap berdoa kepada Allah. Beliau tetap sabar. Beliau yakin bahwa Allah tidak pernah meninggalkan hamba-Nya yang beriman.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Nabi Muhammad SAW duduk sendirian di bawah pohon di pinggiran kota Tha'if menjelang maghrib. Wajah beliau tertunduk, sandal berlumuran darah di sampingnya. Tapi kedua tangan beliau terangkat dalam doa. Di langit, warna senja keunguan yang indah. Warna ungu senja, coklat tamerah darah, emas cahaya terakhir. Mood sedih namun penuh harapan. Komposisi luas — figur kecil di bawah langit besar, menunjukkan kesendirian namun juga keagungan.*

---

## 🌟 Janji Allah yang Indah

Meskipun tahun itu sangat berat, Allah tidak meninggalkan Nabi-Nya. Allah berfirman kepada Nabi Muhammad SAW:

"Bukankah Engkau dulu seorang yatim, lalu Aku melindungimu? Bukankah Engkau dulu bingung, lalu Aku memberimu petunjuk? Bukankah Engkau dulu kekurangan, lalu Aku mencukupkanmu?"

Kata-kata Allah ini seperti pelukan hangat di malam yang dingin. Allah mengingatkan Nabi bahwa dari dulu Allah selalu menjaganya, dan Allah akan terus menjaganya.

Apa yang terjadi setelah tahun kesedihan ini? Allah memberikan hadiah yang sangat luar biasa kepada Nabi Muhammad SAW — yaitu perjalanan Isra' Mi'raj! Sebuah perjalanan ajaib dari Makkah ke Masjidil Aqsha, lalu naik menembus tujuh langit hingga bertemu Allah secara langsung. Subhanallah!

Itulah pelajaran penting dari kisah ini: setelah kesedihan yang berat, pasti akan datang kebahagiaan yang indah. Allah tidak pernah lupa kepada hamba-Nya yang sabar.

> **🎨 Brief Ilustrasi:**
> *Langit malam bertabur bintang yang sangat terang di atas kota Makkah. Dari bawah, Nabi Muhammad SAW mendongak ke langit dengan wajah penuh harapan, dan dari langit turun cahaya keemasan yang hangat menyinari beliau. Di kejauhan, siluet Ka'bah terlihat. Warna biru tua langit, emas cahaya ilahi, putih bintang. Mood penuh pengharapan dan keajaiban. Komposisi vertikal — cahaya dari atas turun ke bawah, menghubungkan langit dan bumi.*
am-al-huzn-wafat-khadijah-abu-thalib.general-en:
# 'Am al-Huzn — The Year of Sorrow: The Deaths of Khadijah and Abu Talib (619 CE)

## Context

The tenth year of prophethood (619 CE) was one of the most difficult periods in the life of **Prophet Muhammad SAW**. This year is known in *sirah* literature as *'Am al-Huzn* — the Year of Sorrow — because during it, the Prophet lost the two closest people who had served as the main pillars supporting his mission: **Khadijah bint Khuwaylid**, his beloved wife, and **Abu Talib** ibn Abd al-Muttalib, his uncle and protector.^1

Both losses occurred within a very short span of time — some narrations mention a gap of only three days, while others indicate several months — and the impact on the Prophet Muhammad's emotional state and political situation was profound.^2

To understand the weight of this loss, it must be remembered that both events occurred immediately after the three-year boycott at Shi'b Abi Talib (616–619 CE), which had drained the physical and mental reserves of all members of Banu Hashim. Bodies weakened by prolonged starvation most likely accelerated the decline in both their health.^3

## The Events

### The Death of Abu Talib

**Abu Talib** ibn Abd al-Muttalib passed away in the month of Rajab in the tenth year of prophethood (around mid-619 CE).^4 For over forty years, he had been Muhammad's guardian and protector — from the time Muhammad was orphaned at the age of eight until he became a prophet and messenger.

Abu Talib's relationship with his nephew is one of the most touching narratives in Islamic history. Although he never openly pronounced the *shahadah*, his entire life was devoted to protecting Muhammad from the threats of the Quraysh. He sacrificed his social standing, economic comfort, and even his own safety during the three years of boycott.^5

Ibn Hisham narrated that when Abu Talib fell gravely ill, Prophet Muhammad SAW visited him and earnestly pleaded with his uncle to utter the testimony of faith. At Abu Talib's bedside were also **Abu Jahl** and **Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah**, who continuously urged Abu Talib to remain upon the religion of his forefathers, Abd al-Muttalib.^6

In Sahih al-Bukhari it is narrated^23:

> قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لأَبِي طَالِبٍ: قُلْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ أَشْهَدُ لَكَ بِهَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ. قَالَ: لَوْلاَ أَنْ تُعَيِّرَنِي قُرَيْشٌ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّمَا حَمَلَهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ الْجَزَعُ لأَقْرَرْتُ بِهَا عَيْنَكَ. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: إِنَّكَ لاَ تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ
>
> *"The Prophet SAW said to Abu Talib: 'Say La ilaha illallah, and I will testify on your behalf with it on the Day of Judgment.' Abu Talib replied: 'Were it not that the Quraysh would reproach me — they would say I uttered it only out of fear — I would have cooled your eyes with it.' Then Allah revealed: 'Indeed, you do not guide whom you love.'"*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 1360, Kitab al-Jana'iz

The verse revealed in connection with this event was:

> إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ ﴿٥٦﴾
>
> *"Indeed, you (O Muhammad) do not guide whom you love, but Allah guides whom He wills, and He is most knowing of the rightly guided. ﴾56﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Qasas (28): 56

Abu Talib's death left an enormous vacuum of protection. Without the protection of a respected clan chief, Prophet Muhammad SAW was now exposed to direct physical attacks from the Quraysh. Not long after Abu Talib's death, someone threw dust on the Prophet's head as he walked through the marketplace — an act of humiliation that no one would have dared commit while Abu Talib was alive.^7

### The Death of Khadijah bint Khuwaylid

**Khadijah bint Khuwaylid** passed away in the month of Ramadan of the tenth year of prophethood, only a short time after the death of Abu Talib.^8 She was the first and only wife of Prophet Muhammad SAW during her lifetime — their marriage lasted approximately twenty-five years.

Khadijah was far more than a wife. She was the first person to believe in Muhammad's message. When the first revelation descended at the Cave of Hira and the Prophet returned home trembling, it was Khadijah who calmed him with soothing words:^9

Ibn Hisham narrated Khadijah's words:

"Rejoice, O son of my uncle! Be steadfast! By Allah, Allah will never humiliate you. Indeed, you maintain the ties of kinship, bear the burdens of others, provide for the destitute, honor your guests, and assist those afflicted by calamity."^1 ^0

Khadijah was also the primary economic supporter of the Islamic mission in its earliest days. As a successful businesswoman before her marriage to Muhammad, her wealth was used to fund the struggle of preaching and to sustain the lives of persecuted Muslims.^1 ^1

The Messenger of Allah SAW loved Khadijah deeply and never forgot her throughout his life. In a hadith narrated by Aisha:

> مَا غِرْتُ عَلَى أَحَدٍ مِنْ نِسَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَا غِرْتُ عَلَى خَدِيجَةَ وَمَا رَأَيْتُهَا وَلَكِنْ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يُكْثِرُ ذِكْرَهَا
>
> *"I was never jealous of any of the Prophet's wives as I was jealous of Khadijah, even though I never saw her. But the Prophet SAW would frequently mention her."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 3818, Kitab Fada'il al-Sahabah

Moreover, Allah the Almighty Himself sent greetings to Khadijah through the angel Jibril:

> أَنَّ جِبْرِيلَ قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: هَذِهِ خَدِيجَةُ قَدْ أَتَتْ مَعَهَا إِنَاءٌ فِيهِ إِدَامٌ أَوْ طَعَامٌ أَوْ شَرَابٌ، فَإِذَا هِيَ أَتَتْكَ فَاقْرَأْ عَلَيْهَا السَّلَامَ مِنْ رَبِّهَا وَبَشِّرْهَا بِبَيْتٍ فِي الْجَنَّةِ مِنْ قَصَبٍ لَا صَخَبَ فِيهِ وَلَا نَصَبَ
>
> *"Jibril said to the Prophet SAW: 'Here is Khadijah coming with a vessel containing condiment, food, or drink. When she comes to you, convey to her greetings of peace from her Lord, and give her glad tidings of a house in Paradise made of pearl, in which there is no noise nor fatigue.'"*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 3820, Kitab Fada'il al-Sahabah

### The Dual Impact of Loss

The loss of Abu Talib and Khadijah within close succession created a dual crisis for Prophet Muhammad SAW:^1 ^2

**Politically**, the Prophet lost his tribal protection. Abu Talib was succeeded by **Abu Lahab** as head of Banu Hashim, who was Islam's greatest adversary. Abu Lahab openly withdrew tribal protection from the Prophet — an act virtually unprecedented in Arab tradition, whereby a tribal chief refused to protect a member of his own tribe.^1 ^3

**Emotionally**, the Prophet lost the life companion who for twenty-five years had been his confidante, source of strength, and first partner in the mission. Khadijah was his emotional anchor during the most difficult times.^1 ^4

**Economically**, Khadijah's death meant the loss of the primary source of funding that had sustained the activities of preaching and the Prophet's family life.^1 ^5

## Dialogue and Sacred Texts

Prophet Muhammad SAW's grief over both losses was profound. Historians record that he named this year *'Am al-Huzn* — a designation reflecting the depth of sorrow he experienced.^1 ^6

It was in this state of anguish that Allah the Almighty sent consolation through His words:

> أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيمًا فَآوَىٰ ﴿٦﴾ وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا فَهَدَىٰ ﴿٧﴾ وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلًا فَأَغْنَىٰ ﴿٨﴾
>
> *"Did He not find you an orphan and give you refuge? ﴾6﴿ And He found you lost and guided you. ﴾7﴿ And He found you in need and made you self-sufficient. ﴾8﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Duha (93): 6–8

These verses, though the precise timing of their revelation is debated, serve as an eternal consolation that Allah never abandons His Prophet. Every loss would be replaced with something greater — and indeed, not long afterward, Allah bestowed upon the Prophet the event of Isra' Mi'raj as heavenly consolation.^1 ^7

## Analysis and Lessons

### The Humanity of a Prophet

*'Am al-Huzn* reveals the deeply human side of Prophet Muhammad SAW. He was not a figure untouched by sadness. He wept, grieved, and felt loss as any human being does. This, in fact, makes him a real and emulable exemplar — a human being who faced suffering with patience and surrender to Allah, not with indifference.^1 ^8

### The Role of Women in Islamic History

Khadijah's death highlights how central the role of women was in Islamic history from its very inception. Khadijah was not a passive figure — she was an entrepreneur, advisor, financial supporter, and the first believer. Without her courage in proposing to Muhammad, without her economic support, and without her emotional strength, the journey of Islamic preaching in its earliest years would have been profoundly different.^1 ^9

### Trial Before Bestowal

In the Islamic tradition of faith, *'Am al-Huzn* is understood as a severe trial preceding a great gift. After this year of sorrow, Allah bestowed upon Prophet Muhammad SAW the event of Isra' Mi'raj — the night journey that elevated him beyond the seven heavens. This pattern — severe trial followed by magnificent grace — is one of the central themes in the prophetic narrative.^2 ^0

### The Journey to Ta'if

After losing his two pillars of support, Prophet Muhammad SAW sought new backing outside Mecca. He traveled to **Ta'if** to invite the leaders of Banu Thaqif to embrace Islam. However, instead of a welcome, he was driven out and pelted with stones by the townspeople until his feet bled.^2 ^1

The Ta'if episode added another layer of suffering atop *'Am al-Huzn*, yet it also produced one of the most moving supplications in Islamic history — the Prophet's prayer as he sat alone, bleeding and rejected, yet still surrendering himself to Allah and refusing to seek vengeance upon the people of Ta'if.^2 ^2

# 'Am al-Huzn — Tahun Kesedihan: Wafatnya Khadijah dan Abu Thalib (619 M)

## Konteks

Tahun kesepuluh kenabian (619 M) merupakan salah satu periode paling berat dalam kehidupan **Nabi Muhammad SAW**. Tahun ini dikenal dalam literatur sirah sebagai *'Am al-Huzn* — Tahun Kesedihan — karena di dalamnya Nabi kehilangan dua orang terdekat yang selama ini menjadi pilar utama penyangga dakwah beliau: **Khadijah binti Khuwaylid**, istri tercinta, dan **Abu Thalib** bin Abdul Muththalib, paman dan pelindung beliau.^2

Kedua kepergian ini terjadi dalam jarak waktu yang sangat berdekatan — sebagian riwayat menyebut hanya selang tiga hari, sementara riwayat lain menyebut selang beberapa bulan — dan dampaknya terhadap kondisi emosional dan situasi politik Nabi Muhammad SAW sangat mendalam.^1

Untuk memahami beratnya kehilangan ini, perlu diingat bahwa kedua peristiwa ini terjadi segera setelah tiga tahun pemboikotan di Syi'b Abu Thalib (616–619 M), yang telah menguras fisik dan mental seluruh anggota Bani Hasyim. Tubuh yang telah dilemahkan oleh kelaparan berkepanjangan kemungkinan besar menjadi faktor yang mempercepat kemunduran kesehatan keduanya.^4

## Peristiwa

### Wafatnya Abu Thalib

**Abu Thalib** bin Abdul Muththalib wafat pada bulan Rajab tahun kesepuluh kenabian (sekitar pertengahan 619 M).^1 Selama lebih dari empat puluh tahun, ia telah menjadi pengasuh dan pelindung Muhammad — sejak Muhammad yatim piatu di usia delapan tahun hingga menjadi nabi dan rasul.

Hubungan Abu Thalib dengan keponakannya merupakan salah satu narasi paling menyentuh dalam sejarah Islam. Meskipun ia tidak pernah secara terbuka mengucapkan syahadat, seluruh hidupnya dicurahkan untuk melindungi Muhammad dari ancaman Quraisy. Ia mengorbankan kedudukan sosial, kenyamanan ekonomi, dan bahkan keselamatannya sendiri selama tiga tahun pemboikotan.^2

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Abu Thalib jatuh sakit keras, Nabi Muhammad SAW mengunjunginya dan memohon dengan sangat agar pamannya mengucapkan kalimat syahadat. Di sisi ranjang Abu Thalib juga hadir **Abu Jahl** dan **Abdullah bin Abi Umayyah** yang terus membujuk Abu Thalib agar tetap pada agama nenek moyangnya, Abdul Muththalib.^1

Dalam Shahih al-Bukhari diriwayatkan:

> قَالَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لأَبِي طَالِبٍ: قُلْ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ أَشْهَدُ لَكَ بِهَا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ. قَالَ: لَوْلاَ أَنْ تُعَيِّرَنِي قُرَيْشٌ يَقُولُونَ إِنَّمَا حَمَلَهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ الْجَزَعُ لأَقْرَرْتُ بِهَا عَيْنَكَ. فَأَنْزَلَ اللَّهُ: إِنَّكَ لاَ تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ
>
> *"Nabi SAW berkata kepada Abu Thalib: 'Ucapkanlah La ilaha illallah, aku akan bersaksi untukmu dengannya pada hari kiamat.' Abu Thalib menjawab: 'Kalau bukan karena Quraisy akan mencela — mereka akan berkata bahwa aku mengucapkannya karena ketakutan — niscaya aku akan menyejukkan matamu dengannya.' Maka Allah menurunkan: 'Sesungguhnya engkau tidak dapat memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang engkau cintai.' ﴾56﴿"*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 1360, Kitab al-Jana'iz

Ayat yang turun terkait peristiwa ini adalah:

> إِنَّكَ لَا تَهْدِي مَنْ أَحْبَبْتَ وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ يَهْدِي مَن يَشَاءُ ۚ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ ﴿٥٦﴾
>
> *"Sesungguhnya engkau (Muhammad) tidak dapat memberi petunjuk kepada orang yang engkau cintai, tetapi Allah memberi petunjuk kepada siapa yang Dia kehendaki, dan Dia lebih mengetahui orang-orang yang mau menerima petunjuk. ﴾56﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Qashash (28): 56

Wafatnya Abu Thalib meninggalkan kekosongan perlindungan yang sangat besar. Tanpa perlindungan seorang kepala kabilah yang dihormati, Nabi Muhammad SAW kini terbuka terhadap serangan fisik langsung dari Quraisy. Tidak lama setelah Abu Thalib wafat, seseorang melemparkan debu ke kepala Nabi ketika beliau berjalan di pasar — suatu tindakan penghinaan yang tidak pernah berani dilakukan siapa pun selama Abu Thalib masih hidup.^5

### Wafatnya Khadijah binti Khuwaylid

**Khadijah binti Khuwaylid** wafat pada bulan Ramadhan tahun kesepuluh kenabian, hanya beberapa waktu setelah wafatnya Abu Thalib.^1 Ia merupakan istri pertama dan satu-satunya istri Nabi Muhammad SAW selama Khadijah masih hidup — pernikahan mereka berlangsung selama kurang lebih dua puluh lima tahun.

Khadijah bukan sekadar istri. Ia adalah orang pertama yang beriman kepada risalah Muhammad. Ketika wahyu pertama turun di Gua Hira dan Nabi pulang dalam keadaan gemetar, Khadijah-lah yang menenangkan beliau dengan kata-kata yang melegakan:^3

Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan perkataan Khadijah:

"Bergembiralah, wahai putra pamanku! Teguhkanlah hatimu! Demi Allah, Allah tidak akan pernah menghinakanmu. Sesungguhnya engkau menyambung tali silaturahmi, menanggung beban orang lain, memberi kepada orang yang tidak punya, memuliakan tamu, dan menolong orang-orang yang tertimpa musibah."^1

Khadijah juga merupakan penyokong ekonomi utama dakwah Islam pada masa-masa awal. Sebagai seorang pengusaha sukses sebelum menikah dengan Muhammad, kekayaannya digunakan untuk membiayai perjuangan dakwah dan menopang kehidupan kaum muslimin yang teraniaya.^2

Rasulullah SAW sangat mencintai Khadijah dan tidak pernah melupakannya sepanjang hidup beliau. Dalam hadits yang diriwayatkan oleh Aisyah:

> مَا غِرْتُ عَلَى أَحَدٍ مِنْ نِسَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم مَا غِرْتُ عَلَى خَدِيجَةَ وَمَا رَأَيْتُهَا وَلَكِنْ كَانَ النَّبِيُّ صلى الله عليه وسلم يُكْثِرُ ذِكْرَهَا
>
> *"Aku tidak pernah cemburu terhadap seorang pun dari istri-istri Nabi SAW sebagaimana cemburuku terhadap Khadijah, padahal aku tidak pernah melihatnya. Namun Nabi SAW selalu banyak menyebutnya."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 3818, Kitab Fada'il al-Shahabah

Lebih dari itu, Allah SWT sendiri mengirimkan salam kepada Khadijah melalui malaikat Jibril:

> أَنَّ جِبْرِيلَ قَالَ لِلنَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم: هَذِهِ خَدِيجَةُ قَدْ أَتَتْ مَعَهَا إِنَاءٌ فِيهِ إِدَامٌ أَوْ طَعَامٌ أَوْ شَرَابٌ، فَإِذَا هِيَ أَتَتْكَ فَاقْرَأْ عَلَيْهَا السَّلَامَ مِنْ رَبِّهَا وَبَشِّرْهَا بِبَيْتٍ فِي الْجَنَّةِ مِنْ قَصَبٍ لَا صَخَبَ فِيهِ وَلَا نَصَبَ
>
> *"Sesungguhnya Jibril berkata kepada Nabi SAW: 'Ini Khadijah datang membawa wadah berisi lauk, makanan, atau minuman. Jika ia datang kepadamu, sampaikan salam dari Tuhannya kepadanya, dan berilah ia kabar gembira tentang sebuah rumah di surga dari mutiara yang di dalamnya tidak ada kebisingan dan tidak ada keletihan.'"*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 3820, Kitab Fada'il al-Shahabah

### Dampak Ganda Kehilangan

Kehilangan Abu Thalib dan Khadijah dalam waktu yang berdekatan menciptakan krisis ganda bagi Nabi Muhammad SAW:^2

**Secara politik**, Nabi kehilangan perlindungan kesukuan. Abu Thalib digantikan oleh **Abu Lahab** sebagai kepala Bani Hasyim, yang justru merupakan musuh terbesar Islam. Abu Lahab secara terbuka mencabut perlindungan kesukuan terhadap Nabi, suatu tindakan yang nyaris tidak pernah terjadi dalam tradisi Arab — seorang kepala suku menolak melindungi anggota sukunya sendiri.^1

**Secara emosional**, Nabi kehilangan pendamping hidup yang selama dua puluh lima tahun menjadi tempat berkeluh kesah, sumber kekuatan, dan partner dakwah pertama. Khadijah adalah sandaran emosional beliau di saat-saat tersulit.^5

**Secara ekonomi**, wafatnya Khadijah berarti hilangnya sumber dana utama yang selama ini menopang aktivitas dakwah dan kehidupan keluarga Nabi.^2

## Dialog dan Dalil

Kesedihan Nabi Muhammad SAW atas kedua kehilangan ini sangat mendalam. Para sejarawan mencatat bahwa beliau menyebut tahun ini sebagai *'Am al-Huzn* — sebuah penamaan yang mencerminkan betapa dalamnya duka yang beliau rasakan.^4

Dalam kondisi terpuruk inilah Allah SWT mengirimkan penghiburan melalui firman-Nya:

> أَلَمْ يَجِدْكَ يَتِيمًا فَآوَىٰ ﴿٦﴾ وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالًّا فَهَدَىٰ ﴿٧﴾ وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلًا فَأَغْنَىٰ ﴿٨﴾
>
> *"Bukankah Dia mendapatimu sebagai seorang yatim, lalu Dia melindungimu? ﴾6﴿ Dan Dia mendapatimu sebagai seorang yang bingung, lalu Dia memberikan petunjuk. ﴾7﴿ Dan Dia mendapatimu sebagai seorang yang kekurangan, lalu Dia memberikan kecukupan. ﴾8﴿"*
> — QS. Al-Dhuha (93): 6–8

Ayat-ayat ini, meskipun turunnya diperdebatkan apakah tepat pada tahun ini, menjadi penghiburan abadi bahwa Allah tidak pernah meninggalkan Nabi-Nya. Setiap kehilangan akan digantikan dengan pemberian yang lebih besar — dan memang, tidak lama kemudian Allah menganugerahi Nabi peristiwa Isra' Mi'raj sebagai penghiburan langit.^2

## Analisis dan Hikmah

### Kemanusiaan Seorang Nabi

*'Am al-Huzn* memperlihatkan sisi kemanusiaan Nabi Muhammad SAW secara mendalam. Beliau bukan sosok yang tidak tersentuh oleh kesedihan. Beliau menangis, berduka, dan merasakan kehilangan sebagaimana manusia lainnya. Hal ini justru menjadikan beliau teladan yang nyata dan dapat diikuti — seorang manusia yang menghadapi penderitaan dengan kesabaran dan kepasrahan kepada Allah, bukan dengan ketidakpedulian.^5

### Peran Perempuan dalam Sejarah Islam

Wafatnya Khadijah menyoroti betapa sentralnya peran perempuan dalam sejarah Islam sejak awal. Khadijah bukan figur pasif — ia adalah pengusaha, penasihat, penyokong dana, dan orang pertama yang beriman. Tanpa keberaniannya melamar Muhammad, tanpa dukungan ekonominya, dan tanpa kekuatan emosionalnya, perjalanan dakwah Islam pada tahun-tahun awal akan sangat berbeda.^2

### Ujian Sebelum Pemberian

Dalam tradisi keimanan Islam, *'Am al-Huzn* dipahami sebagai ujian berat yang mendahului pemberian besar. Setelah tahun penuh kesedihan ini, Allah menganugerahi Nabi Muhammad SAW peristiwa Isra' Mi'raj — perjalanan malam yang mengangkat beliau melampaui tujuh langit. Pola ini — ujian berat diikuti karunia agung — menjadi salah satu tema sentral dalam narasi kenabian.^2

### Perjalanan ke Tha'if

Setelah kehilangan dua pilar penyangganya, Nabi Muhammad SAW berusaha mencari dukungan baru di luar Makkah. Beliau melakukan perjalanan ke **Tha'if** untuk mengajak pemimpin Bani Tsaqif memeluk Islam. Namun, alih-alih disambut, beliau diusir dan dilempari batu oleh penduduk kota hingga kaki beliau berdarah.^1

Peristiwa Tha'if menambah lapisan penderitaan di atas *'Am al-Huzn*, namun juga memunculkan salah satu doa paling mengharukan dalam sejarah Islam — doa Nabi di saat beliau duduk seorang diri, berdarah dan terbuang, namun tetap berserah diri kepada Allah dan menolak untuk membalas dendam kepada penduduk Tha'if.^5

## Daftar Pustaka

1. Ibn Hisham, *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*, jilid 1, tahqiq Musthafa al-Saqqa dkk. (Kairo: Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955).
2. Al-Mubarakfuri, Shafiyyurrahman, *Al-Rahiq al-Makhtum* (Riyadh: Dar al-Salam, 1976).
3. Al-Bukhari, *Shahih al-Bukhari*.
4. Ibn Sa'd, *Al-Thabaqat al-Kubra*, jilid 1 (Beirut: Dar Shadir, 1968).
5. Ibn Kathir, *Al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah*, jilid 3 (Beirut: Dar al-Fikr, 1986).

6. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Al-Qashash (28): 56; QS. Al-Dhuha (93): 6–8.

# The Battle of Yamamah 📖⚔️

---

## The Liar from Yamamah

In a region called **Yamamah**, far to the east of Arabia, there was a man named **Musaylimah**. He claimed to be a prophet — but he was a great liar! That is why he was nicknamed **al-Kadhdhab**, which means "the liar."

Musaylimah once sent a letter to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, claiming to also be a messenger of Allah. But the Prophet replied briefly: *"From Muhammad, messenger of Allah, to Musaylimah the liar."*

After the Prophet ﷺ passed away, Musaylimah became even bolder. He gathered **40,000 followers** and made up false rules — including allowing things that Allah had forbidden!

🎨 *Illustration: A vast Yamamah desert landscape with a large encampment in the distance. In the foreground, a dark silhouette of a figure (Musaylimah) standing atop a sand dune with hands raised. Dominant colors: dark red and barren brown. Mood: threatening and dark. Composition: small figure on hilltop, large encampment in background.*

---

## Khalid ibn al-Walid Is Sent

Caliph **Abu Bakr** knew that Musaylimah was extremely dangerous. So he sent the greatest commander in Islam: **Khalid ibn al-Walid**, known as the *Sword of Allah*.

Khalid brought about **13,000 warriors**. Among them were many **Quran memorizers** — special people who had memorized every verse of the Quran in their hearts! In those days, the Quran had not yet been written into one book, so these memorizers were the ones keeping the Quran safe.

When they reached Yamamah, the Muslim army faced a very tough battle. Musaylimah's forces were far larger and were fighting on their own land. Three times the Muslim army was pushed back!

🎨 *Illustration: Khalid ibn al-Walid on a white horse, leading mounted warriors across the desert. Behind him, rows of Muslim fighters with green banners. Dominant colors: white, green, and sky blue. Mood: valiant and determined. Composition: Khalid in the lead, army stretching out behind him.*

---

## The Companions' Battle Cry

Just when the Muslim army was close to defeat, the companions rallied each other. They shouted to one another:

*"O Ansar! Show your spirit!"*
*"O Muhajirin! Show your tenacity!"*

A companion named **Abu Hudhayfah** cried out: *"O people of the Quran! Adorn the Quran with your deeds!"*

And **Thabit ibn Qays** planted himself in the ground and declared: *"I will not move from this spot!"* He fought bravely until he fell as a martyr.

These companions showed that the Quran is not just for reading and memorizing — it is for living by, even at the cost of the greatest sacrifice.

🎨 *Illustration: Muslim companions raising hands to rally each other on the battlefield. Dust swirling, banners fluttering. In the center, a companion stands firm planting a flag. Dominant colors: gold and sandy brown. Mood: heroic and spirited. Composition: group of warriors from the side, sunlight breaking through the dust.*

---

## The Garden of Death

Musaylimah's forces finally retreated and hid behind the walls of a **large walled garden**. They thought the tall walls would protect them.

But a brave companion named **al-Bara' ibn Malik** shouted: *"Throw me into the garden!"* The companions lifted him on their shields and threw him over the wall! Al-Bara' fought alone inside the garden and managed to open the gate from within!

The Muslim army charged in. Inside that garden, **Musaylimah the liar** was finally killed by the spear of **Wahshi ibn Harb**.

The garden became known as **Hadiqat al-Mawt** — the Garden of Death.

🎨 *Illustration: A high stone-walled garden, its gate thrown wide open. Al-Bara' visible inside, having just opened the door. Muslim soldiers rushing in through the gate. Dominant colors: stone gray, garden green, and gold. Mood: dramatic and action-packed. Composition: garden gate centered as the focal point, warriors running toward it.*

---

## The Price of Victory

The Muslims won, but at a very heavy price. About **700 companions** fell in this battle, and among them were hundreds of **Quran memorizers**.

This loss deeply worried **Umar ibn al-Khattab**. If more and more Quran memorizers died in battle, what if the Quran was lost? Umar urged Abu Bakr: *"We must compile the Quran into one book immediately!"*

This became the beginning of the **compilation of the Quran** — so that Allah's revelation would be preserved forever, not only in people's memories, but also in writing.

🎨 *Illustration: A quiet battlefield after the war, banners planted in the ground, sun setting on the horizon. In the corner, the shadow of a companion kneeling in prayer for the martyrs. Dominant colors: sunset orange and purple sky. Mood: moving and reflective. Composition: wide landscape, small figure in the bottom corner.*

---

## Lessons from Yamamah

The Battle of Yamamah teaches us:

- **The Quran is priceless** — the companions were willing to sacrifice their lives to protect it
- **True bravery** — al-Bara' let himself be thrown alone into the middle of the enemy for victory
- **Every crisis has wisdom** — the fall of the memorizers led to the Quran being compiled into one book
- **Truth always prevails** — even though Musaylimah had 40,000 followers, lies still lost

Allah says: **"Do not think of those who have been killed in the cause of Allah as dead. Rather, they are alive with their Lord. ﴾169﴿"** (QS. Ali Imran: 169)

🎨 *Illustration: An open Quran manuscript on a wooden stand, with gentle golden light radiating from its pages. In the background, the silhouette of the Madinah mosque and a starry sky. Dominant colors: gold and dark blue. Mood: sacred and peaceful. Composition: manuscript centered at bottom, mosque in the upper background, stars scattered above.*
