# The Death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ — 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH

## Context: Signs of Farewell

In the months preceding his death, Prophet Muhammad ﷺ gave subtle indications that his prophetic mission was nearing its end. The revelation of Surah An-Nasr during the Farewell Hajj was understood by several senior companions — particularly **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** and Abdullah ibn Abbas — as a notification of the Prophet's approaching death.^1

Imam al-Bukhari narrates that Abdullah ibn Abbas said: Umar ibn al-Khattab once invited me to a gathering of the veterans of Badr — though some objected due to my young age. Umar asked about the meaning of Surah An-Nasr. When the others were silent, I answered: *"It is a notification of the approaching death of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ."* Umar said: *"I know nothing of its meaning except what you have stated."*^1

During Ramadan of 10 AH, the Prophet ﷺ performed *i'tikaf* (spiritual retreat) for twenty days — twice his usual ten days. He also reviewed the Quran with Jibril twice in his final year — whereas he normally reviewed it once each Ramadan. Al-Bukhari narrates that the Prophet ﷺ told his daughter **Fatimah**: *"Jibril used to review the Quran with me once every year, but this year he reviewed it twice. I can only see this as a sign that my death is near."*^1 ^4

Fatimah wept upon hearing this. But the Prophet ﷺ then whispered to her again, and this time she smiled. When **Aisha** later asked what he had said, Fatimah revealed that the second whisper was news that she would be the foremost among the women of Paradise and the first of his family to join him.^1

## The Onset of Illness: The Night at Baqi'

Imam al-Bukhari and Ibn Hisham narrate that the Prophet's illness began at the end of the month of Safar, 11 AH. One night, the Prophet ﷺ awoke his servant Abu Muwayhib and said: *"I have been commanded to seek forgiveness for the people of Baqi'."*^1 ^2

They went to the Baqi' cemetery in the darkness of night. There, the Prophet ﷺ stood among the graves of the companions who had preceded him and prayed:

> السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَا أَهْلَ الْمَقَابِرِ لِيَهْنِ لَكُمْ مَا أَصْبَحْتُمْ فِيهِ مِمَّا أَصْبَحَ فِيهِ النَّاسُ أَقْبَلَتِ الْفِتَنُ كَقِطَعِ اللَّيْلِ الْمُظْلِمِ
>
> *"Peace be upon you, O dwellers of the graves. Glad tidings to you for the state you are in compared to what the living face. Trials approach like patches of dark night."*
> — Narrated by Muslim, Kitab al-Jana'iz

Then he told Abu Muwayhib: *"I have been offered the keys to the treasures of this world and eternal life therein followed by Paradise, or to meet my Lord and Paradise. I have chosen to meet my Lord and Paradise."*^2 After that night, the Prophet ﷺ began to suffer severe headaches.

## Days of Illness in Aisha's Chamber

### Moving to Aisha's Room

The Prophet ﷺ maintained a rotating schedule among the rooms of his wives. As the illness grew worse, he began asking repeatedly: *"Where am I tomorrow? Where am I tomorrow?"* His wives understood that he wished to be in Aisha's room, so they all permitted him to remain there.^1

**Aisha bint Abu Bakr** narrated her experience of caring for the Prophet ﷺ during his illness. She recounted that the Prophet ﷺ would lean against her chest or rest in her lap, and she would recite the *mu'awwidhat* (protective surahs) while passing his own hand over his body — for the Prophet's hand carried more blessing than anyone else's.^1

### Severe Fever

Al-Bukhari narrates that the Prophet ﷺ suffered from extremely high fever. Abdullah ibn Mas'ud once touched the Prophet's body and exclaimed: *"O Messenger of Allah, you are suffering a very severe fever!"* The Prophet ﷺ replied:

> إِنِّي أُوعَكُ كَمَا يُوعَكُ رَجُلَانِ مِنْكُمْ
>
> *"Indeed, I suffer from fever as severely as two men among you."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 5648

Ibn Mas'ud asked: *"Is that because you receive a double reward?"* The Prophet ﷺ answered: *"Yes, that is so."*^1 Even in his most acute suffering, the Prophet ﷺ transformed every moment into a lesson for his community.

### The Final Address in the Mosque

Despite his severe illness, the Prophet ﷺ exerted himself to ascend the mosque pulpit a few days before his death. Al-Bukhari narrates that he said:

> إِنَّ عَبْدًا خَيَّرَهُ اللَّهُ بَيْنَ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ مِنْ زَهْرَةِ الدُّنْيَا مَا شَاءَ وَبَيْنَ مَا عِنْدَهُ فَاخْتَارَ مَا عِنْدَهُ
>
> *"Indeed, there is a servant whom Allah has given the choice between the splendor of this world, as much as he wishes, and what is with Allah — and he has chosen what is with Allah."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 466

**Abu Bakr** immediately wept upon hearing this — he alone understood that the "servant" was the Prophet ﷺ himself, and that this meant farewell was imminent.^1 The other companions wondered at Abu Bakr's tears — why would he weep over mention of "a servant"? They would understand the meaning only later.

The Prophet ﷺ then ordered that all doors opening onto the mosque be sealed, except the door of Abu Bakr — a signal that Abu Bakr would be the one to lead the community after him.^1

### The Command for Abu Bakr to Lead the Prayer

When the Prophet ﷺ could no longer lead the prayer himself, he commanded: *"Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer."* Aisha tried to redirect this duty to Umar, knowing her father Abu Bakr would weep. But the Prophet ﷺ insisted:

> مُرُوا أَبَا بَكْرٍ فَلْيُصَلِّ بِالنَّاسِ ... إِنَّكُنَّ لَأَنْتُنَّ صَوَاحِبُ يُوسُفَ
>
> *"Tell Abu Bakr to lead the people in prayer ... You are indeed like the female companions of Yusuf."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 713

His meaning was that, just as the women in the story of Prophet Yusuf displayed one thing while concealing another intention, Aisha's real reason was personal — not that Abu Bakr was unworthy.^1

Abu Bakr led the prayer seventeen times during the Prophet's illness.^3 During one prayer, the Prophet ﷺ felt slightly better and came out to the mosque supported by Ali ibn Abi Talib and al-Fadl ibn Abbas. Seeing the Prophet ﷺ approach, Abu Bakr stepped back from the imam's position, but the Prophet ﷺ gestured for him to remain. The Prophet ﷺ sat beside Abu Bakr and prayed together — Abu Bakr followed the Prophet's movements, and the congregation followed Abu Bakr.^1

## The Final Day: Monday, 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH

### The Last Dawn

Imam al-Bukhari narrates from Anas ibn Malik: on Monday morning, as Abu Bakr was leading the Fajr prayer, the Prophet ﷺ drew aside the curtain of Aisha's room that faced the mosque. The companions saw his face — radiant as a page of the Quran. They nearly abandoned their prayer from sheer joy, believing the Prophet ﷺ had recovered.^1

The Prophet ﷺ smiled at the sight of the neatly ordered rows, then gestured for them to continue their prayer. He let the curtain fall once more. That was the last time the companions saw the face of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in full.

### The Final Moments

Aisha narrated the final moments of the Prophet's life in vivid detail. He was resting against her chest. Abdurrahman ibn Abu Bakr (Aisha's brother) entered carrying a *siwak* (tooth stick). The Prophet ﷺ gazed at the *siwak*, and Aisha understood that he wanted it. She took it, chewed it to soften it, then gave it to the Prophet ﷺ. He cleaned his teeth thoroughly — more thoroughly than Aisha had ever seen.^1

Then the Prophet ﷺ raised his hand — or his finger — and looked upward. Aisha heard him say:

> مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَالصِّدِّيقِينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ وَالصَّالِحِينَ
>
> *"With those upon whom Allah has bestowed favor among the prophets, the truthful, the martyrs, and the righteous."*

Then he said:

> اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَارْحَمْنِي وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالرَّفِيقِ الْأَعْلَى
>
> *"O Allah, forgive me, have mercy on me, and join me with al-Rafiq al-A'la (the Highest Companion)."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 4440

He repeated "al-Rafiq al-A'la" three times. Then his hand fell, and he departed to be with Allah. Aisha said: *"He passed away between my chin and my chest."*^1

Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* records that it was the time of Duha (mid-morning) on Monday, 12 Rabi al-Awwal, 11 AH — corresponding to June 8, 632 CE.^3 He was sixty-three years old.

## The Companions' Reactions

### Umar ibn al-Khattab: Denial and Anguish

The news of the Prophet's death struck the companions with unimaginable force. **Umar ibn al-Khattab** — a man renowned for his strength and resolve — refused to accept reality. Al-Bukhari narrates that Umar stood before the people and swore:

*"By Allah, the Messenger of Allah has not died! He has gone to meet his Lord just as Musa went to meet his Lord and was absent from his people for forty nights. By Allah, the Messenger of Allah will return and sever the hands and feet of those who claim he has died!"*^1

Umar continued to speak passionately, threatening anyone who said the Prophet ﷺ had died. The listeners were confused — some believed him, others doubted.

### Abu Bakr: Steadfastness in Crisis

While Umar was still speaking, **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** arrived at the mosque. He had been at his residence in Sunh (on the outskirts of Madinah) when he heard the news. He went directly to Aisha's room, uncovered the cloth from the Prophet's face, kissed his forehead, and said: *"By my father and mother, O Messenger of Allah. You are good in life and good in death."*^1

Abu Bakr then came out to the mosque. Umar was still speaking. Abu Bakr asked him to be silent, but Umar continued shouting. Abu Bakr did not force the matter — he began speaking on his own, and the people left Umar to listen to Abu Bakr.

Abu Bakr praised Allah, then spoke the words that would echo through the ages of Islamic history:

> مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ مُحَمَّدًا فَإِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ مَاتَ وَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ حَيٌّ لَا يَمُوتُ
>
> *"Whoever among you worshipped Muhammad — then Muhammad has died. And whoever among you worshipped Allah — then Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die."*
> — Narrated by al-Bukhari, no. 3667

Then Abu Bakr recited the Quranic verse:

> وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِكُمْ ﴿١٤٤﴾
>
> *"Muhammad is not but a messenger. Other messengers have passed before him. So if he were to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels?"*
> — QS. Ali Imran (3): 144

Al-Bukhari narrates that when Abu Bakr recited this verse, the people felt as though they were hearing it for the first time — even though they had memorized it years ago. Umar collapsed to the ground — his legs could no longer support him. He said: *"By Allah, it was as though I had never heard this verse until Abu Bakr recited it."*^1

### Grief Engulfing Madinah

Ibn Sa'd in *Tabaqat al-Kubra* describes Madinah on that day as a city submerged in grief unlike anything it had known before. Anas ibn Malik said: *"The day the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Madinah, everything was radiant. And the day he died, everything turned dark. We had scarcely dusted off our hands from burying him before we felt our very hearts had changed."*^3

## Washing, Funeral Prayer, and Burial

### Washing the Body

Ibn Sa'd narrates that the Prophet's body was washed by Ali ibn Abi Talib, al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib (the Prophet's uncle), al-Fadl and Qutham (both sons of al-Abbas), Usamah ibn Zayd, and Shuqran (the Prophet's servant). Ali performed the actual washing while al-Abbas and his sons helped turn the body.^3

Ali remarked during the washing: *"By my father and mother, how fragrant you are — in life and in death."*^3 The Prophet ﷺ was washed without his garments being removed — a distinction unique to him.

### Funeral Prayer

What was unique about the Prophet's funeral was that **no single imam led the prayer**. The companions entered in waves — group after group — and each prayed individually over his body. Men first, then women, then children.^1 ^3

This process continued from Monday until the middle of the night between Tuesday and Wednesday.

### Burial

The companions initially disagreed about the burial site. Some proposed the mosque, others proposed Baqi'. Abu Bakr resolved the matter by conveying a hadith he had heard from the Prophet ﷺ:

> مَا قَبَضَ اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا إِلَّا فِي الْمَوْضِعِ الَّذِي يُحِبُّ أَنْ يُدْفَنَ فِيهِ
>
> *"Allah does not take the soul of a prophet except in the place where he wishes to be buried."*
> — Narrated by al-Tirmidhi

Thus the Prophet ﷺ was buried in Aisha's room — the place where he had died — beneath his bed. The grave was dug by Abu Talhah al-Ansari, who constructed a *lahd* (a lateral niche in the side of the grave, the burial style of Madinah).^3

The burial took place in the middle of the night on Wednesday. The last person to descend into the grave was Qutham ibn al-Abbas.

## Analysis: The Meaning of the Prophet's Death for Islamic Civilization

### The End of Revelation, the Beginning of Responsibility

With the death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ, revelation from heaven was severed forever. No prophet would come after him — this is a foundational belief of Islam. Yet the end of revelation did not mean the end of guidance; the Quran and Sunnah he left behind became an eternal compass for humanity.

### The Test of Leadership

The moments following the Prophet's death constituted the most severe test of leadership in Islamic history. The contrast between Umar's reaction (emotional, in denial) and Abu Bakr's response (composed, decisive, rooted in the Quran) illustrates why Abu Bakr was destined to become the first caliph. True leadership is tested not when all is well, but when crisis strikes.

### The Humanity of the Prophet ﷺ

One of the most important lessons from the Prophet's death is the affirmation of his humanity. Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was not God — he was a human being who fell ill, suffered, and ultimately died like all others. Yet the way he faced death — with prayer, surrender to Allah, and compassion for those closest to him — made his very death a final lesson for his community.

### Eternal Love

The relationship between the Prophet ﷺ and his companions — especially Aisha who nursed him with devoted tenderness, Abu Bakr who demonstrated unwavering faith in the darkest moment, and Umar whose emotional reaction revealed the depth of his love — represents the most beautiful portrait of human bonds in Islamic history.

## Lessons: Teachings from the Darkest Day

The death of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ teaches Muslims several fundamental lessons:

**First**, that Islam does not depend on a single individual — however great that individual may be — but on the teachings he brought. Abu Bakr reminded: *"Whoever worshipped Muhammad — Muhammad has died. Whoever worshipped Allah — Allah is Ever-Living and shall never die."*

**Second**, that death is a certainty for every creature. If the Prophet ﷺ, the most beloved of Allah, died, then no one is immortal in this world.

**Third**, that how a person faces crisis defines their character. Abu Bakr became the model for managing grief — he wept, kissed the Prophet's forehead, then rose and led the community. Grief did not destroy him; he transformed his sorrow into strength.

**Fourth**, that the Prophet's final words — choosing "al-Rafiq al-A'la" (the Highest Companion) — demonstrate that for the believer, death is not an ending, but a reunion with Allah and His beloved ones.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad ibn Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Sahih* (Sahih Bukhari). Hadith no. 4440 (death of the Prophet), no. 3667 (Abu Bakr's address), no. 5648 (the Prophet's fever), no. 466 (sermon of the servant given a choice), no. 713 (command for Abu Bakr to lead prayer).

2. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abd al-Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah*. Ed. Mustafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, and Abd al-Hafiz Shalabi. Cairo: Maktabat Mustafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Vol. 2 (chapter on the death of the Messenger of Allah). Beirut: Dar Sadir.
4. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Ali Imran (3): 144.

# Wafat Rasulullah ﷺ — 12 Rabiul Awal 11 H

## Konteks: Tanda-Tanda Perpisahan

Beberapa bulan sebelum wafatnya, Nabi Muhammad ﷺ telah memberikan isyarat-isyarat halus bahwa misi kenabiannya mendekati akhir. Turunnya Surah An-Nashr saat Haji Wada' dipahami oleh sejumlah sahabat senior — terutama **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** dan Abdullah bin Abbas — sebagai pemberitahuan tentang dekatnya ajal Nabi ﷺ.^1

Imam al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Abdullah bin Abbas berkata: Umar bin Khattab pernah mengundangku dalam majelis para tokoh Badr — meskipun sebagian mereka keberatan karena usiaku yang masih muda. Umar bertanya tentang makna Surah An-Nashr. Ketika yang lain diam, aku menjawab: *"Itu adalah pemberitahuan tentang dekatnya ajal Rasulullah ﷺ."* Umar berkata: *"Aku tidak mengetahui maknanya kecuali seperti apa yang kauketahui."*^1

Selama bulan Ramadhan tahun 10 Hijriyah, Nabi ﷺ melakukan *i'tikaf* selama dua puluh hari — dua kali lipat dari kebiasaan beliau yang biasanya sepuluh hari. Beliau juga mempelajari al-Quran bersama Jibril sebanyak dua kali dalam tahun terakhirnya — padahal biasanya hanya sekali setiap Ramadhan. Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkata kepada putrinya **Fatimah**: *"Jibril biasa mengulang al-Quran bersamaku sekali setiap tahun, dan tahun ini ia mengulangnya dua kali. Aku tidak melihat itu kecuali sebagai pertanda bahwa ajalku telah dekat."*^1 ^4

Fatimah menangis mendengar ini. Namun Nabi ﷺ kemudian berbisik lagi kepadanya, dan kali ini ia tersenyum. Ketika ditanya kemudian oleh **Aisyah**, Fatimah menjawab bahwa bisikan kedua itu adalah kabar bahwa ia akan menjadi penghulu wanita penghuni surga dan orang pertama dari keluarga Nabi yang menyusul beliau.^1

## Awal Sakit: Peristiwa di Baqi'

Imam al-Bukhari dan Ibn Hisham meriwayatkan bahwa sakit Nabi ﷺ dimulai pada akhir bulan Safar tahun 11 Hijriyah. Suatu malam, Nabi ﷺ membangunkan pelayannya Abu Muwayhib dan berkata: *"Aku diperintahkan untuk memintakan ampunan bagi ahli Baqi'."*^1 ^2

Mereka pergi ke pemakaman Baqi' di tengah kegelapan malam. Di sana, Nabi ﷺ berdiri di antara kubur-kubur para sahabat yang telah mendahuluinya dan berdoa:

> السَّلَامُ عَلَيْكُمْ يَا أَهْلَ الْمَقَابِرِ لِيَهْنِ لَكُمْ مَا أَصْبَحْتُمْ فِيهِ مِمَّا أَصْبَحَ فِيهِ النَّاسُ أَقْبَلَتِ الْفِتَنُ كَقِطَعِ اللَّيْلِ الْمُظْلِمِ
>
> *"Salam sejahtera atas kalian, wahai penghuni kubur. Beruntunglah kalian dengan apa yang kalian alami dibandingkan apa yang dialami manusia. Fitnah-fitnah datang silih berganti bagaikan potongan malam yang gelap."*
> — HR Muslim, Kitab al-Jana'iz

Lalu beliau berkata kepada Abu Muwayhib: *"Aku telah ditawari kunci-kunci perbendaharaan dunia dan kekekalan di dalamnya kemudian surga, atau bertemu Tuhanku dan surga. Aku memilih bertemu Tuhanku dan surga."*^2 Setelah malam itu, Nabi ﷺ mulai merasakan sakit kepala yang berat.

## Hari-Hari Sakit di Rumah Aisyah

### Perpindahan ke Kamar Aisyah

Nabi ﷺ memiliki jadwal bergilir di antara kamar-kamar istri beliau. Ketika sakit semakin berat, beliau mulai bertanya berulang kali: *"Di mana aku besok? Di mana aku besok?"* Para istri memahami bahwa beliau ingin berada di kamar Aisyah, maka mereka semua mengizinkan beliau untuk menetap di sana.^1

**Aisyah binti Abu Bakr** meriwayatkan pengalamannya merawat Nabi ﷺ selama sakit. Ia menceritakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersandar di dadanya atau di pangkuannya, dan ia membacakan *mu'awwidzat* (surah-surah pelindung) sambil mengusapkan tangan Nabi ﷺ sendiri ke tubuh beliau — karena tangan Nabi ﷺ lebih berkah dari tangan siapa pun.^1

### Demam yang Sangat Berat

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ menderita demam yang sangat tinggi. Abdullah bin Mas'ud pernah menyentuh tubuh Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: *"Wahai Rasulullah, engkau menderita demam yang sangat berat!"* Nabi ﷺ menjawab:

> إِنِّي أُوعَكُ كَمَا يُوعَكُ رَجُلَانِ مِنْكُمْ
>
> *"Sesungguhnya aku menderita demam seberat yang diderita dua orang dari kalian."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 5648

Ibn Mas'ud bertanya: *"Apakah itu karena engkau mendapat dua pahala?"* Nabi ﷺ menjawab: *"Ya, begitulah."*^1 Ini menunjukkan bahwa bahkan dalam penderitaan terberatnya, Nabi ﷺ tetap menjadikan setiap momen sebagai pelajaran bagi umatnya.

### Khutbah Terakhir di Masjid

Meskipun sakit berat, Nabi ﷺ memaksakan diri untuk naik ke mimbar masjid beberapa hari sebelum wafatnya. Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa beliau bersabda:

> إِنَّ عَبْدًا خَيَّرَهُ اللَّهُ بَيْنَ أَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُ مِنْ زَهْرَةِ الدُّنْيَا مَا شَاءَ وَبَيْنَ مَا عِنْدَهُ فَاخْتَارَ مَا عِنْدَهُ
>
> *"Sesungguhnya ada seorang hamba yang diberi pilihan oleh Allah antara kemewahan dunia yang ia kehendaki atau apa yang ada di sisi Allah, dan ia memilih apa yang ada di sisi Allah."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 466

**Abu Bakr** langsung menangis ketika mendengar ini — dialah satu-satunya yang memahami bahwa "hamba" yang dimaksud adalah Nabi ﷺ sendiri, dan bahwa ini berarti perpisahan sudah sangat dekat.^1 Para sahabat lain heran melihat Abu Bakr menangis — mengapa ia menangis hanya karena mendengar tentang "seorang hamba"? Mereka baru memahami maknanya kemudian.

Nabi ﷺ kemudian memerintahkan agar semua pintu yang menghadap ke masjid ditutup, kecuali pintu Abu Bakr — sebagai isyarat bahwa Abu Bakr-lah yang akan menggantikan beliau memimpin umat.^1

### Perintah Shalat kepada Abu Bakr

Ketika Nabi ﷺ sudah tidak mampu lagi mengimami shalat, beliau memerintahkan: *"Suruh Abu Bakr mengimami shalat."* Aisyah mencoba mengalihkan tugas ini kepada Umar, karena ia tahu ayahnya Abu Bakr akan menangis. Namun Nabi ﷺ bersikeras:

> مُرُوا أَبَا بَكْرٍ فَلْيُصَلِّ بِالنَّاسِ ... إِنَّكُنَّ لَأَنْتُنَّ صَوَاحِبُ يُوسُفَ
>
> *"Suruh Abu Bakr mengimami shalat ... Kalian ini seperti teman-teman wanita Yusuf."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 713

Maksudnya: sebagaimana wanita-wanita di zaman Nabi Yusuf yang menampakkan sesuatu tapi menyembunyikan maksud lain, Aisyah sebenarnya ingin Abu Bakr tidak mengimami shalat karena alasan pribadi — bukan karena Abu Bakr tidak layak.^1

Abu Bakr mengimami shalat sebanyak tujuh belas kali selama Nabi ﷺ sakit.^3 Dalam salah satu shalat, Nabi ﷺ merasa sedikit lebih baik dan keluar ke masjid dengan bantuan Ali bin Abi Thalib dan al-Fadhl bin Abbas. Melihat Nabi ﷺ datang, Abu Bakr mundur dari posisi imam, tetapi Nabi ﷺ memberi isyarat agar ia tetap di tempatnya. Nabi ﷺ duduk di samping Abu Bakr dan shalat bersama — Abu Bakr mengikuti gerakan Nabi ﷺ, dan jamaah mengikuti gerakan Abu Bakr.^1

## Hari Terakhir: Senin, 12 Rabiul Awal 11 H

### Subuh Terakhir

Imam al-Bukhari meriwayatkan dari Anas bin Malik: pada hari Senin pagi, saat Abu Bakr mengimami shalat Subuh, Nabi ﷺ menyingkap tabir kamar Aisyah yang menghadap ke masjid. Para sahabat melihat wajah beliau — cerah seperti lembaran mushaf. Mereka nyaris meninggalkan shalat karena gembira, mengira Nabi ﷺ sudah sembuh.^1

Nabi ﷺ tersenyum melihat barisan shalat yang rapi, lalu memberi isyarat agar mereka melanjutkan shalat. Beliau menurunkan kembali tabir kamarnya. Itulah saat terakhir para sahabat melihat wajah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ secara utuh.

### Detik-Detik Terakhir

Aisyah meriwayatkan detik-detik terakhir kehidupan Nabi ﷺ dengan sangat detail. Saat itu beliau bersandar di dadanya. Abdurrahman bin Abu Bakr (saudara laki-laki Aisyah) masuk membawa siwak. Nabi ﷺ memandang siwak itu, dan Aisyah memahami bahwa beliau menginginkannya. Aisyah mengambil siwak itu, mengunyahnya untuk melembutkannya, lalu memberikannya kepada Nabi ﷺ. Beliau bersiwak dengan sangat bersih — yang terbersih yang pernah Aisyah lihat.^1

Kemudian Nabi ﷺ mengangkat tangannya — atau jarinya — dan memandang ke atas. Aisyah mendengar beliau berkata:

> مَعَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ وَالصِّدِّيقِينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ وَالصَّالِحِينَ
>
> *"Bersama orang-orang yang Allah beri nikmat dari kalangan para nabi, orang-orang yang benar, para syuhada, dan orang-orang saleh."*

Lalu beliau berkata:

> اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَارْحَمْنِي وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالرَّفِيقِ الْأَعْلَى
>
> *"Ya Allah, ampunilah aku, rahmatilah aku, dan pertemukanlah aku dengan al-Rafiq al-A'la (Kawan Yang Mahatinggi)."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 4440

Beliau mengulangi kalimat "al-Rafiq al-A'la" tiga kali. Kemudian tangannya terkulai, dan beliau berpulang ke sisi Allah. Aisyah berkata: *"Beliau wafat antara dagu dan dadaku."*^1

Ibn Sa'd dalam *Tabaqat al-Kubra* mencatat bahwa saat itu adalah waktu Dhuha (pagi menjelang siang) hari Senin, 12 Rabiul Awal tahun 11 Hijriyah — atau 8 Juni 632 M menurut penanggalan Masehi.^3 Beliau berusia 63 tahun.

## Reaksi Para Sahabat

### Umar bin Khattab: Penolakan dan Kemarahan

Kabar wafatnya Nabi ﷺ menghantam para sahabat dengan kekuatan yang tidak terbayangkan. **Umar bin Khattab** — seorang yang dikenal dengan kekuatan dan ketegasannya — menolak menerima kenyataan. Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa Umar berdiri di hadapan orang-orang dan bersumpah:

*"Demi Allah, Rasulullah tidak wafat! Beliau pergi menemui Tuhannya sebagaimana Musa pergi menemui Tuhannya dan menghilang dari kaumnya selama empat puluh malam. Demi Allah, Rasulullah akan kembali dan memotong tangan dan kaki orang-orang yang mengira beliau telah wafat!"*^1

Umar terus berbicara dengan berapi-api, mengancam siapa pun yang mengatakan Nabi ﷺ telah wafat. Orang-orang yang mendengarnya bingung — sebagian percaya, sebagian ragu.

### Abu Bakr: Keteguhan di Saat Krisis

Saat Umar masih berbicara, **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** tiba di masjid. Ia telah berada di kediamannya di Sunh (pinggiran Madinah) ketika mendengar kabar duka. Ia langsung menuju kamar Aisyah, membuka kain yang menutupi wajah Nabi ﷺ, mencium kening beliau, dan berkata: *"Demi ayah dan ibuku, wahai Rasulullah. Engkau baik saat hidup dan baik saat wafat."*^1

Kemudian Abu Bakr keluar ke masjid. Umar masih berbicara. Abu Bakr memintanya diam, tetapi Umar terus berteriak. Abu Bakr tidak memaksa — beliau mulai berbicara sendiri, dan orang-orang meninggalkan Umar untuk mendengarkan Abu Bakr.

Abu Bakr memuji Allah, lalu berkata dengan kalimat yang akan dikenang sepanjang sejarah Islam:

> مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ مُحَمَّدًا فَإِنَّ مُحَمَّدًا قَدْ مَاتَ وَمَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ يَعْبُدُ اللَّهَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ حَيٌّ لَا يَمُوتُ
>
> *"Barangsiapa di antara kalian yang menyembah Muhammad, maka sesungguhnya Muhammad telah wafat. Dan barangsiapa di antara kalian yang menyembah Allah, maka sesungguhnya Allah Maha Hidup dan tidak akan pernah mati."*
> — HR al-Bukhari, no. 3667

Lalu Abu Bakr membacakan ayat al-Quran:

> وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ انقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰ أَعْقَابِكُمْ ﴿١٤٤﴾
>
> *"Muhammad itu tidak lain hanyalah seorang rasul; sesungguhnya telah berlalu sebelumnya beberapa orang rasul. Apakah jika dia wafat atau dibunuh, kamu berbalik ke belakang?"*
> — QS. Ali Imran (3): 144

Al-Bukhari meriwayatkan bahwa ketika Abu Bakr membacakan ayat ini, orang-orang merasa seolah-olah mereka baru pertama kali mendengarnya — padahal mereka sudah menghafalnya bertahun-tahun. Umar jatuh terduduk — kakinya tidak mampu lagi menopangnya. Ia berkata: *"Demi Allah, seolah aku baru mendengar ayat ini ketika Abu Bakr membacakannya."*^1

### Duka yang Melanda Madinah

Ibn Sa'd dalam *Tabaqat al-Kubra* menggambarkan Madinah pada hari itu sebagai kota yang tenggelam dalam kesedihan yang belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya. Anas bin Malik berkata: *"Hari ketika Rasulullah ﷺ datang ke Madinah, segala sesuatu bersinar cerah. Dan hari ketika beliau wafat, segala sesuatu menjadi gelap. Kami belum selesai menepuk-nepuk tanah dari tangan kami setelah menguburkan beliau, dan kami sudah mengingkari hati kami."*^3

Artinya: perubahan yang mereka rasakan begitu mendalam sehingga hati mereka terasa berbeda — seolah kehilangan sesuatu yang tidak akan pernah bisa dikembalikan.

## Pemandian, Shalat Jenazah, dan Pemakaman

### Pemandian Jenazah

Ibn Sa'd meriwayatkan bahwa jenazah Nabi ﷺ dimandikan oleh Ali bin Abi Thalib, al-Abbas bin Abdul Muthalib (paman Nabi), al-Fadhl dan Qutsam (keduanya putra al-Abbas), Usamah bin Zaid, dan Syuqran (pelayan Nabi). Ali-lah yang langsung memandikan, sementara al-Abbas dan putra-putranya membantu membalikkan tubuh beliau.^3

Ali berkata saat memandikan: *"Demi ayah dan ibuku, betapa harum engkau, baik saat hidup maupun saat wafat."*^3 Nabi ﷺ dimandikan tanpa dilepas pakaiannya — sebuah kekhususan yang hanya berlaku untuk beliau.

### Shalat Jenazah

Yang unik dari jenazah Nabi ﷺ adalah bahwa **tidak ada seorang imam yang memimpin shalat jenazah**. Para sahabat masuk secara bergelombang — kelompok demi kelompok — dan masing-masing shalat sendiri-sendiri atas jenazah beliau. Laki-laki terlebih dahulu, kemudian perempuan, kemudian anak-anak.^1 ^3

Proses ini berlangsung dari hari Senin hingga tengah malam Selasa malam Rabu.

### Pemakaman

Para sahabat sempat berbeda pendapat tentang tempat pemakaman. Ada yang mengusulkan di masjid, ada yang mengusulkan di Baqi'. Abu Bakr menyelesaikan perdebatan ini dengan menyampaikan hadits yang ia dengar dari Nabi ﷺ:

> مَا قَبَضَ اللَّهُ نَبِيًّا إِلَّا فِي الْمَوْضِعِ الَّذِي يُحِبُّ أَنْ يُدْفَنَ فِيهِ
>
> *"Allah tidak mencabut nyawa seorang nabi kecuali di tempat yang ia sukai untuk dimakamkan di sana."*
> — HR at-Tirmidzi

Maka Nabi ﷺ dimakamkan di kamar Aisyah — tempat beliau wafat — di bawah tempat tidurnya. Penggalian kubur dilakukan oleh Abu Thalhah al-Anshari, yang membuat *lahd* (liang lahat di sisi kubur, model pemakaman Madinah).^3

Pemakaman berlangsung pada tengah malam Rabu. Orang terakhir yang turun ke liang kubur adalah Qutsam bin al-Abbas.

## Analisis: Makna Wafatnya Nabi ﷺ bagi Peradaban Islam

### Akhir Wahyu, Awal Tanggung Jawab

Dengan wafatnya Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, wahyu dari langit terputus untuk selamanya. Tidak akan ada lagi nabi setelah beliau — inilah keyakinan dasar Islam. Namun berakhirnya wahyu bukan berarti berakhirnya bimbingan; al-Quran dan Sunnah yang beliau tinggalkan menjadi panduan abadi bagi umat manusia.

### Ujian Kepemimpinan

Detik-detik setelah wafatnya Nabi ﷺ adalah ujian kepemimpinan terberat dalam sejarah Islam. Kontras antara reaksi Umar (emosional, menolak kenyataan) dan Abu Bakr (tenang, tegas, berakar pada al-Quran) menunjukkan mengapa Abu Bakr layak menjadi khalifah pertama. Kepemimpinan sejati diuji bukan saat semuanya baik-baik saja, melainkan saat krisis melanda.

### Kemanusiaan Nabi ﷺ

Salah satu pelajaran terpenting dari peristiwa wafatnya Nabi ﷺ adalah penegasan kemanusiaan beliau. Nabi Muhammad ﷺ bukan Tuhan — beliau adalah manusia yang sakit, menderita, dan pada akhirnya wafat seperti manusia lainnya. Namun cara beliau menghadapi kematian — dengan doa, penyerahan diri kepada Allah, dan kasih sayang kepada orang-orang terdekat — menjadikan kematian beliau sendiri sebagai pelajaran terakhir yang beliau berikan kepada umatnya.

### Cinta yang Abadi

Hubungan antara Nabi ﷺ dan para sahabatnya — terutama Aisyah yang merawatnya dengan penuh kasih sayang, Abu Bakr yang menunjukkan keteguhan iman di saat paling gelap, dan Umar yang reaksi emosionalnya justru menunjukkan betapa dalamnya cintanya — adalah gambaran hubungan manusiawi yang paling indah dalam sejarah Islam.

## Hikmah: Pelajaran dari Hari Tergelap

Wafatnya Rasulullah ﷺ mengajarkan umat Islam beberapa pelajaran fundamental:

**Pertama**, bahwa Islam bukan bergantung pada satu pribadi — betapapun agung pribadi itu — melainkan pada ajaran yang beliau bawa. Abu Bakr mengingatkan: *"Barangsiapa yang menyembah Muhammad, Muhammad telah wafat. Barangsiapa yang menyembah Allah, Allah Maha Hidup dan tidak akan pernah mati."*

**Kedua**, bahwa kematian adalah kepastian bagi setiap makhluk. Jika Nabi ﷺ yang paling dicintai Allah saja wafat, maka tidak ada seorang pun yang kekal di dunia ini.

**Ketiga**, bahwa cara seseorang menghadapi krisis menentukan kualitas dirinya. Abu Bakr menjadi teladan dalam mengelola duka — ia menangis, mencium kening Nabi ﷺ, lalu bangkit dan memimpin umat. Duka tidak menghancurkannya; ia menjadikan dukanya sebagai kekuatan.

**Keempat**, bahwa wasiat terakhir Nabi ﷺ — memilih "al-Rafiq al-A'la" (Kawan Yang Mahatinggi) — menunjukkan bahwa bagi orang beriman, kematian bukanlah akhir, melainkan pertemuan dengan Allah dan para kekasih-Nya.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Al-Bukhari, Abu Abdullah Muhammad bin Ismail.** *Al-Jami' al-Shahih* (Shahih Bukhari). Hadits no. 4440 (wafat Nabi), no. 3667 (khutbah Abu Bakr), no. 5648 (demam Nabi), no. 466 (khutbah hamba yang diberi pilihan), no. 713 (perintah Abu Bakr mengimami shalat).

2. **Ibn Hisham, Abu Muhammad Abdul Malik.** *Al-Sirah al-Nabawiyyah* (Sirah Nabawiyyah). Tahqiq: Musthafa al-Saqqa, Ibrahim al-Abyari, dan Abdul Hafiz Syalabi. Kairo: Maktabah Musthafa al-Babi al-Halabi, 1955.

3. **Ibn Sa'd, Abu Abdullah Muhammad.** *Al-Tabaqat al-Kubra*. Jilid 2 (bab wafat Rasulullah). Beirut: Dar Shadir.

4. Al-Qur'an al-Karim. QS. Ali Imran (3): 144.

abu-bakr-as-siddiq-khalifah-pertama.children-en:
# Abu Bakr — The First Caliph 🌟

---

## Who Was Abu Bakr?

**Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** was the closest companion of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. He was the first adult man to accept Islam. He was the one who accompanied the Prophet ﷺ during the migration to Madinah and hid with him in the Cave of Thawr. His title means **"The Supremely Truthful"** — because he always believed whatever the Prophet ﷺ said without any doubt.

After Prophet Muhammad ﷺ passed away, the Muslim community needed a leader. Who would take the Prophet's place? The companions gathered and discussed. In the end, they all agreed: **Abu Bakr** was the most worthy to lead — the first caliph of Islam.

In his very first speech as caliph, Abu Bakr said: *"I have been appointed as your leader, yet I am not the best among you. If I do well, support me. If I err, correct me."* How humble he was!

🎨 *Illustration: Abu Bakr (an elderly man with a white beard, a gentle and wise face) standing at the simple pulpit of the Prophet's Mosque. Before him, rows of companions listening attentively. Warm light enters through the mosque windows. Dominant colors of olive green, cream, and gold. An atmosphere of dignity yet simplicity. Composition of Abu Bakr at the pulpit with the congregation below.*

---

## The Army of Usamah — Keeping the Prophet's Promise

One of the first things Abu Bakr did was send out the **army of Usamah ibn Zayd**. The Prophet ﷺ had ordered this army to march before he passed away, but their departure had been delayed.

Many companions said: "Don't send the army yet! We're in danger!" But Abu Bakr said firmly: **"I will not untie a knot that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ has tied!"**

This meant: if the Prophet ﷺ had given a command, that command must be carried out — no matter how difficult things were. And this decision turned out to be exactly right! Usamah's army succeeded, and the Arab tribes knew that Madinah was still strong.

🎨 *Illustration: A cavalry force with Islamic banners departing from the gates of Madinah into the desert. Abu Bakr stands behind them, seeing them off with his hand raised. A bright golden morning sky. Dominant colors of gold, sandy brown, and green. An atmosphere of determination and courage. Composition of the army moving from right to left with Madinah in the background.*

---

## The Riddah Wars — Defending the Truth

After the Prophet ﷺ passed away, many Arab tribes **apostatized** — they left Islam. Some returned to worshipping idols, some followed false prophets like **Musaylimah the Liar**, and some refused to pay **zakat** (charitable tax).

Abu Bakr was very firm. He said: **"If they withhold even the rope used to tie a camel that they used to give to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, I will fight them for it!"**

The biggest battle was the **Battle of Yamamah** against Musaylimah the false prophet. The fighting was very fierce. In the end, the Muslim forces won and Musaylimah was defeated. In less than a year, the entire Arabian Peninsula was reunited under Islam.

🎨 *Illustration: A child-friendly map of the Arabian Peninsula with markers showing locations of the Riddah battles. Arrows from Madinah pointing in different directions. In the center of the map, an Islamic banner flying. Dominant colors of green, brick red, and gold. An energetic atmosphere. A decorative map composition easy for children to understand.*

---

## Compiling the Quran — Preserving Allah's Word

During the Battle of Yamamah, many **memorizers of the Quran** were killed as martyrs. Umar ibn al-Khattab was very worried: what if more and more memorizers died and parts of the Quran were lost?

Umar proposed to Abu Bakr: **"Let us collect the entire Quran into one book!"**

Abu Bakr was hesitant at first — because the Prophet ﷺ had never done this. But after careful thought, he agreed. He assigned **Zayd ibn Thabit** — the Prophet's scribe — to collect every verse of the Quran from written records and the memorization of the companions.

This was an **incredibly important** decision! Thanks to this project, the Quran we read today is still exactly the same as the Quran revealed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. SubhanAllah!

🎨 *Illustration: Zayd ibn Thabit (a young man) sitting at a desk with scrolls of parchment, writing carefully. Around him, piles of bones, leather, and palm stalks inscribed with verses. An oil lamp illuminates the room. Dominant colors of cream, brown, and gold. An atmosphere of diligence and responsibility. Close-up composition of Zayd writing.*

---

## Abu Bakr's Death — A Simple Leader

Abu Bakr served as caliph for only **two years and three months**. Short, but filled with extraordinary achievements!

Before he died, he appointed **Umar ibn al-Khattab** as his successor. And he requested to be wrapped in his **old, worn-out clothes** — not new cloth. He said: *"The living need new cloth more than the dead."*

Abu Bakr passed away in 634 CE at the age of **63** — the same age as Prophet Muhammad ﷺ when he died. He was buried beside the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.

**Lessons from Abu Bakr:**

⭐ **Humility** — even though he was the highest leader, he never considered himself the best

⚖️ **Firm in truth** — he never compromised when it came to matters of faith

📖 **Preserving the Quran** — thanks to his decision, the Quran has been protected to this day

🤲 **Simplicity** — he lived simply and never pursued luxury

Abu Bakr was an example of the perfect leader — firm yet humble, strong yet compassionate, and always holding on to the truth!

🎨 *Illustration: Two graves side by side in a simple room (Aisha's chamber) with soft light. Small flowers growing around them. Dominant colors of soft green, white, and gold. A peaceful atmosphere filled with blessing. A calm and contemplative composition.*
abu-bakr-as-siddiq-khalifah-pertama.children-id:
# Abu Bakr — Khalifah Pertama 🌟

---

## Siapakah Abu Bakr?

**Abu Bakr as-Siddiq** adalah sahabat terdekat Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Dialah orang pertama dari kalangan laki-laki dewasa yang memeluk Islam. Dialah yang menemani Nabi ﷺ saat hijrah ke Madinah dan bersembunyi di Gua Tsur. Namanya berarti **"Yang Amat Benar"** — karena ia selalu membenarkan apa pun yang dikatakan Nabi ﷺ tanpa ragu.

Setelah Nabi Muhammad ﷺ wafat, umat Islam membutuhkan seorang pemimpin. Siapa yang akan menggantikan Nabi ﷺ? Para sahabat berkumpul dan berdiskusi. Akhirnya, mereka semua sepakat: **Abu Bakr** adalah orang yang paling layak menjadi pemimpin — khalifah pertama umat Islam.

Saat pertama kali berpidato sebagai khalifah, Abu Bakr berkata: *"Aku telah diangkat sebagai pemimpin kalian, padahal aku bukan yang terbaik. Jika aku benar, bantulah aku. Jika aku salah, luruskanlah aku."* Betapa rendah hatinya!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Abu Bakr (pria tua berjenggot putih, wajah teduh dan bijak) berdiri di mimbar masjid Nabawi yang sederhana. Di hadapannya barisan sahabat yang mendengarkan. Cahaya hangat masuk dari jendela masjid. Warna dominan hijau zaitun, krem, dan emas. Suasana penuh wibawa namun sederhana. Komposisi Abu Bakr di mimbar dengan jamaah di bawah.*

---

## Pasukan Usamah — Menepati Janji Nabi ﷺ

Salah satu hal pertama yang dilakukan Abu Bakr adalah mengirimkan **pasukan Usamah bin Zaid**. Nabi ﷺ sudah memerintahkan pasukan ini pergi berperang sebelum beliau wafat, tapi keberangkatan mereka tertunda.

Banyak sahabat berkata: "Jangan kirim pasukan itu dulu! Kita sedang dalam bahaya!" Tapi Abu Bakr berkata dengan tegas: **"Aku tidak akan melepaskan ikatan yang telah diikat oleh Rasulullah ﷺ!"**

Artinya: jika Nabi ﷺ sudah memerintahkan sesuatu, maka perintah itu harus dilaksanakan — tidak peduli betapa sulitnya keadaan. Dan ternyata keputusan ini sangat tepat! Pasukan Usamah berhasil, dan suku-suku Arab tahu bahwa Madinah masih kuat.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Pasukan berkuda dengan bendera Islam berangkat dari gerbang Madinah menuju padang pasir. Abu Bakr berdiri di belakang melepas mereka dengan tangan terangkat. Langit pagi cerah keemasan. Warna dominan emas, cokelat pasir, dan hijau. Suasana penuh tekad dan keberanian. Komposisi pasukan bergerak dari kanan ke kiri dengan Madinah di latar belakang.*

---

## Perang Riddah — Mempertahankan Kebenaran

Setelah Nabi ﷺ wafat, banyak suku Arab yang **murtad** — mereka meninggalkan Islam. Ada yang kembali menyembah berhala, ada yang mengikuti nabi-nabi palsu seperti **Musailamah al-Kadzdzab**, dan ada yang menolak membayar **zakat**.

Abu Bakr sangat tegas. Ia berkata: **"Seandainya mereka menolak memberikan tali pengikat unta yang dulu mereka berikan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, aku akan memerangi mereka!"**

Perang yang paling besar adalah **Perang Yamamah** melawan Musailamah si nabi palsu. Pertempurannya sangat sengit. Akhirnya, pasukan Islam menang dan Musailamah dikalahkan. Dalam waktu kurang dari setahun, seluruh Jazirah Arab kembali bersatu di bawah Islam.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Peta Jazirah Arab bergaya anak-anak dengan tanda-tanda lokasi pertempuran Riddah. Panah-panah dari Madinah menuju berbagai arah. Di tengah peta, bendera Islam berkibar. Warna dominan hijau, merah bata, dan emas. Suasana penuh semangat. Komposisi peta dekoratif yang mudah dipahami anak-anak.*

---

## Mengumpulkan Al-Quran — Menjaga Wahyu Allah

Dalam Perang Yamamah, banyak **penghafal al-Quran** yang gugur sebagai syuhada. Umar bin Khattab sangat khawatir: bagaimana jika semakin banyak penghafal yang wafat dan bagian-bagian al-Quran hilang?

Umar mengusulkan kepada Abu Bakr: **"Mari kita kumpulkan seluruh al-Quran dalam satu kitab!"**

Abu Bakr awalnya ragu — karena Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah melakukan ini. Tapi setelah berpikir matang, ia setuju. Ia menugaskan **Zaid bin Tsabit** — sekretaris Nabi ﷺ — untuk mengumpulkan seluruh ayat al-Quran dari tulisan-tulisan dan hafalan para sahabat.

Ini adalah keputusan yang **sangat penting**! Berkat proyek ini, al-Quran yang kita baca hari ini masih sama persis dengan al-Quran yang diturunkan kepada Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. SubhanAllah!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Zaid bin Tsabit (pemuda) duduk di meja dengan gulungan-gulungan perkamen, menulis dengan hati-hati. Di sekelilingnya tumpukan tulang, kulit, dan pelepah kurma bertuliskan ayat-ayat. Cahaya lampu minyak menerangi ruangan. Warna dominan krem, cokelat, dan emas. Suasana tekun dan penuh tanggung jawab. Komposisi close-up Zaid sedang menulis.*

---

## Wafat Abu Bakr — Pemimpin yang Sederhana

Abu Bakr menjadi khalifah hanya selama **dua tahun tiga bulan**. Singkat, tapi penuh pencapaian.

Sebelum wafat, ia menunjuk **Umar bin Khattab** sebagai penggantinya. Dan ia berwasiat agar dikafankan dengan **pakaian lamanya yang sudah usang** — bukan kain baru. Ia berkata: *"Orang hidup lebih membutuhkan kain baru daripada orang mati."*

Abu Bakr wafat pada tahun 634 M dalam usia **63 tahun** — usia yang sama dengan Nabi Muhammad ﷺ saat wafat. Ia dimakamkan di samping Rasulullah ﷺ.

**Pelajaran dari Abu Bakr:**

⭐ **Rendah hati** — meskipun menjadi pemimpin tertinggi, ia tidak merasa dirinya yang terbaik

⚖️ **Tegas dalam kebenaran** — ia tidak berkompromi dalam urusan agama

📖 **Menjaga al-Quran** — berkat keputusannya, al-Quran terjaga hingga hari ini

🤲 **Sederhana** — ia hidup sederhana dan tidak mengejar kemewahan

Abu Bakr adalah contoh pemimpin yang sempurna — tegas tapi rendah hati, kuat tapi penuh kasih sayang, dan selalu berpegang pada kebenaran!

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Dua kubur berdampingan di dalam ruangan sederhana (kamar Aisyah) dengan cahaya lembut. Bunga-bunga kecil tumbuh di sekitarnya. Warna dominan hijau lembut, putih, dan emas. Suasana damai dan penuh keberkahan. Komposisi tenang dan contemplatif.*
abu-bakr-as-siddiq-khalifah-pertama.general-en:
# Abu Bakr as-Siddiq — The First Caliph

## Context: Crisis After the Death of the Prophet ﷺ

The death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ on 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH (632 CE) left a leadership vacuum without precedent in Islamic history. The Prophet had not explicitly designated a successor — though there were strong indications pointing toward **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq**, such as the command for him to lead prayers during the Prophet's illness and the order to seal all doors to the mosque except Abu Bakr's.^1

The situation on the day of the Prophet's death was critical. His body had not yet been buried, while the question of leadership demanded immediate resolution. The Ansar (the native people of Madinah) gathered at the **Saqifah of Banu Sa'idah** — a meeting hall — to discuss the selection of a leader. They intended to pledge allegiance to Sa'd ibn Ubadah, chief of the Khazraj tribe, as caliph.^1 ^2

## The Event at Saqifah Bani Sa'idah

### The Decisive Meeting

Al-Tabari in *Tarikh al-Rusul wa al-Muluk* narrates the events at Saqifah in considerable detail. When Abu Bakr and Umar learned that the Ansar had assembled at the Saqifah, they hurried there together with Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah.^1

At the Saqifah, the Ansar had already begun their deliberations. Sa'd ibn Ubadah — who was ill at the time — delivered a speech emphasizing the contributions of the Ansar: they were the ones who sheltered the Prophet ﷺ, they provided refuge for the Muhajirin, and they formed the military backbone of Islam.^1

When Abu Bakr arrived, he listened carefully. Then he stood and spoke calmly yet decisively. Ibn Kathir in *al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah* records that Abu Bakr acknowledged every contribution of the Ansar without reservation. However, he then presented the key argument: that the leadership of Islam must rest with the Quraysh, because all the Arab tribes would only accept authority from this tribe, and the Prophet ﷺ himself was from the Quraysh.^2

Abu Bakr then offered: *"I am content to pledge allegiance to either of these two men: Umar ibn al-Khattab or Abu Ubaidah ibn al-Jarrah."*^1 Both immediately refused. Umar said: *"By Allah, we will not accept this leadership while you are alive. You are the most worthy among the Muhajirin. You were the Prophet's companion in the cave. You led the prayer when he was ill."*

### The Pledge to Abu Bakr

Umar immediately extended his hand and pledged allegiance to Abu Bakr. Abu Ubaidah followed. Then the Ansar — save for a handful — pledged as well. Al-Tabari records that the process, though filled with debate, concluded swiftly.^1

The following day, Abu Bakr ascended the pulpit of the Prophet's Mosque and received the general pledge of allegiance from all the people of Madinah. It was then that he delivered his famous inaugural address:

*"O people, I have been appointed as your leader, yet I am not the best among you. If I do well, support me. If I err, correct me. Truthfulness is a trust, and lying is a betrayal. The weak among you is strong in my sight until I restore his right to him. And the strong among you is weak in my sight until I take from him the rights of others. Obey me so long as I obey Allah and His Messenger. But if I disobey Allah and His Messenger, then you owe me no obedience."*^1 ^2

This speech — brief yet monumental — established several revolutionary principles of governance: the accountability of the ruler, the supremacy of law, the people's right to correct their leader, and conditional obedience contingent upon the leader's adherence to divine command.

## Dispatching the Army of Usamah

One of Abu Bakr's first and most controversial decisions as caliph was dispatching the **army of Usamah ibn Zayd**. The Prophet ﷺ had ordered a military expedition to the Roman frontier under the command of Usamah — a young man of approximately 18-20 years — before falling ill. The army had already encamped outside Madinah when the Prophet ﷺ died.^1

Many senior companions urged Abu Bakr to cancel or postpone the expedition. Their reasoning was sound: Madinah was in crisis, tribes were beginning to apostatize, and sending a large force northward would weaken Madinah's defenses. Some even objected to Usamah's youthful command.

Abu Bakr, however, was resolute:

*"By Allah, in Whose hand is my soul, even if I were certain that wild beasts would drag me away, I would still dispatch the army of Usamah as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded. I will not untie a knot that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ has tied."*^1 ^2

This decision demonstrated Abu Bakr's unwavering principle: the Prophet's commands must be carried out without negotiation. Strategically, the decision proved brilliant — Usamah's successful expedition signaled to the Arab tribes that Madinah remained formidable despite the Prophet's death.

## The Riddah Wars: Defending the Integrity of Islam

### The Wave of Apostasy

The greatest crisis Abu Bakr faced was the wave of *riddah* (apostasy) that swept across much of the Arabian Peninsula. Al-Tabari records that the apostasy took several forms:^1

1. **Complete apostasy** — tribes that returned to idol worship and abandoned Islam entirely
2. **False prophets** — individuals who claimed prophethood, most notably **Musaylimah al-Kadhdhab** in Yamamah, Sajah bint al-Harith, and Tulayhah ibn Khuwaylid
3. **Zakat refusers** — tribes that still professed Islam but refused to pay the *zakat* tax to the central government in Madinah

### Abu Bakr's Resolve

The zakat refusers sparked heated debate. Umar and several other companions argued that they should not be fought because they still pronounced the *shahadah*. Abu Bakr, however, refused to compromise:

*"By Allah, if they withhold even the rope used to tie a camel that they used to give to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, I will fight them for withholding it."*^1

Ibn Kathir in *al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah* notes that Abu Bakr's argument rested on the principle that *zakat* is an obligation inseparable from prayer — both are mentioned together in dozens of Quranic verses. To refuse *zakat* was to reject a fundamental pillar of Islam.^2

### The Battle of Yamamah and the Defeat of Musaylimah

The fiercest battle of the Riddah Wars was the **Battle of Yamamah** against Musaylimah al-Kadhdhab and his forces from the Banu Hanifah. Al-Tabari narrates that Musaylimah commanded a very large army and held easily defensible terrain.^1

Abu Bakr dispatched **Khalid ibn al-Walid** to lead the campaign against Musaylimah. The battle was ferocious — many *huffazh* (memorizers of the Quran) fell as martyrs. Ibn Kathir records that approximately seventy memorizers of the Quran were killed at Yamamah — a staggering number given the limited total of memorizers at the time.^2

Ultimately, Wahshi ibn Harb — the same man who had killed Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib at the Battle of Uhud — hurled his spear and struck Musaylimah. The battle ended in a decisive victory for the Muslim forces.

The Riddah Wars as a whole succeeded in restoring the entire Arabian Peninsula to Islamic authority in less than a year — a remarkable military and political achievement.

## The Compilation of the Quran: An Eternal Project

### Background

The large number of Quran memorizers killed at Yamamah raised serious alarm. **Umar ibn al-Khattab** approached Abu Bakr and proposed that the Quran be compiled into a single written manuscript — for if memorizers continued to fall, portions of the Quran could be lost.^1 ^2

Abu Bakr was initially hesitant: *"How can I do something that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ did not do?"* But Umar persisted until Abu Bakr agreed — and he entrusted **Zayd ibn Thabit**, the Prophet's scribe of revelation, with leading the compilation project.

### The Methodology of Compilation

Zayd ibn Thabit employed an extraordinarily rigorous methodology: every verse had to be verified by at least two witnesses who had heard it directly from the Prophet ﷺ, in addition to being cross-checked against the memorization of the *huffazh*. Al-Tabari records that Zayd said: *"By Allah, had they tasked me with moving a mountain, it would not have been heavier than what they commanded me to do in compiling the Quran."*^1

The resulting manuscript was kept by Abu Bakr, then passed to Umar, then to Hafsah bint Umar — until it eventually served as the basis for the standardized copies produced during the caliphate of Uthman ibn Affan.

## The Death of Abu Bakr (634 CE)

Abu Bakr served as caliph for only two years and three months. In the month of Jumada al-Akhirah, 13 AH (634 CE), he fell ill. Ibn al-Jawzi in *Sifat al-Safwa* describes how Abu Bakr faced his illness with the same composure and surrender that the Prophet ﷺ had demonstrated.^3

Before his death, Abu Bakr consulted with the senior companions and designated **Umar ibn al-Khattab** as his successor — a decision accepted by the majority of Muslims.

Abu Bakr requested that he be buried beside the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He also requested that his shroud be his old, worn garments — not new cloth — because *"the living need new cloth more than the dead."*^3

Abu Bakr died on the night of Tuesday, 22 Jumada al-Akhirah, 13 AH, at the age of sixty-three — the same age as the Messenger of Allah ﷺ at his death. He was buried beside the Prophet ﷺ in Aisha's room.

## Analysis: Abu Bakr's Legacy for Islamic Civilization

### Preserving Unity in Times of Division

Abu Bakr's greatest achievement was preserving the unity of the Muslim community at its most critical juncture. When tribes apostatized, when false prophets arose, and when the foundations of the Islamic state trembled, Abu Bakr stood firm. Without his decisiveness in the Riddah Wars, Islam might well have contracted back into a local movement in Madinah.

### A Model of Servant Leadership

Abu Bakr's inaugural address established a standard of leadership unprecedented in the Arab world — and perhaps anywhere in the world at that time. A ruler who openly declares that he is not the best, that the people have the right to correct him, and that obedience to him is conditional — this was an early form of public accountability that remains remarkably modern.

### Preserving Revelation

Abu Bakr's decision to compile the Quran into a single manuscript was one of the most consequential decisions in Islamic history — and in human history. Without this project, the integrity of the Quranic text we read today might have been jeopardized. Abu Bakr understood that preserving revelation was more important than any military conquest.

### The Title "As-Siddiq" — The Truthful

Ibn al-Jawzi in *Sifat al-Safwa* explains why Abu Bakr received the title *as-Siddiq* (the Supremely Truthful). He earned it because he was the first to affirm the Prophet ﷺ during the Night Journey*Isra' wa Mi'raj* — when all of Makkah mocked the Prophet's claim of traveling to Jerusalem in a single night. Abu Bakr said: *"If he said it, then he is truthful."* Without question, without hesitation.^3

This truthfulness was not mere verbal affirmation — it manifested in every action Abu Bakr took: he affirmed the Prophet ﷺ with his wealth, his family, his life, and ultimately with his brief yet monumental leadership.

## Lessons from the First Caliph

**First**, that true leadership is tested in crisis. Abu Bakr did not become caliph during peacetime — he assumed office at the most critical juncture and successfully navigated a storm that threatened total destruction.

**Second**, that principles must not be compromised. Abu Bakr's refusal to compromise on the matter of *zakat* and the Prophet's commands demonstrates that certain things are more important than political convenience.

**Third**, that good leaders prepare their successors. By designating Umar, Abu Bakr ensured a stable continuity of leadership — rather than leaving the community in a vacuum.

**Fourth**, that simplicity and integrity are a leader's true crown. Abu Bakr did not accumulate wealth during his tenure, did not build palaces, and even requested burial in his old garments. Power, for him, was a trust — not a privilege.

---

## Bibliography

1. **Al-Tabari, Abu Ja'far Muhammad ibn Jarir.** *Tarikh al-Rusul wa al-Muluk* (The History of Prophets and Kings). Vol. 3. Cairo: Dar al-Ma'arif.

2. **Ibn Kathir, Ismail ibn Umar.** *Al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah* (The Beginning and the End). Vol. 6. Beirut: Dar al-Fikr, 1986.

3. **Ibn al-Jawzi, Abdurrahman ibn Ali.** *Sifat al-Safwa* (Qualities of the Elite). Vol. 1. Beirut: Dar al-Ma'rifah, 1979.

# Abu Bakr as-Siddiq — Khalifah Pertama

## Konteks: Krisis Setelah Wafatnya Rasulullah ﷺ

Wafatnya Nabi Muhammad ﷺ pada 12 Rabiul Awal 11 Hijriyah (632 M) meninggalkan kekosongan kepemimpinan yang belum pernah terjadi dalam sejarah Islam. Beliau tidak menunjuk pengganti secara eksplisit — meskipun terdapat isyarat-isyarat kuat yang mengarah kepada **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq**, seperti perintah untuk mengimami shalat selama Nabi ﷺ sakit dan perintah menutup semua pintu masjid kecuali pintu Abu Bakr.^1

Situasi pada hari wafatnya Nabi ﷺ sangat genting. Jenazah beliau belum dimakamkan, sementara persoalan kepemimpinan sudah mendesak. Kaum Anshar (penduduk asli Madinah) berkumpul di **Saqifah Bani Sa'idah** — sebuah balai pertemuan — untuk membahas pemilihan pemimpin. Mereka berencana membaiat Sa'd bin Ubadah, pemimpin suku Khazraj, sebagai khalifah.^1 ^2

## Peristiwa Saqifah Bani Sa'idah

### Pertemuan yang Menentukan

Al-Tabari dalam *Tarikh al-Rusul wa al-Muluk* meriwayatkan peristiwa Saqifah dengan sangat detail. Ketika Abu Bakr dan Umar mendengar bahwa kaum Anshar berkumpul di Saqifah, mereka bergegas ke sana bersama Abu Ubaidah bin al-Jarrah.^1

Di Saqifah, kaum Anshar telah memulai pembicaraan. Sa'd bin Ubadah — yang saat itu sedang sakit — menyampaikan pidato yang menekankan jasa-jasa kaum Anshar: merekalah yang melindungi Nabi ﷺ, merekalah yang memberikan tempat bagi kaum Muhajirin, dan merekalah yang menjadi tulang punggung militer Islam.^1

Ketika Abu Bakr tiba, ia mendengarkan dengan saksama. Lalu ia berdiri dan berpidato dengan tenang namun tegas. Ibn Kathir dalam *al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah* mencatat bahwa Abu Bakr mengakui seluruh jasa kaum Anshar — tidak ada yang diragukan. Namun beliau kemudian menyampaikan argumen kunci: bahwa kepemimpinan Islam harus berada di tangan kaum Quraisy, karena seluruh bangsa Arab hanya akan tunduk kepada suku ini, dan Nabi ﷺ sendiri berasal dari Quraisy.^2

Abu Bakr kemudian menawarkan: *"Aku rela membaiat salah satu dari dua orang ini: Umar bin Khattab atau Abu Ubaidah bin al-Jarrah."*^1 Namun keduanya langsung menolak. Umar berkata: *"Demi Allah, kami tidak akan menerima kepemimpinan ini selagi engkau masih hidup. Engkaulah yang paling utama di antara kaum Muhajirin. Engkaulah yang menemani Rasulullah ﷺ di gua. Engkaulah yang mengimami shalat ketika beliau sakit."*

### Baiat Abu Bakr

Umar langsung menjulurkan tangannya dan membaiat Abu Bakr. Abu Ubaidah menyusul. Kemudian kaum Anshar — kecuali segelintir — turut membaiat. Al-Tabari mencatat bahwa proses ini berlangsung cepat meskipun penuh perdebatan.^1

Keesokan harinya, Abu Bakr naik ke mimbar masjid Nabawi dan menerima baiat umum dari seluruh penduduk Madinah. Pada saat itulah beliau menyampaikan pidato pelantikannya yang termasyhur:

*"Wahai manusia, aku telah diangkat sebagai pemimpin kalian, padahal aku bukanlah yang terbaik di antara kalian. Jika aku berbuat baik, bantulah aku. Jika aku berbuat salah, luruskanlah aku. Kejujuran adalah amanah, dan kebohongan adalah pengkhianatan. Orang lemah di antara kalian adalah kuat di sisiku hingga aku mengembalikan haknya. Dan orang kuat di antara kalian adalah lemah di sisiku hingga aku mengambil hak orang lain darinya. Taatilah aku selama aku taat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya. Namun jika aku bermaksiat kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya, maka tidak ada kewajiban taat kalian kepadaku."*^1 ^2

Pidato ini — yang singkat namun monumental — menetapkan beberapa prinsip pemerintahan yang revolusioner: akuntabilitas pemimpin, supremasi hukum, hak rakyat untuk mengkoreksi penguasa, dan ketaatan bersyarat yang bergantung pada kepatuhan pemimpin kepada Allah.

## Mengirimkan Pasukan Usamah

Salah satu keputusan pertama dan paling kontroversial Abu Bakr sebagai khalifah adalah mengirimkan **pasukan Usamah bin Zaid**. Nabi ﷺ telah memerintahkan ekspedisi militer ke perbatasan Romawi di bawah komando Usamah — seorang pemuda berusia sekitar 18-20 tahun — sebelum beliau jatuh sakit. Pasukan ini sudah berkemah di luar Madinah ketika Nabi ﷺ wafat.^1

Banyak sahabat senior mendesak Abu Bakr untuk membatalkan atau menunda ekspedisi ini. Alasannya logis: Madinah sedang dalam krisis, suku-suku mulai murtad, dan mengirimkan pasukan besar ke utara akan melemahkan pertahanan Madinah. Beberapa bahkan keberatan dengan kepemimpinan Usamah yang masih muda.

Namun Abu Bakr bersikeras:

*"Demi Allah yang jiwaku ada di tangan-Nya, seandainya aku yakin bahwa binatang buas akan menyeretku, aku tetap akan mengirimkan pasukan Usamah sebagaimana Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan. Aku tidak akan melepaskan ikatan yang telah diikat oleh Rasulullah ﷺ."*^1 ^2

Keputusan ini menunjukkan prinsip Abu Bakr yang teguh: perintah Rasulullah ﷺ harus dilaksanakan tanpa tawar-menawar. Secara strategis, keputusan ini terbukti brilian — ekspedisi Usamah yang berhasil memberi sinyal kepada suku-suku Arab bahwa Madinah tetap kuat meskipun Nabi ﷺ telah wafat.

## Perang Riddah: Mempertahankan Integritas Islam

### Gelombang Kemurtadan

Krisis terbesar yang dihadapi Abu Bakr adalah gelombang *riddah* (kemurtadan) yang melanda sebagian besar Jazirah Arab. Al-Tabari mencatat bahwa kemurtadan ini terjadi dalam beberapa bentuk:^1

1. **Murtad total** — suku-suku yang kembali menyembah berhala dan meninggalkan Islam sepenuhnya
2. **Nabi-nabi palsu** — munculnya orang-orang yang mengaku sebagai nabi, terutama **Musailamah al-Kadzdzab** di Yamamah, Sajah binti al-Harits, dan Tulaihah bin Khuwailid
3. **Penolak zakat** — suku-suku yang tetap mengaku Muslim tetapi menolak membayar zakat kepada pemerintah pusat di Madinah

### Ketegasan Abu Bakr

Penolak zakat menjadi perdebatan sengit. Umar dan beberapa sahabat lain berpendapat bahwa mereka tidak boleh diperangi karena mereka masih mengucapkan syahadat. Namun Abu Bakr menolak kompromi:

*"Demi Allah, seandainya mereka menolak memberikan tali pengikat unta yang dahulu mereka berikan kepada Rasulullah ﷺ, niscaya aku akan memerangi mereka karena penolakannya itu."*^1

Ibn Kathir dalam *al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah* mencatat bahwa argumen Abu Bakr didasarkan pada prinsip bahwa zakat adalah kewajiban yang tidak terpisahkan dari shalat — keduanya disebutkan bersama dalam puluhan ayat al-Quran. Menolak zakat berarti menolak bagian fundamental dari Islam.^2

### Perang Yamamah dan Kekalahan Musailamah

Pertempuran paling dahsyat dalam Perang Riddah adalah **Perang Yamamah** melawan Musailamah al-Kadzdzab dan pasukannya dari Bani Hanifah. Al-Tabari meriwayatkan bahwa Musailamah memiliki pasukan yang sangat besar dan bertahan di wilayah yang mudah dipertahankan.^1

Abu Bakr mengirimkan **Khalid bin al-Walid** untuk memimpin pasukan melawan Musailamah. Pertempuran ini sangat sengit — banyak *huffazh* (penghafal al-Quran) yang gugur sebagai syuhada. Ibn Kathir mencatat bahwa sekitar tujuh puluh penghafal al-Quran tewas di Yamamah — angka yang sangat besar mengingat jumlah total penghafal saat itu masih terbatas.^2

Akhirnya, Wahsyi bin Harb — orang yang dahulu membunuh Hamzah bin Abdul Muthalib di Perang Uhud — berhasil melemparkan tombaknya yang mengenai Musailamah. Pertempuran berakhir dengan kemenangan telak bagi pasukan Islam.

Perang Riddah secara keseluruhan berhasil mengembalikan seluruh Jazirah Arab ke dalam kekuasaan Islam dalam waktu kurang dari satu tahun — sebuah pencapaian militer dan politik yang luar biasa.

## Pengumpulan Al-Quran: Proyek Abadi

### Latar Belakang

Gugurnya banyak penghafal al-Quran di Perang Yamamah menimbulkan kekhawatiran serius. **Umar bin Khattab** mendatangi Abu Bakr dan mengusulkan agar al-Quran dikumpulkan dalam satu mushaf — karena jika penghafal terus gugur, bagian-bagian al-Quran bisa hilang.^1 ^2

Abu Bakr awalnya ragu: *"Bagaimana aku bisa melakukan sesuatu yang tidak dilakukan oleh Rasulullah ﷺ?"* Namun Umar terus mendesak hingga Abu Bakr menerima — dan beliau menugaskan **Zaid bin Tsabit**, sekretaris wahyu Nabi ﷺ, untuk memimpin proyek pengumpulan ini.

### Metodologi Pengumpulan

Zaid bin Tsabit menggunakan metodologi yang sangat ketat: setiap ayat harus diverifikasi oleh minimal dua saksi yang mendengar langsung dari Nabi ﷺ, selain dicocokkan dengan hafalan para penghafal. Al-Tabari mencatat bahwa Zaid berkata: *"Demi Allah, seandainya mereka membebaniku memindahkan gunung, tidak akan lebih berat dari apa yang mereka perintahkan kepadaku untuk mengumpulkan al-Quran."*^1

Mushaf yang dihasilkan disimpan oleh Abu Bakr, kemudian berpindah ke Umar, lalu ke Hafshah binti Umar — hingga kemudian menjadi dasar penggandaan mushaf pada masa Utsman bin Affan.

## Wafat Abu Bakr (634 M)

Abu Bakr menjabat sebagai khalifah selama hanya dua tahun tiga bulan. Pada bulan Jumadil Akhir tahun 13 Hijriyah (634 M), beliau jatuh sakit. Ibn al-Jawzi dalam *Sifat al-Safwa* menggambarkan bahwa Abu Bakr menghadapi sakitnya dengan ketenangan dan penyerahan diri yang sama seperti yang ditunjukkan Nabi ﷺ.^3

Sebelum wafat, Abu Bakr bermusyawarah dengan para sahabat senior dan menunjuk **Umar bin Khattab** sebagai penggantinya — sebuah keputusan yang diterima oleh mayoritas umat Islam.

Abu Bakr berwasiat agar dimakamkan di samping Rasulullah ﷺ. Ia juga berwasiat agar kain kafannya adalah pakaian lamanya yang sudah usang — bukan kain baru — karena *"orang hidup lebih membutuhkan kain baru daripada orang mati."*^3

Abu Bakr wafat pada malam Selasa, 22 Jumadil Akhir 13 H, dalam usia 63 tahun — usia yang sama dengan Rasulullah ﷺ saat wafat. Beliau dimakamkan di samping Nabi ﷺ di kamar Aisyah.

## Analisis: Warisan Abu Bakr bagi Peradaban Islam

### Menjaga Persatuan di Saat Perpecahan

Pencapaian terbesar Abu Bakr adalah menjaga persatuan umat Islam di saat paling kritis. Ketika suku-suku murtad, ketika nabi-nabi palsu bermunculan, dan ketika fondasi negara Islam goyah, Abu Bakr berdiri teguh. Tanpa ketegasannya dalam Perang Riddah, Islam mungkin akan menyusut kembali menjadi gerakan lokal di Madinah.

### Model Kepemimpinan yang Melayani

Pidato pelantikan Abu Bakr menetapkan standar kepemimpinan yang belum pernah ada sebelumnya di dunia Arab — dan mungkin di dunia mana pun pada masa itu. Seorang penguasa yang secara terbuka menyatakan bahwa ia bukan yang terbaik, bahwa rakyat berhak mengkoreksinya, dan bahwa ketaatan kepadanya bersyarat — ini adalah bentuk awal dari *akuntabilitas publik* yang sangat modern.

### Melestarikan Wahyu

Keputusan Abu Bakr untuk mengumpulkan al-Quran dalam satu mushaf adalah salah satu keputusan paling berpengaruh dalam sejarah Islam — dan sejarah manusia. Tanpa proyek ini, kelestarian teks al-Quran yang kita baca hari ini mungkin akan terancam. Abu Bakr memahami bahwa melestarikan wahyu lebih penting daripada penaklukan militer mana pun.

### Gelar "As-Siddiq" — Yang Amat Benar

Ibn al-Jawzi dalam *Sifat al-Safwa* menjelaskan mengapa Abu Bakr mendapat gelar *as-Siddiq* (Yang Amat Benar). Gelar ini diberikan karena ia adalah orang pertama yang membenarkan Nabi ﷺ saat peristiwa Isra' Mi'raj — ketika seluruh Makkah mengejek klaim Nabi tentang perjalanan malam ke Baitul Maqdis. Abu Bakr berkata: *"Jika ia mengatakannya, maka ia benar."* Tanpa bertanya, tanpa ragu.^3

Kebenaran ini bukan sekadar pembenaran lisan — ia termanifestasi dalam setiap tindakan Abu Bakr: ia membenarkan Nabi ﷺ dengan hartanya, dengan keluarganya, dengan nyawanya, dan pada akhirnya dengan kepemimpinannya yang singkat namun monumental.

## Hikmah: Pelajaran dari Khalifah Pertama

**Pertama**, bahwa kepemimpinan sejati diuji di saat krisis. Abu Bakr tidak menjadi khalifah di masa damai — ia menjabat di saat paling kritis dan berhasil menavigasi badai yang mengancam kehancuran total.

**Kedua**, bahwa prinsip tidak boleh dikompromikan. Penolakan Abu Bakr untuk berkompromi dalam masalah zakat dan perintah Rasulullah ﷺ menunjukkan bahwa ada hal-hal yang lebih penting dari kenyamanan politik.

**Ketiga**, bahwa pemimpin yang baik mempersiapkan penggantinya. Dengan menunjuk Umar, Abu Bakr memastikan kesinambungan kepemimpinan yang stabil — tidak membiarkan umat dalam kekosongan.

**Keempat**, bahwa kesederhanaan dan integritas adalah mahkota sejati seorang pemimpin. Abu Bakr tidak menumpuk kekayaan selama menjabat, tidak membangun istana, dan bahkan meminta dikafankan dengan kain lamanya. Kekuasaan baginya adalah amanah — bukan keistimewaan.

---

## Daftar Pustaka

1. **Al-Tabari, Abu Ja'far Muhammad bin Jarir.** *Tarikh al-Rusul wa al-Muluk* (Sejarah Para Rasul dan Raja-Raja). Jilid 3. Kairo: Dar al-Ma'arif.

2. **Ibn Kathir, Ismail bin Umar.** *Al-Bidayah wa al-Nihayah*. Jilid 6. Beirut: Dar al-Fikr, 1986.

3. **Ibn al-Jawzi, Abdurrahman bin Ali.** *Sifat al-Safwa* (Sifat Orang-Orang Pilihan). Jilid 1. Beirut: Dar al-Ma'rifah, 1979.

perang-riddah-perang-kemurtadan.children-en:
# The Riddah Wars ⚔️

---

## Sad News from Madinah

The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had passed away. All of Madinah was grief-stricken. The companions wept, and even the mighty **Umar ibn al-Khattab** was so overcome with sorrow that he could not accept the reality.

But one man remained calm and strong: **Abu Bakr al-Siddiq**, the Prophet's closest companion. Abu Bakr stood before the people and said: *"Whoever worshipped Muhammad, know that Muhammad has died. Whoever worships Allah, know that Allah is alive and will never die."*

Abu Bakr was then chosen as the **caliph** — the first leader of the Muslim community after the Prophet.

🎨 *Illustration: Interior of the Madinah mosque, Abu Bakr standing firm before the pulpit with a calm yet resolute expression, companions seated and listening with sorrowful faces. Dominant colors: warm brown and muted gold. Mood: grieving yet hopeful. Composition: Abu Bakr centered, soft light from mosque windows.*

---

## The Tribes That Rebelled

After the Prophet's death, many tribes across the Arabian Peninsula refused to obey the new leader. Some stopped paying **zakat** — the obligatory charity to help the poor. Others followed people who falsely claimed to be prophets!

There was **Musaylimah al-Kadhdhab** (the liar) who claimed prophethood in Yamamah. There was **Tulayha** who claimed prophethood among the Banu Asad. And there were others in Yemen and other distant lands.

Nearly the entire Arabian Peninsula was in rebellion! Only Makkah, Madinah, and Taif remained loyal.

🎨 *Illustration: A simple cartoon-style map of the Arabian Peninsula with small icons: green flags on Makkah, Madinah, and Taif; red flags on other regions marking rebellion. Dominant colors: sandy yellow with green-red contrast. Mood: tense but informative. Composition: map centered, compass in the corner.*

---

## Abu Bakr Would Not Compromise

The companions suggested Abu Bakr compromise — let the tribes skip *zakat*, as long as they still prayed. But Abu Bakr firmly refused!

Abu Bakr declared: *"By Allah, I will fight anyone who separates prayer from zakat! If they were to refuse me even a hobbling cord that they used to pay to the Messenger of Allah, I would fight them for it!"*

Abu Bakr knew that Islam is complete — you cannot pick and choose which parts to follow and which to ignore.

🎨 *Illustration: Abu Bakr seated in council with companions, raising his hand firmly as he speaks. Umar listening with a serious expression. Dominant colors: earthy brown and white. Mood: determined and resolute. Composition: close-up of the council, Abu Bakr in the central position.*

---

## Eleven Armies Sent Out

Abu Bakr then prepared **eleven armies**, each led by the best companions! Each army was sent in a different direction across the Arabian Peninsula.

The most famous commander was **Khalid ibn al-Walid** — the warrior known as the *Sword of Allah*. Khalid moved with incredible speed, defeating one rebel force after another. From one battlefield to the next, he brought victory after victory.

Abu Bakr instructed every commander: *"Offer Islam first. If they accept, welcome them back. If they refuse, only then fight them."*

🎨 *Illustration: Eleven banners flying at the gates of Madinah, each held by a mounted commander. Abu Bakr standing at the gate seeing them off. Dominant colors: green, white, and morning-sky blue. Mood: grand and spirited. Composition: wide panorama, armies lined up facing the desert.*

---

## Victory and Reunification

One by one, the rebellious tribes were defeated or returned to Islam. The false prophets were stopped. **Tulayha** even repented and became a good Muslim again!

In less than a year, the entire Arabian Peninsula was reunited under the banner of Islam. Abu Bakr had saved the Muslim community from falling apart. Without his firmness, Islam might have remained a small religion in just Makkah and Madinah.

The Riddah Wars teach us that **having the courage to stand for what is right** matters most, especially when many people tell you to give up.

🎨 *Illustration: The Arabian Peninsula from above, now all flags are green, showing unity. In the center, the silhouette of the Ka'bah glows. Dominant colors: green and gold. Mood: peaceful and triumphant. Composition: same map as section 2, but now all flags are green — a visual transformation.*

---

## Lessons from the Riddah Wars

The Riddah Wars teach us many things:

- **Steadfastness** — Abu Bakr did not waver even when almost everyone advised compromise
- **Islam is complete** — prayer, *zakat*, and other acts of worship cannot be separated
- **Good leadership** — Abu Bakr was always fair, even to enemies who wished to repent
- **The door of repentance is always open** — even those who rebelled could return to the truth

Allah says: **"Whoever among you turns back from his religion, Allah will bring forth a people whom He loves and who love Him."** (QS. Al-Ma'idah: 54)

🎨 *Illustration: Abu Bakr sitting peacefully in a Madinah garden, a wise expression on his face, with sunset behind palm trees. Dominant colors: sunset orange and leaf green. Mood: reflective and peaceful. Composition: half-body portrait, sunset light from the side.*

# Perang Riddah ⚔️

---

## Kabar Sedih dari Madinah

Rasulullah ﷺ telah wafat. Seluruh Madinah berduka. Para sahabat menangis, dan bahkan **Umar bin al-Khattab** yang perkasa begitu sedih hingga tidak bisa menerima kenyataan.

Tapi ada satu orang yang tetap tenang dan kuat: **Abu Bakr as-Siddiq**, sahabat terdekat Rasulullah. Abu Bakr berdiri di hadapan orang banyak dan berkata: *"Barang siapa menyembah Muhammad, ketahuilah bahwa Muhammad telah wafat. Barang siapa menyembah Allah, ketahuilah bahwa Allah hidup dan tidak pernah mati."*

Abu Bakr kemudian dipilih menjadi **khalifah** — pemimpin umat Islam yang pertama setelah Nabi.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Interior masjid MadiAbu Bakr berdiri tegap di depan mimbar dengan wajah tenang namun tegas, para sahabat duduk mendengarkan dengan wajah sedih. Warna dominan: cokelat hangat dan emas redup. Mood: duka namun penuh harap. Komposisi: Abu Bakr di tengah, cahaya lembut dari jendela masjid.*

---

## Kabilah-Kabilah yang Memberontak

Setelah Rasulullah wafat, banyak kabilah di seluruh Jazirah Arab yang menolak taat kepada pemimpin baru. Sebagian berhenti membayar **zakat** — sedekah wajib untuk membantu orang miskin. Sebagian lagi mengikuti orang-orang yang mengaku sebagai nabi palsu!

Ada **Musailamah al-Kadzab** (si pembohong) yang mengaku nabi di daerah Yamamah. Ada **Thulaihah** yang mengaku nabi di kalangan Bani Asad. Dan ada lagi yang lain di Yaman dan tempat-tempat jauh.

Hampir seluruh Jazirah Arab memberontak! Hanya Makkah, Madidan Thaif yang tetap setia.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Peta sederhana Jazirah Arab bergaya kartun, dengan ikon-ikon kecil: bendera hijau di Makkah, Madidan Thaif; bendera merah di wilayah lain menandai pemberontakan. Warna dominan: kuning pasir dan hijau-merah kontras. Mood: tegang tapi informatif. Komposisi: peta di tengah, kompas di pojok.*

---

## Abu Bakr Tidak Mau Kompromi

Para sahabat menyarankan Abu Bakr untuk berkompromi — biarkan saja kabilah-kabilah itu tidak membayar zakat, yang penting mereka masih shalat. Tapi Abu Bakr menolak tegas!

Abu Bakr berkata: *"Demi Allah, aku akan memerangi siapa pun yang memisahkan antara shalat dan zakat! Seandainya mereka menolak memberikan seutas tali pengikat unta yang dulu mereka bayarkan kepada Rasulullah, niscaya aku akan memerangi mereka!"*

Abu Bakr tahu bahwa Islam itu utuh — tidak bisa dipilih-pilih mana yang mau dijalankan dan mana yang tidak.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Abu Bakr duduk di majelis bersama para sahabat, mengangkat tangan dengan tegas saat berbicara. Umar mendengarkan dengan wajah serius. Warna dominan: cokelat tanah dan putih. Mood: tegas dan penuh tekad. Komposisi: close-up majelis, Abu Bakr di posisi sentral.*

---

## Sebelas Pasukan Dikirim

Abu Bakr lalu menyiapkan **sebelas pasukan** yang dipimpin oleh para sahabat terbaik! Setiap pasukan dikirim ke arah yang berbeda di Jazirah Arab.

Yang paling terkenal adalah **Khalid bin al-Walid** — panglima perang yang dijuluki *Pedang Allah*. Khalid bergerak sangat cepat, mengalahkan satu pemberontak demi satu pemberontak. Dari satu medan pertempuran ke medan pertempuran lain, ia membawa kemenangan demi kemenangan.

Abu Bakr berpesan kepada para komandan: *"Tawarkan Islam terlebih dahulu. Jika mereka menerima, terimalah. Jika mereka menolak, barulah perangi mereka."*

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Sebelas bendera berkibar di depan gerbang Madimasing-masing dipegang oleh seorang komandan berkuda. Abu Bakr berdiri di gerbang melepas mereka. Warna dominan: hijau, putih, dan biru langit pagi. Mood: megah dan penuh semangat. Komposisi: panorama lebar, pasukan berjajar menghadap padang pasir.*

---

## Kemenangan dan Persatuan Kembali

Satu per satu, kabilah yang memberontak dikalahkan atau kembali memeluk Islam. Para nabi palsu dihentikan. **Thulaihah** bahkan bertobat dan kembali menjadi Muslim yang baik!

Dalam waktu kurang dari setahun, seluruh Jazirah Arab bersatu kembali di bawah panji Islam. Abu Bakr telah menyelamatkan umat Islam dari perpecahan. Tanpa keteguhannya, Islam mungkin hanya akan menjadi agama kecil di kota Makkah dan Madinah saja.

Perang Riddah mengajarkan kita bahwa **keberanian mempertahankan kebenaran** itu penting, terutama ketika banyak orang menyuruh kita menyerah.

🎨 *Ilustrasi: Jazirah Arab dari atas, kini semua bendera berwarna hijau, menandai persatuan. Di tengah, siluet Ka'bah bersinar. Warna dominan: hijau dan emas. Mood: damai dan penuh kemenangan. Komposisi: peta yang sama seperti section 2, tapi kini semua bendera hijau — transformasi visual.*

---

## Hikmah dari Perang Riddah

Perang Riddah mengajarkan banyak hal:

- **Keteguhan hati** — Abu Bakr tidak goyah meskipun hampir semua orang menyarankan kompromi
